source.subject: HelixHealth renewal: proposed on-prem expansion & isolated clusters
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Rahul Gupta <r.gupta@helixhealth.com>, Aditi Mehra <procurement@helixhealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal conversation: on-prem expansion at renewal

Hi Rahul / Aditi —

Quick note as we approach the June renewal for HelixHealth: you mentioned considering an on-prem environment split for regulatory isolation across two new clusters (one for analytics, one for patient-facing inference) as part of the renewal. We should scope this into the upcoming contract so delivery can start right after renewal sign.

High-level ask: can you confirm the target scope (number of clusters, any hard isolation/regulatory requirements, and expected go-live window)? From our side we’ll prepare a draft SOW, cost delta, and an updated term proposal (option to extend from 12->36 months with capacity commits to lower unit pricing).

If helpful I can set a 30-min call this week to align stakeholders — propose Wed 4/14 10am PT.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0168

Attachments: helix_request_notes.txt (summary notes)
From: Rahul Gupta <r.gupta@helixhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Aditi Mehra <procurement@helixhealth.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal conversation: on-prem expansion at renewal

Thanks Ben — we are definitely interested. A few immediate clarifications: 

- Scope: we want two *physically isolated* clusters (air-gapped network segments within our data centers) with separate VPCs and KMS keys; one cluster sized for sustained low-latency inference (customer-facing), the other for large-batch analytics (nightly jobs).
- Timeline: target production by end of Q3 (Sept 30).
- Compliance: we will need SOC 2 evidence and an inventory of data residency and logging features.
- Commercial: looking at a 36-month committed term if price/SLAs look good.

Please send topology diagrams and an estimated cost delta vs our existing private footprint. We can take a call on Wed — 10am PT works.

—Rahul
Rahul Gupta
CTO, HelixHealth
rahul.gupta@helixhealth.com

On Mon, Apr 12, 2027 at 3:02 PM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> (original message)
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 09:10:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: HelixHealth renewal: on-prem expansion (internal)

Team — forwarding Rahul's note. Key asks: two physically isolated clusters, separate VPCs/KMS, SOC2 artifacts, target prod by 2027-09-30, interested in 36-month term.

Action items I want from the delivery/pricing side before Wed 10am call: 
- Marcus: high-level topology diagram and ops approach for isolation + rough delivery timeline.
- Evelyn: draft SOW (milestones, acceptance criteria, risk items) and resource needs.
- Priya: confirm whether private package supports separate KMS per cluster and any additional runbook items for air-gapped network setup.
- Laura: please prep an initial commercial delta (monthly and committed pricing) and options for 12/24/36 months.

I’ll be on the call with the customer. Please share artifacts to me and I’ll circulate to HelixHealth before the meeting.

Thanks —
Ben

--- Forwarded message attached: helix_request_notes.txt
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 11:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: HelixHealth renewal: on-prem expansion (internal)

Thanks Ben — I pulled together a first-pass SOW and timeline. High-level notes below and attached is SOW_Helix_OnPrem_Expansion_v1.pdf plus a network topology (HelixTopology_v2.png).

Summary (draft):
- Scope: provision two isolated clusters in HelixHealth data centers (Cluster-A: low-latency inference; Cluster-B: batch analytics), each in separate VPCs, separate KMS keys, distinct logging sinks.
- Timeline: 12 weeks from contract signature to pilot (Cluster-A limited inference), 20 weeks to full production for both clusters. Buffer for procurement and rack provisioning not included.
- Deliverables: infra buildbook, runbooks, acceptance test suite, 6-month hypercare.
- Preconditions: HelixHealth to provide network racking windows, firewall change windows, and KMS provisioning details.
- Pricing drivers: cluster sizing, desired replication, and committed term. Estimated capex/one-time setup and OPEX delta included in pricing spreadsheet (pricing_delta_breakdown.xlsx).

Risks and notes:
- Air-gap/physical isolation introduces extended lead times for hardware/network approvals; recommend we include explicit procurement lead-time in SOW.
- If they commit to 36 months we can give a tiered discount that materially reduces per-token inference cost.

I’ll iterate once Marcus and Priya confirm the KMS/network details.

Evelyn Hart
Director, Customer Delivery | Redwood Inference
From: Aditi Mehra <procurement@helixhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Rahul Gupta <r.gupta@helixhealth.com>
Cc: procurement-ops@helixhealth.com
Date: Fri, 16 Apr 2027 08:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal conversation: on-prem expansion at renewal

Ben / Rahul — thanks for the context. Procurement needs two things to move: 

1) An updated quote that shows one-time setup fees and ongoing recurring fees (monthly) for 12/24/36 month terms. Please highlight the savings at 36 months.
2) Security/compliance artifacts (we need SOC2 attestation, logging/retention policy, and KMS key handling documentation) to run through our security review.

Also: can you confirm proposed payment terms and any termination clauses if hardware provisioning is delayed? We prefer net 60 but can be flexible with a small discount for net 30.

Please send the commercial package and the SOC2 stub by EOD Mon so we can start internal review.

Regards,
Aditi Mehra
Sr. Procurement Manager | HelixHealth
procurement@helixhealth.com
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Rahul Gupta <r.gupta@helixhealth.com>, Aditi Mehra <procurement@helixhealth.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: HelixHealth renewal: proposed on-prem expansion & isolated clusters

Hi Rahul / Aditi — appreciate the quick turnaround. Attached are the consolidated artifacts for your review: SOW_Helix_OnPrem_Expansion_v1.pdf, HelixTopology_v2.png, pricing_delta_breakdown.xlsx, and a SOC2 attestation stub. Summary of our commercial proposal: 

- Term options: 12/24/36 months (see pricing spreadsheet). 36-month committed term includes a 22% reduction on per-token fees and waived onboarding fees if signed by 2027-06-01.
- One-time setup: covers infra build, runbooks, and on-site validation (detailed in SOW).
- Payment terms: default Net 60; Net 30 available with 1.5% early-payment discount.
- SLA: production latency and availability targets included in SOW; we propose 99.95% uptime for production clusters with standard remedies (credits).

Next steps: 
1) Aditi — if you can confirm receipt and flag any legal/contracting constraints we’ll get a redline-ready contract over to you by EOD Wed.
2) Rahul — if the topology looks good I’ll ask Evelyn to prep a detailed delivery milestone plan for pilot cutover.
3) Internal: Evelyn/Marcus/Priya — please post the final SOW + pricing into the Drive link below and I’ll push the package to DocuSign.

Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-helix-expansion
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/HE-0043
DocuSign (preview): https://www.docusign.com/helixt-2027-onprem

Thanks — we’re aligned to move quickly if you are.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0168

Attachments referenced above: SOW_Helix_OnPrem_Expansion_v1.pdf, HelixTopology_v2.png, pricing_delta_breakdown.xlsx, SOC2_Attestation_stub.pdf

source.subject: AetherLabs dedicated sizing: token velocity safety band & cold-start mitigation
messages:
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@aetherlabs.ai>
Cc: Maria Gonzales <mgonzales@aetherlabs.ai>
Date: 2027-04-12T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: Dedicated sizing: token velocity safety band & cold-start mitigation

Hi Sanjay / Maria,

Great meeting earlier — thanks for walking through the product flows. As requested, I put together a short ask so we can size a dedicated slab for AetherLabs that meets your 99th-percentile (p99/p95) latency expectations while giving reasonable headroom for growth.

Short list of what would be most useful from your side:
- Recent 7-day token velocity by hour (rough CSV is fine) — typical peak hour and 95th percentile of per-minute TPS.
- Example request distributions by prompt length (median tokens, 95th token length).
- Which model family you plan to run in prod (we discussed open-llm-13B quantized and a smaller 7B for routing).
- Any SLO constraints: target p95 latency and hard p99 max.

We can start with a conservative baseline calculation assuming: 1) 60% KV-cache hit for repeated context, 2) model is 13B quantized to 4-bit, 3) steady-state batch efficiency of ~1.6x over single-request. If you want, attach a sample CSV to this thread and I will ask our infra team to produce a quick headroom sketch.

Attaching a token profile template: aether_token_profile.csv (use this if you can).

Next steps: if you send the sample CSV I’ll loop in engineering for the GPU-slab mapping and suggested burst/fallback plan.

Thanks,
David
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: aether_token_profile.csv (stub)
From: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@aetherlabs.ai>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-04-14T11:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated sizing: token velocity safety band & cold-start mitigation

David — thanks, sending over a sanitized sample of our last 14 days. See attached aether_demand_sample.csv. High level:
- Peak minute observed: ~1,150 reqs/min with median 320 tokens/request (we expect that to rise).
- 95th token length ~1,100 tokens; median ~280 tokens.
- 30% of requests are new sessions (cold KB/cache), 70% are returning sessions (we expect cache hit to be better over time).

Target SLO: p95 < 450ms for the 13B inference endpoint; p99 soft target 900ms (we understand this is aggressive).

Also, we have a procurement window in early May and would like a sizing + cost estimate by end of April for an initial 6-month reserved slab.

Thanks — Sanjay
Sanjay Rao
Head of Platform | AetherLabs
---
Attachment: aether_demand_sample.csv (stub)
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>

> On Apr 14, 2027, at 11:22, Sanjay Rao <sanjay@aetherlabs.ai> wrote:

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Sanjay Rao <sanjay@aetherlabs.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-04-16T17:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Dedicated sizing: token velocity safety band & cold-start mitigation

Team / Sanjay — quick engineering sketch attached (aether_capacity_reco_v1.pdf). Summary below.

Executive summary:
- Recommendation: Reserve a slab equivalent to 6x H100-80GB equivalent inference nodes (logical serving capacity), with burst pool of 2x additional GPUs for microbursts. This balances p95 targets with cost for the 6-month initial commit under 2x growth.

Assumptions (explicit):
- Model: 13B quantized to Q4 (int4-like path), end-to-end token compute cost ~0.28 ms/token on target runtime (steady-state, warmed).
- Batch efficiency: steady-state effective throughput multiplier = 1.55 (empirically from similar customers).
- KV-cache hit: steady-state 70% for returning sessions; cold-start mix at peak: 30% cold. Cold request penalty: +120 ms per request for cache repopulation.
- Average effective tokens/request (weighted median): 360 tokens (median 320, tail pushes avg up).

Sizing math (steady-state):
- Observed peak baseline: 1,150 req/min = 19.17 req/sec.
- Effective tokens/sec = 19.17 req/s * 360 tokens/req = 6,900 tokens/sec.
- With 70% cache hits, warmed-token throughput demand = 0.7*6,900 = 4,830 warmed tokens/sec; cold tokens/sec = 2,070 tokens/sec.
- Using compute cost 0.28 ms/token => warmed compute time/sec = 4,830 * 0.00028 = 1.3524 sec of GPU-time per wall-second; cold compute + penalty ~ (2,070 * 0.00028) + (19.17 req/s * 0.120s * 0.30 fraction) ≈ 0.5796 + 0.690 ≈ 1.2696 sec of GPU-time per wall-second.
- Total GPU-time demand ≈ 2.622 sec GPU-time per wall-second => cloud equivalent of ~2.622 GPUs at 1.0 GPU-second/s.

Adjust for batching efficiency (1.55x) and headroom: baseline GPUs = 2.622 / 1.55 ≈ 1.69 -> round up to 2 logical GPUs. For production headroom and 2x demand growth, multiply by 3x -> recommended reserved slab = 6 logical GPUs.

Latency check (p95 estimate):
- Warm p95: single-request token latency = tokens * 0.28 ms/token / batching factor + overhead (~40 ms) => for 360 tokens: (360*0.28)/1.55 + 40 ≈ (100.8/1.55)+40 ≈ 65 + 40 ≈ 105 ms p95 for warmed path.
- Cold p95: warmed latency + cold penalty (~120 ms) => ~225 ms p95. Both meet their target p95 < 450ms comfortably. p99 tail from queuing under bursts may approach 700–900ms; burst pool recommended.

Operational recommendations:
- Reserve: 6 logical GPUs (H100-equivalent).
- Burst: add 2 GPUs via autoscale-burst (pay-as-you-go) to cover microbursts; pre-warm cache on known heavy sessions during announced campaigns.
- Fallback: route to 7B model for latency-sensitive flows or reduce max context to 512 tokens if sustained overload.
- Pre-warm hooks: use a small scheduling job to pre-populate KV cache for returning users during ramp windows.

Costs / deliverables for David + Priya:
- We will produce a 6-month reserved cost estimate with monthly amortized GPU cost + estimated network egress. Priya, can you attach reserved-vs-burst pricing options?

Attached: aether_capacity_reco_v1.pdf (one-page summary)

-Marissa Cole
Senior Infra Engineer | Redwood Inference

From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@atlashealth.ai>

Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 09:00:00 -0700
Subject: MAP kickoff: seat assignments, tech-check & eval slotting

Hi Elena —

Great to be wrapping up contract items this week. To make the MAP kickoff productive, please confirm the attendee list below and whether Atlas prefers the 60- or 90-minute format. I attached a first-pass agenda and a small set of eval prompts so we can reserve a short eval slot during the MAP.

Proposed attendees (please add names/emails if I'm missing anyone):
- Elena Morales (Product Lead)
- Carlos Vega (ML Ops)
- Procurement (decision owner)

Redwood attendees and roles:
- Hannah Schmitt — MAP lead / success owner
- Marcus Lin — inference architect (will lead tech check + eval plan)
- Priya Natarajan — solution engineer (demo + preflight)
- Ben Carter — customer success (onboarding cadence)

Logistics:
- Tentative kickoff: Tue, Apr 20, 2027 10:00–11:00 PT (I can extend to 11:30 if you prefer 90m)
- Zoom: https://zoom.us/j/1234567890?pwd=map-kickoff (dial-in on the calendar invite)
- Calendar hold: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=map-kickoff-atlas-20270420
- Tech check: Apr 18, 2027 09:00–09:30 PT (we'll verify Zoom, SSO, and sample data)

Attachments: MAP_Agenda_v1.pdf (agenda), eval_prompts.csv (initial prompt set)

Please confirm: attendee list, preferred duration (60/90), and whether any vendor/SSO folks need to join the tech check. If procurement needs a separate 15-minute pre-call that week, tell me and we'll schedule.

Thanks — looking forward to getting this moving.

— Hannah
Hannah Schmitt
Senior Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0123

To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>

I'll own the tech check and the eval slot. Marcus here — I suggest we reserve 20 minutes in the MAP for a short live eval (run 3 prompts) so Atlas can see end-to-end latency and output quality with your sample prompts. If Atlas prefers 60m, we can keep the eval to a demo+Q&A. If 90m, we can run the live eval and a short triage on tokenization/latency.

I can do the tech check Apr 18 at 09:00 PT. Ben — can you run the attendee roll call and follow-up cadence notes?

— Marcus

source.subject: Quick pulse — counsel ok to progress?
messages:
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Torres <elena@finstack.io>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 28 Mar 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Discovery recap + next steps (FinStack)

Hi Elena — great talking earlier today. Quick recap and agreed next steps so we’re aligned:

- Goals: move FinStack search assistant from prototype to production for customer support (target 25k queries/day).
- Success metrics: 95th pct latency < 300ms on most frequent routes; avg cost per 1k queries < $X (we’ll share estimates).
- Data & compliance: OLAP logs to stay internal to FinStack, no long-term retention on hosted model outputs unless you opt in.

Next steps we agreed:
1) I’ll send a draft SOW + standard NDA/terms for internal review (today).
2) You’ll route to counsel and procurement; estimated 7–10 business day review.
3) Follow-up sync once counsel flags any substantive changes.

I attached a first-pass SOW (FinStack_Redwood_SOW_v1.pdf) so your team has context.

If timelines shift or procurement wants a different review cadence, let me know and we can re-prioritize.

Thanks again,
Marcus
Marcus Lin
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0117

Attachments: FinStack_Redwood_SOW_v1.pdf
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Torres <elena@finstack.io>, FinStack Legal <legal@finstack.io>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Draft SOW + proposed terms (redlines attached)

Elena / FinStack Legal — forwarding the draft agreement and a tracked redline from our counsel. We tried to keep changes narrow and focused on:
- Data residency and logging retention window
- SLA language (latency vs availability)
- Indemnity carve-outs around customer-provided data

Please review the attached doc and let me know if you’d prefer a brief 20-min call to walk through the redlines.

Best,
Marcus

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 09:00:00 -0700
Subject: Draft SOW + redlines (FinStack)
To: legal@finstack.io
Cc: priya.natarajan@redwood.ai, priyom.das@redwood.ai

Attachments: FinStack_agreement_redlines.docx (tracked changes)
From: Elena Torres <elena@finstack.io>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Cc: FinStack Legal <legal@finstack.io>
Date: Tue, 30 Mar 2027 10:17:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Draft SOW + proposed terms (redlines attached)

Thanks Marcus — I’ve sent the redlined SOW to our counsel and procurement team this morning. They asked for the following rough timelines: counsel 5–7 business days, procurement another 3–5 after legal signs off. If anything material comes up I’ll loop you in.

Also flagged the SLA language since our ops team is sensitive to how latency vs availability language is applied to bursty traffic.

Elena
Director of Product | FinStack
elena@finstack.io | +1 646-555-2122
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Torres <elena@finstack.io>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 08:39:00 -0700
Subject: Checking in — small pulse after redlines sent

Elena — hope your week is going well. Quick follow-up on the SOW I forwarded last week: any update from counsel/procurement on timing?

We’re tracking a Q2 capacity window on our side that lines up well with FinStack’s goals; if procurement needs to push the review out, we can discuss a flexible kickoff or prioritize key routes to meet an earlier go-live.

If it helps, I can:
- Join a call with your counsel to walk through our redline rationale (20 min), or
- Prepare a short one-page summary that isolates the operational/SLAs changes for procurement.

No rush — just checking priority so we can plan capacity and any exec-level escalation if helpful.

Best,
Marcus
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.ai

Attachments: none
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Torres <elena@finstack.io>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 3 Apr 2027 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Quick pulse — counsel ok to progress?

Elena — sorry to ping over the weekend. If procurement/counsel are still in review, no problem — could you share a ballpark (e.g., do they expect sign-off this week vs next)? We want to avoid double-booking dedicated capacity in case the timeline slips.

If timeline is slipping past mid-April, I’ll propose a short interim plan (limited pilot capacity) so we hold pricing and onboarding momentum without committing full reserved capacity.

Also copying Laura in case we should discuss an internal short-term credit or expedited review support from our side. Happy to escalate to ensure there’s no blocker on your end.

Thanks again — appreciate any color.
Marcus

--
Marcus Lin
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0117

Note: attachments referenced earlier: FinStack_Redwood_SOW_v2.pdf, FinStack_agreement_redlines.docx

From: Marco Ruiz <marco@acmesupply.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 16:02:00 -0700
Subject: Onboarding + invoice submission for ACME Supply

Hi Camila,

Thanks for the quick intro last week. We are ready to start sending invoices to Redwood for POs issued earlier this quarter. A couple quick questions before we go live:

1) Is the vendor portal mandatory for invoices, or can we email invoices to invoices@redwood.com?
2) If email is acceptable as a fallback, do you have a required subject line or filename schema we should follow?
3) Are there any recent changes to the onboarding form (tax, remittance, W-9, bank details) we should be aware of?

I attached our standard invoice template to show the fields we usually provide.

Attachment: sample_invoice_template.xlsx (stub)

Best,
Marco Ruiz
AP Manager, ACME Supply
marco@acmesupply.com
+1 415-555-0192
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
To: Marco Ruiz <marco@acmesupply.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 30 Mar 2027 09:24:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + invoice submission for ACME Supply

Hi Marco — great to connect.

Short answers: portal preferred; email allowed as fallback with strict conventions; onboarding form updated (see link). Details below so you can circulate internally.

Portal (preferred)
- URL: https://invoices.redwood.com/vendor-portal
- Benefits: automated PO matching, immediate validation (missing PO, invalid tax ID), and faster payment runs.
- File types accepted: PDF (preferred), CSV (line-level), or XLSX (if you include a single invoice per workbook).

Email fallback (only when portal is not available)
- Send to: invoices@redwood.com AND copy AP mailbox: ap-team@redwood.com
- Required subject prefix: [REDFWD-INVOICE] followed by PO# (e.g., [REDFWD-INVOICE] PO123456)
- Filename convention: REDWOOD_PO123456_INV<your-invoice-number>.pdf (no spaces)
- Minimum fields in body or attached PDF: Invoice number, invoice date, PO number, line items (description, qty, unit price), total, remit-to name/address, vendor tax ID/EIN.

New onboarding form (action required)
- We just released version 2 of the vendor onboarding form. It now requires: remittance address, vendor legal name (exact as on W-9), 1099 classification, EFT/ACH boolean, and tax ID type.
- Please complete: https://drive.google.com/open?id=onboarding-form-2027

PO stamping and payment terms
- PO stamping: Redwood requires either a stamped invoice (we stamp on receipt) or a clear PO reference in subject and on the invoice. If the PO is missing we will hold for manual review.
- Payment terms remain Net 45 for first 6 months for new vendors; after verification we can update to contractual terms.

Attachments: I attached the vendor onboarding form and a short sample CSV row you can use to validate column mapping. 

Attachment: vendor_onboarding_form_v2.pdf (stub)

Next steps:
1) Please complete the onboarding form (link above).
2) If you choose email fallback, mirror the subject/filename conventions exactly.
3) If you want SFTP ingestion for high-volume batches, let me know; I will loop in our integrations team.

Cc'ing Ben from Finance to confirm payment workflow.

Thanks,
Camila Reyes
Procurement Program Manager | Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.com
From: Marco Ruiz <marco@acmesupply.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
Cc: ACME Supply AP <invoices@acmesupply.com>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 08:11:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + invoice submission for ACME Supply

Thanks Camila — that's helpful. A couple follow-ups from our AP team:

- SFTP: we'd prefer SFTP for daily batches. Is there a provisioning lead?
- CSV schema: can you confirm the exact column order for CSV ingest (we want to avoid repeated rejects)?
- If portal is down, is it sufficient to put PO number only in subject and filename, or do you require the PO to be a separate visible field in the PDF?

I've attached a single-row CSV (sample_invoice_row.csv) showing our production export. If you could flag any column mismatches we will update the feed before going live.

Attachment: sample_invoice_row.csv (stub)

Best,
Marco
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marco Ruiz <marco@acmesupply.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 11:48:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + invoice submission for ACME Supply

Hi Marco — Ben here (Finance). Quick confirmations so you can proceed:

1) SFTP: we can provision a vendor SFTP. Camila, please send the onboarding checklist to the integrations team (PROC-412) and start the onboarding task.
2) CSV schema: we validate on these columns (exact order): invoice_number, invoice_date (YYYY-MM-DD), po_number, line_description, qty, unit_price, line_total, invoice_total, remit_name, remit_address_line1, remit_city, remit_state, remit_zip, vendor_tax_id. If any fields are omitted the portal will flag the invoice and create a manual ticket.
3) Email fallback: subject prefix + PO in filename is acceptable, but PO must also be present inside the invoice PDF (top-right header or on the first page). If missing, we will hold the invoice and open a ticket which delays payment.

Action for ACME:
- Complete vendor_onboarding_form_v2.pdf and attach W-9 (or local tax equivalent).
- Confirm whether you prefer SFTP or portal; if SFTP, Marco, send an example daily file (we got the sample CSV) and we will provision a user.

Priya (Finance Ops) is looped for vendor activation and ACH setup. Priya — please confirm the Net 45 provisional terms and advise on any exceptions for recurring suppliers.

Regards,
Ben Carter
Director, Accounts Payable | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 650-555-0147

source.subject: Supplier portal enrollment — MFA & tax ID sync error
messages:
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Green <ava.green@nimbleparts.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Kickoff: Supplier portal enrollment — next steps

Hi Ava —

Thanks for agreeing to kick off the procurement onboarding for NimbleParts this week. We want to get your supplier account in Coupa linked to our tax profile and enable PO ingestion so finance can route POs automatically. Quick checklist to get started (can you confirm the items below and reply with ETA?):

- Confirm primary contact and email for invoices (we have vendor@nimbleparts.com on file)
- Upload W-9 / tax form and confirm legal entity name exactly as on W-9
- Complete MFA enrollment (Coupa sends a 6-digit code to the mobile number)
- Confirm bank remit details (for payment set-up — we will need an ACH form later)

I’m copying Marcus (payments) and Priya (vendor ops). If you can share the W-9 and a screenshot of the portal screen that appears after login, we’ll triage any mismatch quickly. I’ll also post a short checklist in the shared procurement folder for your access.

Best,
Hannah

Hannah Schmitt
Senior Procurement Manager
Redwood Inference

Attachments: none

From: Ava Green <ava.green@nimbleparts.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 10:02:13 -0700
Subject: Re: Kickoff: Supplier portal enrollment — next steps

Hi Hannah —

Thanks for the checklist. I completed the W-9 upload and MFA enrollment but the portal shows an error after I verify the code: "Account not linked to tax entity: TIN mismatch (error 4123)." I double-checked that the legal name on the W-9 is "NimbleParts Inc" and the TIN matches our records. I attached the W-9 and a screenshot of the error.

Can you confirm whether Redwood is expecting a different legal entity or suffix? Also, the MFA flow sent the code to my personal phone; is there a way to add an alternate contact? Our IT uses a shared mobile number for admin recovery (Main: +1-415-555-0199).

Thanks,
Ava

Attachments: W-9_NimbleParts.pdf, screenshot-portal-error.png

From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Green <ava.green@nimbleparts.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Kickoff: Supplier portal enrollment — next steps

Hi Ava —

Thanks for the quick response and for attaching the files. Marcus — can you take a look at the TIN sync step? Sometimes CVR/TIN mapping on our side expects the full legal name to include the corp suffix exactly (e.g., Inc. vs Inc). Priya — I’m asking procurement ops to confirm whether we created a placeholder vendor profile earlier for a previous engagement (that can sometimes lock the expected TIN).

Ava — two quick asks so we can escalate with our Coupa admin team if needed:
1) From the portal, if you go to Settings > Company Info, can you paste the exact string listed for "Legal Name" and the TIN (last 4 digits are fine if you prefer)?
2) If you can temporarily enroll MFA to the shared admin number (+1-415-555-0199) and confirm the exact error timestamp, we will pull server logs.

If this is a name normalisation problem we can usually resolve it within one business day. I’ll open a ticket with Coupa (scope: tax id sync) and include the screenshots. I’ll also share a short triage playbook in the procurement folder.

Best,
Hannah

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Ava Green <ava.green@nimbleparts.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 30 Mar 2027 15:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Kickoff: Supplier portal enrollment — next steps

Hi all —

I pulled the vendor-sync logs for the event at 2027-03-29T17:02:25Z (matching Ava's screenshot timestamp). The Coupa webhook returned a 4123 code with payload indicating a normalized_name mismatch: expected "NimbleParts, Inc." but received "NimbleParts Inc" (no comma). That explains the TIN mapping failure — our normaliser requires exact punctuation for legacy vendor records.

Ava — two remediation paths:
- quickest: re-upload the W-9 with the legal name exactly matching the portal's expected string ("NimbleParts, Inc.") and re-trigger the TIN verification; or
- we can update the vendor placeholder in our system to accept the alternate spelling, but that needs an internal change and will take 24–48 hours and a quick compliance sign-off.

Given your timeline, re-uploading with the exact punctuation is the fastest. If you prefer us to update the placeholder, Priya can start the change request and include the compliance checklist. I’ve created a HubSpot ticket (987654321) and linked the procurement folder for traceability.

Marcus Lin
Payments Operations Lead
Redwood Inference

Attachments: none

Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Kickoff: Supplier portal enrollment — next steps

Hi Ava —

Thanks for agreeing to kick off the procurement onboarding for NimbleParts this week. We want to get your supplier account in Coupa linked to our tax profile and enable PO ingestion so finance can route POs automatically. Quick checklist to get started (can you confirm the items below and reply with ETA?):

- Confirm primary contact and email for invoices (we have vendor@nimbleparts.com on file)
- Upload W-9 / tax form and confirm legal entity name exactly as on W-9
- Complete MFA enrollment (Coupa sends a 6-digit code to the mobile number)
- Confirm bank remit details (for payment set-up — we will need an ACH form later)

I’m copying Marcus (payments) and Priya (vendor ops). If you can share the W-9 and a screenshot of the portal screen that appears after login, we’ll triage any mismatch quickly. I’ll also post a short checklist in the shared procurement folder for your access.

Best,
Hannah

Hannah Schmitt
Senior Procurement Manager
Redwood Inference

Attachments: none


From: Ava Green <ava.green@nimbleparts.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 10:02:13 -0700
Subject: Re: Kickoff: Supplier portal enrollment — next steps

Hi Hannah —

Thanks for the checklist. I completed the W-9 upload and MFA enrollment but the portal shows an error after I verify the code: \"Account not linked to tax entity: TIN mismatch (error 4123).\" I double-checked that the legal name on the W-9 is \"NimbleParts Inc\" and the TIN matches our records. I attached the W-9 and a screenshot of the error.

Can you confirm whether Redwood is expecting a different legal entity or suffix? Also, the MFA flow sent the code to my personal phone; is there a way to add an alternate contact? Our IT uses a shared mobile number for admin recovery (Main: +1-415-555-0199).

Thanks,
Ava

Attachments: W-9_NimbleParts.pdf, screenshot-portal-error.png


From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Green <ava.green@nimbleparts.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Kickoff: Supplier portal enrollment — next steps

Hi Ava —

Thanks for the quick response and for attaching the files. Marcus — can you take a look at the TIN sync step? Sometimes CVR/TIN mapping on our side expects the full legal name to include the corp suffix exactly (e.g., Inc. vs Inc). Priya — I’m asking procurement ops to confirm whether we created a placeholder vendor profile earlier for a previous engagement (that can sometimes lock the expected TIN).

Ava — two quick asks so we can escalate with our Coupa admin team if needed:
1) From the portal, if you go to Settings > Company Info, can you paste the exact string listed for \"Legal Name\" and the TIN (last 4 digits are fine if you prefer)?
2) If you can temporarily enroll MFA to the shared admin number (+1-415-555-0199) and confirm the exact error timestamp, we will pull server logs.

If this is a name normalisation problem we can usually resolve it within one business day. I’ll open a ticket with Coupa (scope: tax id sync) and include the screenshots. I’ll also share a short triage playbook in the procurement folder.

Best,
Hannah


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Ava Green <ava.green@nimbleparts.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 30 Mar 2027 15:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Kickoff: Supplier portal enrollment — next steps

Hi all —

I pulled the vendor-sync logs for the event at 2027-03-29T17:02:25Z (matching Ava's screenshot timestamp). The Coupa webhook returned a 4123 code with payload indicating a normalized_name mismatch: expected \"NimbleParts, Inc.\" but received \"NimbleParts Inc\" (no comma). That explains the TIN mapping failure — our normaliser requires exact punctuation for legacy vendor records.

Ava — two remediation paths:
- quickest: re-upload the W-9 with the legal name exactly matching the portal's expected string (\"NimbleParts, Inc.\") and re-trigger the TIN verification; or
- we can update the vendor placeholder in our system to accept the alternate spelling, but that needs an internal change and will take 24–48 hours and a quick compliance sign-off.

Given your timeline, re-uploading with the exact punctuation is the fastest. If you prefer us to update the placeholder, Priya can start the change request and include the compliance checklist. I’ve created a HubSpot ticket (987654321) and linked the procurement folder for traceability.

Marcus Lin
Payments Operations Lead
Redwood Inference

Attachments: none

source.subject: PO field linting & weekly staggered payments — SigmaCloud deal
messages:
From: Laura Mills <laura@sigmacloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: AP Team <ap@sigmacloud.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: PO metadata requirements + weekly invoices (SigmaCloud)

Hi Ben,

Quick follow from procurement: we have a pilot PO ready but our AP team requires invoices to be issued weekly for the pilot (rather than monthly) and each invoice needs the following fields present in the PDF metadata and electronic XML: po_reference (<= 12 chars), billing_entity_uuid, subscription_id, and usage_summary_line. Our PO template is attached.

Also important: SigmaCloud prefers Net45, but procurement asked whether Redwood can support Net30 for a tiered early-pay discount. There are two constraints on our side: (1) our ERP only accepts PO numbers that are alphanumeric and max 12 characters, (2) we need the invoice cadence to allow a month-end reconciliation file that aggregates weekly invoices into a single monthly ledger entry.

Can you confirm whether Redwood can:
- accept the PO template (attached) as-is, or whether you need a normalized PO number style
- support weekly invoice issuance with a month-end reconciliation CSV
- offer any early-pay discounts for Net30 vs Net45

If you prefer, loop in billing/finance and legal; happy to include them.

Thanks,
Laura
Laura Mills
Head of Procurement, SigmaCloud
Attachment: SigmaCloud_PO_template.pdf

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Laura Mills <laura@sigmacloud.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Mar 2027 13:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: PO metadata requirements + weekly invoices (SigmaCloud)

Laura — thanks for the clear ask and the template. Looping Vanessa (billing ops) and Priya (solutions) to confirm implementation details.

High-level answers:
- We can accept weekly invoices for a pilot program; we typically default to monthly for standard accounts but will enable weekly cadence for Enterprise pilots. That said, weekly invoices require a small operational processing fee (one-time setup + $250/wk) unless we consolidate at month-end for reconciliation.
- We can map the PO fields you listed. Two requests from our side:
  1) PO reference string must be alphanumeric and we prefer a consistent prefix (e.g., SC-######) to make ingestion idempotent. If SigmaCloud cannot change format, we can accept up to 12 chars but will need an example PO to validate parsing.
  2) Include subscription_id in the invoice line items (we can accept either UUID or your internal SKU).
- Discount options: for flexibility we can offer a tiered early-pay program: 1.75% if you commit to Net30, 0.75% for Net45. Alternatively, we can do Net45 with a one-time onboarding credit for a 12-month commitment.

Next steps I propose:
1) You share a sample PO (real example) so Priya can validate field parsing.
2) Vanessa will draft the billing-side PO acceptance checklist and confirm whether we can waive the weekly processing fee if you agree to monthly reconciliation (we often do for strategic pilots).
3) If terms look good, legal will draft a short amendment to the Master Services Agreement to lock cadence + PO field obligations.

Vanessa/Priya — can you confirm ETA on checklist + PO parsing run?

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive, Redwood Inference
--
Redwood Inference | ben@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123
Attachment: Redwood_PO_acceptance_checklist.xlsx

From: Laura Mills <laura@sigmacloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: AP Team <ap@sigmacloud.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Mar 2027 09:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: PO metadata requirements + weekly invoices (SigmaCloud)

Ben — appreciate the quick response and options. A few clarifications from our side (copied AP guidelines attached):

- We can adopt a consistent prefix (SC-) for new POs going forward, but the PO number that triggers this pilot will be generated by our legacy system and will be 10 chars alphanumeric — I’ll send a sample.
- The AP team will accept monthly reconciliation if Redwood provides a CSV that aggregates the weekly invoices into a single ledger line with a breakdown attached. That CSV should include invoice_id, week_range, po_reference, subscription_id, gross_amount, tax, and net_amount.
- On discounts: SigmaCloud is targeting Net45 as the default. We can consider Net30 if Redwood can move the discount to 2.0% for Net30 and 1.0% for Net45, but we need written confirmation on how discounts apply to tax and credits (our finance says discounts should apply to gross for reconciliation).

Would Redwood be open to:
- weekly invoices for the first 90 days with a month-end CSV for reconciliation,
- a temporary waiver of the weekly processing fee during the pilot,
- a capped onboarding credit that you mentioned if we commit to 12 months?

I’ve attached our AP guidelines and will send a PO sample in a separate email.

Thanks,
Laura
Attachment: SigmaCloud_AP_guidelines.pdf

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Laura Mills <laura@sigmacloud.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Mar 2027 16:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: PO metadata requirements + weekly invoices (SigmaCloud)

Laura — great, thanks for the sample rules. We’re aligned on the pilot approach. To summarize and confirm in writing: 

1) Invoice cadence and reconciliation
   - Redwood will issue weekly invoices for the 90-day pilot.
   - At month-end Redwood will generate an aggregated reconciliation CSV (invoice_id, week_range, po_reference, subscription_id, gross_amount, tax, net_amount) as requested.
   - Weekly processing fee will be waived for the 90-day pilot; after pilot we'll revisit cadence and fees.

2) PO & metadata
   - We can ingest your 10-char PO format (no change required for the pilot) but request that future POs use the SC- prefix to aid idempotency. Please send a real PO sample (Priya will run a parse test).
   - subscription_id must be present on line items; if you deliver UUIDs we accept them, otherwise you can provide internal SKUs and we'll map to subscription_id during onboarding.

3) Net terms & discounts
   - Redwood offers 1.75% discount for Net30 and 0.75% for Net45 as a standard. For SigmaCloud we can stretch to 2.0% for Net30 and 1.0% for Net45 conditional on a 12-month commitment. Discounts apply to gross invoice amount (pre-tax) and will be reflected in the reconciliation CSV.
   - One-time onboarding credit: Redwood will provide a $10,000 credit applied against the first invoice(s) if SigmaCloud signs a 12-month committed term.

4) Next steps
   - Laura: send sample PO (real example) and confirm whether Finance requires any alternate tax handling.
   - Priya: run parse against the sample PO and confirm mapping results (ETA 48 hours).
   - Vanessa: prepare short billing amendment and work with Legal on the contract addendum (I’ll introduce legal once we confirm PO parsing).

If that all looks correct I’ll mark the deal in HubSpot as "billing pilot agreed" and we’ll push legal to draft the amendment.

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive, Redwood Inference
--
Redwood Inference | ben@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

source.subject: MAP: Technical evaluation playbook — owners, success criteria, timeline
messages:
From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
To: James Holloway <james.h@healthgrid.com>, Maya Singh <maya@healthgrid.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP: Technical evaluation playbook — owners, success criteria, timeline

Hi James / Maya —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap + proposed next steps to get the Technical Evaluation milestone on a tight cadence so we can hit the MAP target close date (details below).

Summary of what we agreed on
- Objective: Validate Redwood inference performance and cost vs your production SLAs with a 2-week technical eval.
- Success criteria (pass = all three):
  • Latency: p95 < 220ms for 512-token interactive queries at 8 concurrent users (internal load spec attached)
  • Cost: < $0.0009/token (measured after batching and cache) for steady-state load
  • Quality: model responds correctly to 95% of the 120 test prompts in the eval suite (acceptance thresholds in the spec)
- Model shortlist for the slate: Llama 2 70B (quantized), Mistral-Instruct-7B, and Redwood-v1-medium (baseline).

Owners & responsibilities
- HealthGrid (you): provide a sample of 1,000 representative prompts + expected outputs, and a service account for VPN access to the staging data store (Maya).
- Redwood (Rafael): run benchmark sweep, produce latency/cost report, and share experimental configs (batch sizes, kv-cache policy).
- Redwood (Karthik): test integration with your API and confirm end-to-end trace for p95 measurement.
- Redwood (Priya): collect security/compliance checklist items (data flow, retention) to ensure we're ok to run on dedicated staging env.

Proposed timeline (target close date in MAP: 2027-05-20)
- Mar 17: HealthGrid provides dataset + access (dependency)
- Mar 18–23: Redwood runs initial benchmark sweep and posts results to Drive
- Mar 24: Joint sync (30 min) to review results and agree on acceptance
- Mar 25–30: If needed, follow-up tuning run and final acceptance

Dependencies / blockers
- VPN/service account access (required by 3/17)
- Clarified prompt rubric for quality scoring (see column "expected_behavior" in attached CSV)
- SOC2 evidence for staging if you want the run on dedicated infra (Priya will follow-up)

Attachments: latency_test_spec.pdf (test harness), healthgrid_eval_dataset.csv (sample prompts), redwood-map-draft-v2.docx (MAP copy with milestones)

Next steps for this thread
1) James / Maya: confirm dataset delivery date and whether you'd like the run on our hosted staging or on a dedicated environment (affects cost).
2) Rafael / Karthik / Priya: confirm owners (done) and call out any additional needs.

Thanks — I'll schedule the Mar 24 sync once we have the data delivery date.

Best,
Grace
Grace O'Connor
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Mobile: (415) 555-0142

From: James Holloway <james.h@healthgrid.com>

title: MAP: Technical evaluation playbook — owners, success criteria, timeline
preview:
From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
To: James Holloway <james.h@healthgrid.com>, Maya Singh <maya@healthgrid.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP: Technical evaluation playbook — owners, success criteria, timeline

Hi James / Maya —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap + proposed next steps to get the Technical Evaluation milestone on a tight cadence so we can hit the MAP target close date (details below).

Summary of what we agreed on
- Objective: Validate Redwood inference performance and cost vs your production SLAs with a 2-week technical eval.
- Success criteria (pass = all three):
  • Latency: p95 < 220ms for 512-token interactive queries at 8 concurrent users (internal load spec attached)
  • Cost: < $0.0009/token (measured after batching and cache) for steady-state load
  • Quality: model responds correctly to 95% of the 120 test prompts in the eval suite (acceptance thresholds in the spec)
- Model shortlist for the slate: Llama 2 70B (quantized), Mistral-Instruct-7B, and Redwood-v1-medium (baseline).

Owners & responsibilities
- HealthGrid (you): provide a sample of 1,000 representative prompts + expected outputs, and a service account for VPN access to the staging data store (Maya).
- Redwood (Rafael): run benchmark sweep, produce latency/cost report, and share experimental configs (batch sizes, kv-cache policy).
- Redwood (Karthik): test integration with your API and confirm end-to-end trace for p95 measurement.
- Redwood (Priya): collect security/compliance checklist items (data flow, retention) to ensure we're ok to run on dedicated staging env.

Proposed timeline (target close date in MAP: 2027-05-20)
- Mar 17: HealthGrid provides dataset + access (dependency)
- Mar 18–23: Redwood runs initial benchmark sweep and posts results to Drive
- Mar 24: Joint sync (30 min) to review results and agree on acceptance
- Mar 25–30: If needed, follow-up tuning run and final acceptance

Dependencies / blockers
- VPN/service account access (required by 3/17)
- Clarified prompt rubric for quality scoring (see column \"expected_behavior\" in attached CSV)
- SOC2 evidence for staging if you want the run on dedicated infra (Priya will follow-up)

Attachments: latency_test_spec.pdf (test harness), healthgrid_eval_dataset.csv (sample prompts), redwood-map-draft-v2.docx (MAP copy with milestones)

Next steps for this thread
1) James / Maya: confirm dataset delivery date and whether you'd like the run on our hosted staging or on a dedicated environment (affects cost).
2) Rafael / Karthik / Priya: confirm owners (done) and call out any additional needs.

Thanks — I'll schedule the Mar 24 sync once we have the data delivery date.

Best,
Grace
Grace O'Connor
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Mobile: (415) 555-0142

messages:
From: Michael Chen <m.chen@aurorasys.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Lee <sara.lee@aurorasys.com>
Date: 2027-03-08T09:15:00Z
Subject: Aurora MSA + Order Form ready for signature (attached)

Hi Aditya,

Thanks for getting us this far. Attached are the clean-ish copies from our side: Aurora_MSA_v3_redlines.pdf and OrderForm_Aurora_v2.docx. Legal is comfortable with the redlines and we just need to execute. Before we proceed we need confirmation on two items from your side:

- Who will be the executing signer on the Redwood side (title and authority)?
- Can you confirm the DocuSign envelope will include a final clean PDF and the audit log for our procurement?

We'd prefer parallel signing if possible — our CEO and procurement lead will sign at the same time. Also: one of our signers will send a notarized POA (power of attorney) for delegated authority; can you accept a notarized scanned POA prior to signing?

If all good, please send the envelope and confirm the expected timeframe — procurement needs the fully executed PDF by EOD Friday (their cut-off).

Thanks,
Michael Chen
Director, Strategic Partnerships
Aurora Systems

---
Attachment stubs:
- Aurora_MSA_v3_redlines.pdf (pdf, 1.2MB)
- OrderForm_Aurora_v2.docx (docx, 48KB)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Michael Chen <m.chen@aurorasys.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-03-08T10:02:00Z
Subject: Re: Aurora MSA + Order Form ready for signature (attached)

Michael —

Appreciate the quick turnaround and the attachments. High level: we can do parallel signing and we will include the final clean PDF + full DocuSign audit trail. A few internal asks before I send the envelope:

1) Signer authority: please confirm the exact name and title of your legal signer and the CEO so we can set the role order correctly in the envelope.

2) POA: yes — we can accept a notarized scanned POA ahead of signing. Our compliance team (cc'd) will review and confirm acceptance; if the POA is valid we'll mark the signer as authorized and proceed with the envelope.

3) Order Form: we will re-generate the final Order Form as a PDF for inclusion so there are no last-minute .docx formatting issues.

Next steps from Redwood: I'll queue the envelope once Priya confirms the execution checklist and authority. Tentative plan is to send the DocuSign envelope tomorrow morning (Wed) for parallel signing with a 48-hour window. If procurement needs EOD Friday, we should be able to meet that assuming POA arrives today.

I'll follow up here once legal/ops sign off.

Thanks —
Aditya Rao
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0211

---
Attachments referenced: none (we'll attach final PDFs when sending)

From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-03-08T14:30:00Z
Subject: Re: Aurora MSA + Order Form ready for signature (attached)

Aditya / team —

Quick legal checklist before you send the envelope: I reviewed Aurora_MSA_v3_redlines.pdf and the Order Form. We're cleared on the commercial redlines; two process notes for execution compliance:

- Authority confirmation: we need either (A) a named officer listed on Aurora's corporate resolution OR (B) a notarized POA for the delegate. A scanned notarized POA is acceptable, but please obtain the original retained by the customer and note in our records who provided it.

- Witnessing / notarization: If Aurora's signatory is signing under a POA, we prefer the envelope flow to include a "witness" signer (informational) so the envelope shows the witness name and email. Alternatively, capture the notarized POA PDF as an attachment to the envelope.

- Sequencing: Because procurement asked for simultaneous signature, set Redwood's signer and Aurora's signers in parallel groups in DocuSign. We should not require countersignature sequencing that would delay finalization.

- Audit artifacts: ensure the envelope includes the DocuSign certificate of completion and the completed combined PDF (clean MSA + signed Order Form). Mark in HubSpot (link below) when envelope is sent and completed.

I'm attaching a quick execution checklist for e-sig envelopes (MSA_execution_checklist.xlsx). Once the POA arrives, I can greenlight sending. If procurement pushes for a hard-copy notarization, escalate to me and we'll handle on a case-by-case basis.

Priya Natarajan
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

---
Attachment stubs:
- MSA_execution_checklist.xlsx (xlsx, 22KB)

From: Michael Chen <m.chen@aurorasys.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Lee <sara.lee@aurorasys.com>, contracts@aurorasys.com
Date: 2027-03-09T08:10:00Z
Subject: Re: Aurora MSA + Order Form ready for signature (attached)

Aditya / Priya —

Thanks for the clarity. Quick update from our side: our legal (Sara) will send over a notarized POA PDF by close of day today — we will retain originals on file as requested. We are fine with embedding the POA into the envelope as an attachment and including a witness placeholder. Please proceed with parallel signing once you confirm the POA is accepted.

Also: can you confirm we will receive both the combined signed PDF and the DocuSign certificate as separate files in the envelope completion files? Procurement needs both for their audit.

On a related note I'm forwarding our procurement note (below) — they reiterate EOD Friday for the fully executed package. If you can send the envelope tomorrow morning that should be fine.

---- Forwarded message ----
From: Sara Lee <sara.lee@aurorasys.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 8, 2027 at 08:50 AM
Subject: Procurement deadline and final copy request
To: contracts@aurorasys.com

Team — please ensure we get the final executed PDFs and the DocuSign audit log; finance will not process supplier onboarding until both are in the system.

Regards,
Sara

Thanks,
Michael Chen
Director, Strategic Partnerships
Aurora Systems

---
Requested attachments on our side when envelope completes:
- Aurora_MSA_v3_executed.pdf (combined signed PDF)
- DocuSign_Audit_Log_Aurora_0x9a7b2f.pdf (certificate + audit trail)

Requested envelope config:
- Parallel signing group: Aurora CEO (signer), Aurora Legal (signer), Redwood Authorized Signer (signer)
- Attachment: notarized POA (customer provides)
- Completion files: combined clean PDF + docuSign certificate

Please reply with a quick confirmation once envelope is sent and include the DocuSign envelope link. We will monitor and coordinate internally.

--
Attachment stubs: none (POA to follow)

source.subject: Framework: Upfront credits vs monthly smoothing for annual token commit
messages:
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Paul Gomez <paul.gomez@olivetech.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 28 Feb 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Framework: Upfront credits vs monthly smoothing for annual token commit

Paul —

Thanks again for the time yesterday. Per our conversation, I put together a short framework for annual token commits that shows three anchor tiers and two billing/credit patterns (upfront credits vs monthly smoothing). I attached a simple worksheet (Redwood_Annual_Commit_Tiers_v1.xlsx) with line items; high level summary below:

Starter (good for proof-of-concept)
- Commit: 100M tokens / year
- Effective per-token: $0.00060 (hosted API)
- Options: 25% upfront credit or monthly smoothing (12 equal invoices)

Growth (productizing, scaling)
- Commit: 1B tokens / year
- Effective per-token: $0.00050 (5% discount off Starter)
- Options: 40% upfront credit + 8 monthly invoices, or full smoothing over 12 months

Enterprise (high volume, SLA)
- Commit: 5B tokens / year
- Effective per-token: $0.00035 (volume ladder, deeper discount)
- Options: tiered upfront credits, carry-forward allowance, dedicated capacity add-on available

Key commercial levers we usually see:
- Upfront credits (reduce unit price, immediate cash)
- Monthly smoothing (reduces cash burden, increases predictability for you)
- Rolling buffer (we allow up to 20-25% of unused tokens to roll into the next year at a reduced conversion rate)
- Burst / overage: automatic burst allowed up to X% with overage pricing or credit drawdown

Operational/SLAs:
- Standard hosted SLA: 99.9% availability; Enterprise tier can add 99.95% with prioritized routing and a dedicated burst pool.
- We include cost and latency dashboards per route, and monthly usage reports.

Request: can you confirm (a) which billing cadence you prefer (upfront credits vs smoothing), and (b) a realistic 12-month token projection range? Once we have that I’ll have Priya produce a formal quote and Ben will prepare an SOW with the onboarding milestones.

Next step: 30-minute sync early next week works for me — I can share the worksheet during the call.

Thanks,
Marcus Lin
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments: Redwood_Annual_Commit_Tiers_v1.xlsx
From: Paul Gomez <paul.gomez@olivetech.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Amira Khan <amira.khan@olivetech.com>, OliveTech Procurement <procurement@olivetech.com>
Date: Mon, 1 Mar 2027 10:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Framework: Upfront credits vs monthly smoothing for annual token commit

Marcus —

Thanks for the clear breakdown and the worksheet — helpful. A few quick clarifying questions before I loop in legal and procurement:

1) Token accounting: does the commit count include both prompt + response tokens? Any differences for embeddings/structured output?
2) Rollover: you mentioned rolling up to 20-25% — can credits be banked beyond the year at a conversion rate, or do they expire?
3) Smoothing: if we choose monthly smoothing but actual consumption is front-loaded, is there an ability to true-up mid-year without penalty?
4) Term: is the anchor pricing above tied to a 12-month term (we assume yes) or do you require longer commitments for the Enterprise tier?

We’re targeting a contractor-reviewed budget by March 18; if we can land on the billing pattern and an outline SOW by March 11, procurement can fast-track. I attached our initial usage projection (high/medium/low) — will send the spreadsheet separately via procurement if needed.

—Paul
Paul Gomez
Director of Product, OliveTech
paul.gomez@olivetech.com

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Paul Gomez <paul.gomez@olivetech.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 1 Mar 2027 14:18:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Framework: Upfront credits vs monthly smoothing for annual token commit

Paul — thanks for the fast notes. Quick answers from billing/pricing perspective: 

- Token accounting: commits cover all hosted API token usage for generation and chat (prompt + model output). Embeddings are billed separately but can be included in a blended commit at negotiated effective rate — we treat embedding tokens as a different SKU in the worksheet. Structured output (function calls) follows the same token accounting as generation.

- Rollover policy: standard rollover allows up to 25% of the committed volume to carry into the next 12-month term at a 50% conversion efficiency (i.e., 1 unused committed token converts to 0.5 available token next year). This prevents banking large unused volumes while giving runway. For Enterprise we can extend a larger carry-forward with a conversion fee.

- Mid-year true-up: we support a one-time true-up per 12-month period without penalty (we'll invoice the incremental committed units at the contracted effective price). For front-loaded consumption we can also front-deadline adjust the smoothing schedule if notified 30 days in advance — this protects both sides from unexpected cashflow hits.

- Term length: Starter & Growth are typically 12-month terms. Enterprise pricing bands can be extended to 24 months for a marginally better rate (we tie deeper discounts to longer financial commitments).

I can convert the worksheet into a formal quote once we have Paul's expected 12-month range and preferred billing pattern. Ben — if you want to include a limited onboarding credit (10% of first-year spend) on early signature, say so and I’ll reflect it in the quote.

Priya Natarajan
Head of Pricing & Commercial Ops, Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Paul Gomez <paul.gomez@olivetech.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Mar 2027 08:46:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Framework: Upfront credits vs monthly smoothing for annual token commit

Paul / Marcus —

Echoing Priya — a couple of commercial options to make procurement comfortable while keeping unit economics predictable:

1) Blended ramp: year 1 ramped effective rate where the first 3 months are billed at a slightly higher per-token rate (to cover onboarding), then step down across quarters to the committed effective rate. This avoids large upfront cash while giving Redwood near-term coverage.

2) Early-sign bonus: if OliveTech signs an MSA + SOW by March 18, we can include a one-time 10% onboarding credit applied against first-year consumption (not refundable).

3) SOW milestones: propose a 60-day pilot milestone (activation, two integrations, smoke test) and a fuller GA onboarding at 90 days. SOW draft attached; Marcus will share official SOW and we can convert to DocuSign once terms are agreed.

Laura — looping you for billing ops review on smoothing cadence and invoicing.

Happy to jump on a 30-min call Wed/Thu to walk through the worksheet and align on a billing pattern.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Head of Sales, Platform Solutions, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com

Attachments: SOW_OliveTech_draft.docx
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Paul Gomez <paul.gomez@olivetech.com>, Amira Khan <amira.khan@olivetech.com>, OliveTech Procurement <procurement@olivetech.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Mar 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Framework: Upfront credits vs monthly smoothing for annual token commit

Paul / Amira —

Thanks for the questions and for looping procurement. Next steps I propose based on the thread: 

1) You confirm preferred billing pattern (Upfront credit vs monthly smoothing vs blended ramp) — if you’re undecided, indicate which of the three scenarios in the worksheet feels closest to expected behavior (High/Medium/Low).
2) OliveTech to share the preferred 12-month volume band (or the three-point projection you mentioned).
3) Redwood will produce: (a) a formal pricing quote reflecting chosen billing pattern, (b) a draft MSA with the agreed rollover and true-up language, and (c) a draft SOW with the 60/90-day milestones and success criteria.

If OliveTech signs by March 18 we can apply the 10% onboarding credit Ben mentioned and target a April 1 activation date. I’m free Thurs Mar 4 between 9–11am PT or Fri Mar 5 after 1pm PT — if either works, I’ll set up a short call and share the worksheet live.

I’ll attach the two files again for convenience. Let me know which slot works and we’ll lock it in.

Best,
Marcus
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments: Redwood_Annual_Commit_Tiers_v1.xlsx; SOW_OliveTech_draft.docx
