From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Paul Gomez <paul.gomez@olivetech.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 28 Feb 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Framework: Upfront credits vs monthly smoothing for annual token commit

Paul —

Thanks again for the time yesterday. Per our conversation, I put together a short framework for annual token commits that shows three anchor tiers and two billing/credit patterns (upfront credits vs monthly smoothing). I attached a simple worksheet (Redwood_Annual_Commit_Tiers_v1.xlsx) with line items; high level summary below:

Starter (good for proof-of-concept)
- Commit: 100M tokens / year
- Effective per-token: $0.00060 (hosted API)
- Options: 25% upfront credit or monthly smoothing (12 equal invoices)

Growth (productizing, scaling)
- Commit: 1B tokens / year
- Effective per-token: $0.00050 (5% discount off Starter)
- Options: 40% upfront credit + 8 monthly invoices, or full smoothing over 12 months

Enterprise (high volume, SLA)
- Commit: 5B tokens / year
- Effective per-token: $0.00035 (volume ladder, deeper discount)
- Options: tiered upfront credits, carry-forward allowance, dedicated capacity add-on available

Key commercial levers we usually see:
- Upfront credits (reduce unit price, immediate cash)
- Monthly smoothing (reduces cash burden, increases predictability for you)
- Rolling buffer (we allow up to 20-25% of unused tokens to roll into the next year at a reduced conversion rate)
- Burst / overage: automatic burst allowed up to X% with overage pricing or credit drawdown

Operational/SLAs:
- Standard hosted SLA: 99.9% availability; Enterprise tier can add 99.95% with prioritized routing and a dedicated burst pool.
- We include cost and latency dashboards per route, and monthly usage reports.

Request: can you confirm (a) which billing cadence you prefer (upfront credits vs smoothing), and (b) a realistic 12-month token projection range? Once we have that I’ll have Priya produce a formal quote and Ben will prepare an SOW with the onboarding milestones.

Next step: 30-minute sync early next week works for me — I can share the worksheet during the call.

Thanks,
Marcus Lin
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments: Redwood_Annual_Commit_Tiers_v1.xlsx


From: Paul Gomez <paul.gomez@olivetech.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Amira Khan <amira.khan@olivetech.com>, OliveTech Procurement <procurement@olivetech.com>
Date: Mon, 1 Mar 2027 10:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Framework: Upfront credits vs monthly smoothing for annual token commit

Marcus —

Thanks for the clear breakdown and the worksheet — helpful. A few quick clarifying questions before I loop in legal and procurement:

1) Token accounting: does the commit count include both prompt + response tokens? Any differences for embeddings/structured output?
2) Rollover: you mentioned rolling up to 20-25% — can credits be banked beyond the year at a conversion rate, or do they expire?
3) Smoothing: if we choose monthly smoothing but actual consumption is front-loaded, is there an ability to true-up mid-year without penalty?
4) Term: is the anchor pricing above tied to a 12-month term (we assume yes) or do you require longer commitments for the Enterprise tier?

We’re targeting a contractor-reviewed budget by March 18; if we can land on the billing pattern and an outline SOW by March 11, procurement can fast-track. I attached our initial usage projection (high/medium/low) — will send the spreadsheet separately via procurement if needed.

—Paul
Paul Gomez
Director of Product, OliveTech
paul.gomez@olivetech.com



From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Paul Gomez <paul.gomez@olivetech.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 1 Mar 2027 14:18:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Framework: Upfront credits vs monthly smoothing for annual token commit

Paul — thanks for the fast notes. Quick answers from billing/pricing perspective: 

- Token accounting: commits cover all hosted API token usage for generation and chat (prompt + model output). Embeddings are billed separately but can be included in a blended commit at negotiated effective rate — we treat embedding tokens as a different SKU in the worksheet. Structured output (function calls) follows the same token accounting as generation.

- Rollover policy: standard rollover allows up to 25% of the committed volume to carry into the next 12-month term at a 50% conversion efficiency (i.e., 1 unused committed token converts to 0.5 available token next year). This prevents banking large unused volumes while giving runway. For Enterprise we can extend a larger carry-forward with a conversion fee.

- Mid-year true-up: we support a one-time true-up per 12-month period without penalty (we'll invoice the incremental committed units at the contracted effective price). For front-loaded consumption we can also front-deadline adjust the smoothing schedule if notified 30 days in advance — this protects both sides from unexpected cashflow hits.

- Term length: Starter & Growth are typically 12-month terms. Enterprise pricing bands can be extended to 24 months for a marginally better rate (we tie deeper discounts to longer financial commitments).

I can convert the worksheet into a formal quote once we have Paul's expected 12-month range and preferred billing pattern. Ben — if you want to include a limited onboarding credit (10% of first-year spend) on early signature, say so and I’ll reflect it in the quote.

Priya Natarajan
Head of Pricing & Commercial Ops, Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Paul Gomez <paul.gomez@olivetech.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Mar 2027 08:46:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Framework: Upfront credits vs monthly smoothing for annual token commit

Paul / Marcus —

Echoing Priya — a couple of commercial options to make procurement comfortable while keeping unit economics predictable:

1) Blended ramp: year 1 ramped effective rate where the first 3 months are billed at a slightly higher per-token rate (to cover onboarding), then step down across quarters to the committed effective rate. This avoids large upfront cash while giving Redwood near-term coverage.

2) Early-sign bonus: if OliveTech signs an MSA + SOW by March 18, we can include a one-time 10% onboarding credit applied against first-year consumption (not refundable).

3) SOW milestones: propose a 60-day pilot milestone (activation, two integrations, smoke test) and a fuller GA onboarding at 90 days. SOW draft attached; Marcus will share official SOW and we can convert to DocuSign once terms are agreed.

Laura — looping you for billing ops review on smoothing cadence and invoicing.

Happy to jump on a 30-min call Wed/Thu to walk through the worksheet and align on a billing pattern.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Head of Sales, Platform Solutions, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com

Attachments: SOW_OliveTech_draft.docx


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Paul Gomez <paul.gomez@olivetech.com>, Amira Khan <amira.khan@olivetech.com>, OliveTech Procurement <procurement@olivetech.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Mar 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Framework: Upfront credits vs monthly smoothing for annual token commit

Paul / Amira —

Thanks for the questions and for looping procurement. Next steps I propose based on the thread: 

1) You confirm preferred billing pattern (Upfront credit vs monthly smoothing vs blended ramp) — if you’re undecided, indicate which of the three scenarios in the worksheet feels closest to expected behavior (High/Medium/Low).
2) OliveTech to share the preferred 12-month volume band (or the three-point projection you mentioned).
3) Redwood will produce: (a) a formal pricing quote reflecting chosen billing pattern, (b) a draft MSA with the agreed rollover and true-up language, and (c) a draft SOW with the 60/90-day milestones and success criteria.

If OliveTech signs by March 18 we can apply the 10% onboarding credit Ben mentioned and target a April 1 activation date. I’m free Thurs Mar 4 between 9–11am PT or Fri Mar 5 after 1pm PT — if either works, I’ll set up a short call and share the worksheet live.

I’ll attach the two files again for convenience. Let me know which slot works and we’ll lock it in.

Best,
Marcus
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments: Redwood_Annual_Commit_Tiers_v1.xlsx; SOW_OliveTech_draft.docx

source.subject: Request: incubator trial extension & milestone-based credits
messages:
From: Samir Shah <sam@greenfork.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 1 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: incubator trial extension & additional credits

Hi Alex,

Thanks again for the intro call this morning — product looks promising. We hit a wall on our final demo because we blew through the initial trial allocation while stress-testing embeddings and streaming. The accelerator requires a working demo for Friday and our usage projection says we need another 25k token-equivalent credits to get through tuning + demo.

Can Redwood extend our incubator trial and add a temporary credit boost? We'd be happy to share an updated milestone plan and a short usage snapshot to justify the ask.

Cc'ing Deb (accelerator ops) who can surface the milestone requirements required by the incubator.

Best,
Samir Shah
Co-founder, GreenFork Labs
---
Attachment: greenfork_mvp_usage_snapshot.csv (csv, 23KB)
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Shah <sam@greenfork.com>
Cc: Deb Nguyen <deb@greenfork.com>
Date: Mon, 1 Mar 2027 11:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: incubator trial extension & additional credits

Samir —

Appreciate the context and the usage snapshot — helps a lot. Short answer: we can usually make an incubator exception for a short extension if it's tied to a clear milestone and a quick follow-up demo.

Proposal:
- 30-day extension on the trial window
- Up to 30k additional token-equivalent credits now (conditional)
- Another 10k credits release if you complete the Friday demo and share a 5-min recording + short success metrics doc within 7 days

If that sounds good, I'll loop in Priya from our program ops to approve and update the grant paperwork. Also flagging internally: please confirm the final demo time so we can watch and prioritize any runtime support.

Quick ask for you: can you list the top 3 API endpoints and expected peak qps during the demo? That will help us ensure no rate-limit surprises.

Thanks —
Alex Martinez
Senior Sales, Startup Programs
Redwood Inference
From: Samir Shah <sam@greenfork.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.ai>
Cc: Deb Nguyen <deb@greenfork.com>
Date: Mon, 1 Mar 2027 12:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: incubator trial extension & additional credits

Perfect — that structure works for us. Final demo is scheduled for Friday 2027-03-05 at 10:00am PT.

Top endpoints / expected peak qps during demo:
1) /v1/generate (multiturn chat, primary) — peak 8 qps
2) /v1/embeddings (batch 16) — peak 3 qps
3) /v1/rerank (single-shot) — peak 2 qps

We'll aim to record the demo and send the 5-min clip + 1-page metrics doc within 48 hours after the demo. Thanks for the quick turnaround on this.

Samir
From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samir Shah <sam@greenfork.com>, Deb Nguyen <deb@greenfork.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Mar 2027 08:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Approval: incubator extension + conditional credits

Alex/Jordan —

I reviewed GreenFork's usage snapshot and the accelerator plan. Given the demo timeline and that this is an active cohort, I approve the following:
- 30-day trial extension (start date remains original, end date extended by 30 days)
- Immediate grant of 30,000 token-equivalent credits
- Hold 10,000 additional credits for milestone release (deliver demo recording + metrics)

Conditions: credits expire at the end of the extended trial window; sampler cannot be used for data-export or redistribution to third parties per incubator terms. Please have the founders sign the incubator_terms_summary.pdf (linked) before credits are applied.

I will open the internal allocation and attach the terms. Jordan, can you confirm the accounting code so finance knows which pool to debit?

Thanks —
Priya Natarajan
Programs Operations Lead
Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: incubator_terms_summary.pdf (pdf, 42KB)
From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.ai>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samir Shah <sam@greenfork.com>, Deb Nguyen <deb@greenfork.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Mar 2027 14:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Approval: incubator extension + conditional credits

All set — finance code INCUB-2027-Q1 assigned and grant requested. Priya, I'll follow up if they ask for any extra info. Samir/Deb: once you sign the attached incubator_terms_summary.pdf I'll push the credits and send a confirmation email with the new expiration date and the grant id.

Alex — thanks for coordinating. Samir — good luck on Friday — we can have an engineer on standby for the demo if you need in-call troubleshooting.

Jordan Blake
Growth Partnerships
Redwood Inference

source.subject: SLA credit request — content-filter fallback and shared remediation plan
messages:
From: Samir Patel <samir@finetext.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement <procurement@finetext.ai>
Date: 2027-02-20T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Major latency/SLA hits this morning — request for credit and plan

Ben,

We saw a significant spike in tail latency starting ~08:40 PST today; our customer-facing endpoints backed by the Redwood API reported increased 500/timeout rates and elevated P95/P99. We believe the root cause is the content-filter module invoking downstream moderation tooling redundantly — when the filter path returned an error the runtime retried and amplified latency.

This impacted three production services at Finetext (indexing, assistant, and summarization). Our ops attach a CSV with the request IDs and timestamps. We need: a formal SLA credit for the period of 08:40–09:50 PST and a remediation plan with timelines. Please escalate — this impacts our renewal conversation.

Attachments: filter-incident-log-20270220.csv (CSV, 124KB)

Regards,
Samir Patel
Head of Platform Engineering
Finetext Labs
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@finetext.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-02-20T10:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Major latency/SLA hits this morning — request for credit and plan

Samir — thanks for the flag and the incident log. I’m sorry for the disruption; I’ve looped in Laura (Account Lead) and Karthik (SRE) so we can move quickly.

Initial read: the CSV shows a cluster of timeouts correlated with a spike in moderation-toolbox latency. Short-term options we can put in place today:
- Temporarily reduce filter strictness (fail-open for non-security signals) to stop retries that blow out latency
- Route critical traffic to a degraded model variant with lower tool-calling dependency (cost/quality tradeoff)

I’m attaching a proposed mitigation plan that outlines a 48–72 hour rollback and patch schedule. Before we finalize credit, we need to confirm scope (which endpoints/consumer apps are in-scope) and verify the incident window. Can you confirm the list of affected endpoint names and whether indexed data loss occurred?

I will coordinate a formal credit proposal and an expedited patch timeline once SRE confirms root cause.

Attachment: proposed-mitigation-plan-FT-31415.pdf (PDF, 85KB)

—
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0198
From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-02-20T12:05:00-08:00
Subject: Internal: approval for provisional credit and fast-track patch

Ben / Karthik — internal note (do not forward to Finetext until legal & finance sign off).

Summary of recommended customer-facing position: offer an operational credit for the measured SLA window and commit to a firm remediation plan with a date-based milestone. Proposed credit: 10% service credit for February billing applied to Finetext's account (FT-31415). Rationale: the incident appears to be caused by interaction between our filter retry logic and an upstream moderation provider slow-down — we own the overall runtime behavior.

Karthik: can SRE provide a short incident RCA and an ETA for the filter patch (we’re aiming for 48–72 hours)? Finance/Legal: (cc’d out of this thread separately) please confirm the mechanics of applying a one-off credit.

If we can hit the patch and a scheduled maintenance window this week, I’ll authorize Ben to present the credit + remediation commitment to Finetext.

Laura Bennett
Senior Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@finetext.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-02-28T16:40:00-08:00
Subject: RE: Major latency/SLA hits this morning — credit and remediation plan

Samir — thank you for your patience while we validated the incident. Below is what we’re committing to and next steps.

Decision and credit
- Redwood will issue a one-time 10% service credit for the billing period that includes 2027-02-20, applied to your February invoice (FT-31415). Finance will process this within two billing cycles.

Mitigation and timeline
- Root cause (summary): filter retry amplification against a transient moderation-provider slowdown led to cascading retries and elevated P99 latency. RCA doc: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/INF-8721
- Immediate action (done): we activated a controlled fail-open for non-security content signals on 2027-02-20 at 11:10 PST which reduced timeouts for critical endpoints.
- Patch schedule: SRE will deploy the filter retry limit and improved backoff on 2027-03-02 during a scheduled maintenance window (02:00–03:00 PST).
- Post-deploy validation: we’ll run a focused synthetic workload and share the runbook/metrics within 24 hours of the deploy.

Shared remediation offer
- Because this impacted your critical production traffic, Redwood will absorb the 10% credit. We also propose a joint post-mortem call and a shared test plan to validate production behavior before your renewal. If Finetext wants an accelerated patch with dedicated engineering support, we can provide a prioritized engagement (SOW/price to be discussed).

Next steps for you
1) Confirm the list of production endpoints you want explicitly included in the credit scope.
2) Provide a convenient window for a post-mortem call (I’ve cc’d Priya from Solutions to coordinate).

Thanks again — we want to make this right and ensure there’s no repeat.

—
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0198

- Immediate action (done): we activated a controlled fail-open for non-security content signals on 2027-02-20 at 11:10 PST which reduced timeouts for critical endpoints.
- Patch schedule: SRE will deploy the filter retry limit and improved backoff on 2027-03-02 during a scheduled maintenance window (02:00–03:00 PST).
- Post-deploy validation: we’ll run a focused synthetic workload and share the runbook/metrics within 24 hours of the deploy.

Shared remediation offer
- Because this impacted your critical production traffic, Redwood will absorb the 10% credit. We also propose a joint post-mortem call and a shared test plan to validate production behavior before your renewal. If Finetext wants an accelerated patch with dedicated engineering support, we can provide a prioritized engagement (SOW/price to be discussed).

Next steps for you
1) Confirm the list of production endpoints you want explicitly included in the credit scope.
2) Provide a convenient window for a post-mortem call (I’ve cc’d Priya from Solutions to coordinate).

Thanks again — we want to make this right and ensure there’s no repeat.

—
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0198

source.subject: Host tenancy mapping + data-flow annotated deliverables (SOC2/SIG request)
messages:
From: Oliver Grant <oliver.grant@bigbank.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 20 Feb 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Host tenancy mapping + data-flow artifacts for SOC2/SIG review

Camila,

Thanks for taking this — BigBank is in the final stages of their vendor risk review and the SOC2/SIG team raised a few follow-ups specific to Dedicated and Private deployments. Their ask is twofold: an annotated "host tenancy mapping" showing which compute pools are used by Dedicated vs Private customers and a data-flow diagram that highlights encryption-in-transit boundaries, KMS boundaries, and any cross-tenant egress paths.

Concretely they requested:
- A single-page annotated network diagram (PDF/SVG) showing control plane vs data plane, edge routing, and optional on-prem connectors.
- A short mapping table (CSV) listing components, tenancy model (shared/isolated/dedicated host), and any customer-facing network endpoints.
- A one-paragraph narrative describing fallback behaviour (e.g., model fallback routes, cross-region failover) and any observable telemetry we emit (logs/flow logs).

Can you confirm turnaround? Ideally by Tue EOD so we can attach to the SIG questionnaire.

Oliver
Security Engineer — BigBank Financial Services
security@bigbank.com

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Oliver Grant <oliver.grant@bigbank.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 20 Feb 2027 11:35:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Host tenancy mapping + data-flow artifacts for SOC2/SIG review

Hi Oliver — appreciate the clear list. We can produce the requested artifacts. Outline below and next steps: 

Planned deliverables (target: Tue 2027-02-22 EOD):
- redwood_host_tenancy_mapping_v1.pdf (single-page annotated diagram) — shows Dedicated GPU pools, Private VPC connectors, control plane endpoints, and optional air-gapped flows.
- redwood_network_annotated.svg — vector version for your architecture team to inspect; will include layer callouts.
- service_boundary_matrix.csv — component / tenancy / encryption / KMS column mapping.
- Short narrative (one paragraph) included in the PDF and in the email body; call out fallback/routing behaviour.

A few clarifications I need before we finalize:
1) For "Private" deployments are you specifically referencing customer-managed on-prem packages (air-gapped) or VPC-hosted Private with our control plane? We will flag differences in the diagram.
2) Do you need any packet-capture evidence or flow logs, or is the mapping sufficient for SIG review?

Ben, Priya — looping you in for technical review and the CSV. Ben, can you take the SVG and populate the callouts? Priya, can you validate the KMS boundary text?

I will assemble the first-pass artifacts in Drive and share links; attaching a short appendix stub in the deliverable.

Thanks,
Camila Reyes
Enterprise Security & Solutions
Redwood Inference
(Attachment: redwood_host_tenancy_mapping_v1.pdf)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Oliver Grant <oliver.grant@bigbank.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Feb 2027 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Host tenancy mapping + data-flow artifacts for SOC2/SIG review

Camila / Oliver — draft artifacts attached. Quick technical summary here so the reviewers have context: 

Attachment notes: redwood_network_annotated.svg (vector) and service_boundary_matrix.csv.

Technical highlights (short):
- Dedicated: customers on Dedicated are scheduled onto reserved GPU pools (node-pinning optional) within our tenant-fenced Kubernetes clusters. Network isolation is achieved via VPC per-tenant routing + host-network policies; data-plane egress is limited to per-tenant NAT gateways where required. All inter-node traffic is encrypted using mTLS; KV caches and model weights rest on encrypted block devices (AES-256).
- Private (VPC-hosted): control plane calls (telemetry, billing, model catalog) can be routed over a peered, TLS-protected control plane endpoint. Customer-managed Private (air-gapped) has an explicit "sync appliance" flow documented in the SVG — no outbound connections from compute nodes by default.
- KMS: Customer-managed Private options can either (a) use Redwood KMS via a peered HSM interface, or (b) integrate their own KMS/HSM; column in CSV shows kms_integration: redwood_kms | customer_kms.
- Fallbacks: when a Dedicated variant is unavailable, the request is routed to a compatible hosted variant in a lower-cost pool; routing decisions logged to the request trace and anonymized in metrics.

Drive link (preview): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aB2cExampleLink/redwood-evidence

Attachment stubs included: redwood_network_annotated.svg, service_boundary_matrix.csv, redwood_host_tenancy_mapping_v1.pdf

--- quoted for context ---
On Sat, Feb 20, 2027 at 09:12 AM Oliver Grant <oliver.grant@bigbank.com> wrote:
> Camila,
>
> Thanks for taking this — BigBank is in the final stages of their vendor risk review and the SOC2/SIG team raised a few follow-ups specific to Dedicated and Private deployments. Their ask is twofold: an annotated \"host tenancy mapping\" showing which compute pools are used by Dedicated vs Private customers and a data-flow diagram that highlights encryption-in-transit boundaries, KMS boundaries, and any cross-tenant egress paths.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Infrastructure Engineer
Redwood Inference
(Attachment: redwood_network_annotated.svg)

From: Oliver Grant <oliver.grant@bigbank.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 23 Feb 2027 16:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Host tenancy mapping + data-flow artifacts for SOC2/SIG review

Camila / Ben — thank you, this is exactly what the SIG engineering reviewers wanted. A couple quick clarifications from their notes: 

1) In the SVG callout for the control plane, can you explicitly annotate which endpoints accept customer identity tokens vs system service tokens? They want the distinction for their threat model.
2) The CSV column kms_integration — can you add a cell noting whether CMK rotation is customer-controlled for Private with customer KMS?

If you can drop a small patch (SVG + updated CSV) we can close the ticket and attach to the SIG submission. Appreciate the quick turnaround.

Oliver
Security Engineer — BigBank Financial Services
(Quoted)
> On Mon, Feb 22, 2027 at 10:05 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> > Camila / Oliver — draft artifacts attached. Quick technical summary here so the reviewers have context:...

(Attachment: acknowledgement.txt)

source.subject: Request: executive value snapshot & reference for PeakCloud renewal
messages:
From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@peakcloud.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Asha Rao <asha.rao@peakcloud.com>
Date: Sat, 20 Feb 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Quick favor — executive snapshot for our renewal review

Hi Jordan / Monica,

We're heading into our procurement/finance review next week and they asked for a short executive one-pager from Redwood that summarizes business outcomes we've realized to date. Specifically:
- a 1-paragraph summary of net business benefit (cost/efficiency/velocity)
- 2–3 quantified metrics we can cite (savings, latency, adoption trends)
- permission to use a short quote from our VP of Engineering in marketing materials (if PeakCloud agrees)

Can you send something by EOD Monday? If you already have a short PDF or dashboard snapshot, that'd be ideal.

Thanks — Liam
Liam O'Connor
Head of Procurement, PeakCloud
liam.oconnor@peakcloud.com

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 20 Feb 2027 11:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Quick favor — executive snapshot for our renewal review

Looping you all in. Procurement wants a tight one-pager they can hand to finance. Need to include: realized cost delta, p95 latency change, and adoption growth (active routes / seats).

Quick notes: 
- From CS instrumentation: PeakCloud token spend per month down ~28% vs baseline due to prefix caching and batching (Jan 2027 vs Sep 2026).
- From telemetry: p95 gen latency improved from 520ms -> 190ms after moving to dedicated pool for core model.
- Product: daily active routes up 3.6x (pilot -> rollout).

Evelyn — can you confirm the dollarized savings figure (we have a draft in the deal workspace, but want verified). Priya — can you drop the usage dashboard screenshot and the short one-pager template here so Jordan can send a polished note to Liam.

Thanks — Monica

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@peakcloud.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 21 Feb 2027 08:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Quick favor — executive snapshot for our renewal review

Hi Liam — thanks for the heads up. Attached is a concise "Value Snapshot" (PDF) and a one-slide dashboard PNG that your team can hand to procurement/finance. Key points up front: 

Executive summary (2 lines):
Redwood enabled PeakCloud to reduce inference unit cost while improving request latency and scaleability for high-throughput routes. That lowered per-request cost and shortened time-to-respond for core customer flows, directly supporting PeakCloud's revenue-critical real-time features.

Quantified outcomes (you can cite these):
- Approx. 28% reduction in inference token spend on production routes (Jan 2027 vs Sep 2026) — engineering optimizations and caching.
- p95 generation latency reduced from ~520ms to ~190ms after Dedicated pool rollout.
- 3.6x growth in daily active inference routes since pilot (higher user engagement on realtime product features).

Attached: value-snapshot-peakcloud-2027Q1.pdf (one-page executive brief), usage-dashboard-feb2027.png (dashboard excerpt).

Permissions: If PeakCloud is comfortable, we'd love a 15–20 word quote from your VP of Engineering (or procurement exec) that we can use in a short renewal case reference and in a customer quote section. Example: 

"Redwood helped us cut inference costs and unlock real-time features across our platform."

If that works, reply to this thread with a sentence we can use and whether we have permission to attribute it to PeakCloud.

Happy to hop on a quick 20-min prep call if you want to align language for procurement.

Best,
Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
[jordan.blake@redwood.com]
[attachment: value-snapshot-peakcloud-2027Q1.pdf (PDF, 98 KB)]
[attachment: usage-dashboard-feb2027.png (PNG, 420 KB)]

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Feb 2027 10:30:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Quick favor — executive snapshot for our renewal review

Nice pack Jordan — added a tiny permission template in case they want to formalize publishing rights. Also confirmed the dollarized savings: based on committed usage and observed batching/caching, the client realized ~$86k/month in inference spend reduction on measured routes (sensitivity band +/-10%). That number is in the attached Drive doc and the CRM note.

Monica — once Liam confirms permission, I'll draft a short case blurb we can use in the upsell playbook and partner materials. Priya — please add the high-res dashboard to the Drive link and mark the version (v1.1).

[attachment: permission-template.txt (text, 1 KB)]

Thanks — Evelyn
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference

From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@peakcloud.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Asha Rao <asha.rao@peakcloud.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Feb 2027 16:47:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Quick favor — executive snapshot for our renewal review

Jordan / team — this is perfect, thanks. We can share the PDF with procurement later today. PeakCloud is happy to give permission to use the following short quote attributed to "PeakCloud, VP Engineering":

"Redwood reduced our inference costs and latency, enabling features that improved customer engagement."

You have permission to use that line in marketing and renewal materials. No logo usage without a quick review, but the sentence above is approved.

Appreciate the quick turnaround — Liam
Liam O'Connor
Head of Procurement, PeakCloud

Subject: Re: Quick favor — executive snapshot for our renewal review

Looping you all in. Procurement wants a tight one-pager they can hand to finance. Need to include: realized cost delta, p95 latency change, and adoption growth (active routes / seats).

Quick notes: 
- From CS instrumentation: PeakCloud token spend per month down ~28% vs baseline due to prefix caching and batching (Jan 2027 vs Sep 2026).
- From telemetry: p95 gen latency improved from 520ms -> 190ms after moving to dedicated pool for core model.
- Product: daily active routes up 3.6x (pilot -> rollout).

Evelyn — can you confirm the dollarized savings figure (we have a draft in the deal workspace, but want verified). Priya — can you drop the usage dashboard screenshot and the short one-pager template here so Jordan can send a polished note to Liam.

Thanks — Monica

To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@peakcloud.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 21 Feb 2027 08:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Quick favor — executive snapshot for our renewal review

Hi Liam — thanks for the heads up. Attached is a concise "Value Snapshot" (PDF) and a one-slide dashboard PNG that your team can hand to procurement/finance. Key points up front: 

Executive summary (2 lines):
Redwood enabled PeakCloud to reduce inference unit cost while improving request latency and scaleability for high-throughput routes. That lowered per-request cost and shortened time-to-respond for core customer flows, directly supporting PeakCloud's revenue-critical real-time features.

Quantified outcomes (you can cite these):
- Approx. 28% reduction in inference token spend on production routes (Jan 2027 vs Sep 2026) — engineering optimizations and caching.
- p95 generation latency reduced from ~520ms to ~190ms after Dedicated pool rollout.
- 3.6x growth in daily active inference routes since pilot (higher user engagement on realtime product features).

Attached: value-snapshot-peakcloud-2027Q1.pdf (one-page executive brief), usage-dashboard-feb2027.png (dashboard excerpt).

Permissions: If PeakCloud is comfortable, we'd love a 15–20 word quote from your VP of Engineering (or procurement exec) that we can use in a short renewal case reference and in a customer quote section. Example: 

"Redwood helped us cut inference costs and unlock real-time features across our platform."

If that works, reply to this thread with a sentence we can use and whether we have permission to attribute it to PeakCloud.

Happy to hop on a quick 20-min prep call if you want to align language for procurement.

Best,
Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
[jordan.blake@redwood.com]

[attachment: usage-dashboard-feb2027.png (PNG, 420 KB)]

That number is in the attached Drive doc and the CRM note.

Monica — once Liam confirms permission, I'll draft a short case blurb we can use in the upsell playbook and partner materials. Priya — please add the high-res dashboard to the Drive link and mark the version (v1.1).

[attachment: permission-template.txt (text, 1 KB)]

Thanks — Evelyn
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference

source.subject: OrbitStack integration exception — private listing + SSO asks
messages:
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Mon, 8 Feb 2027 15:12:00 -0800
Subject: OrbitStack listing — integration exception & product asks

Team,

OrbitStack (contact: Elena Morales) circled back with a hard requirement for their cloud marketplace listing: they need our private deployment offering to be discoverable through the marketplace connector while preserving SSO requirements and keeping pricing off the public listing. Their ask implies two product signals:

- Ability to opt a Private deployment (VPC/on-prem) into the marketplace catalog without exposing list pricing or public signup flows.
- A SAML/OIDC handshake path that can be initiated from the marketplace redirect but completes against the customer's private environment (they have a test tenant).

I attached their integration checklist (orbitstack-integration-checklist.xlsx) and a short SSO spec they supplied. High level asks for us:

1) Product: one-paragraph on feasibility and major risks (compliance/OPS).
2) Eng: rough scope (approx number of stars / effort) and whether a narrow 'listing exception' path is possible short-term.
3) Legal/Sales: whether OrbitStack's request to keep pricing off the public page requires contract exceptions.

Please reply with concise answers — Elena is pushing a procurement timeline and needs internal alignment in the next 48 hours.

Hannah
Hannah Schmitt
Head of Partnerships, Redwood Inference
hannah@redwood.com
Redwood Inference

Attachment: orbitstack-integration-checklist.xlsx (spreadsheet, 64KB)
Attachment: orbitstack-sso-specs.pdf (pdf, 120KB)
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>, Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Mon, 8 Feb 2027 17:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: OrbitStack listing — integration exception & product asks

Thanks Hannah — quick note from infra/serving runtime perspective:

- Private deployments today do not emit the marketplace metadata (catalog entry, discovery metadata) by default. Adding that is feasible but will require changes across the control plane and the private image/packaging layer.
- The SAML flow you describe (marketplace initiates SSO, then redirect to customer-managed IdP) is doable if we add a pass-through token and a short-lived redirect flow. Main risk: we must not persist any marketplace tokens in the customer's KV store unless explicitly requested.

Estimated rough effort: 3-5 sprint-equivalents for a minimal 'exception' path (engineering + QA), assuming we can reuse our existing SSO bridge. If we need a UI/Console surface for making the private instance discoverable, tack on another 2 sprints.

Questions for OrbitStack before we commit: do they require us to host any part of the redirect sequence (i.e., long-lived callback endpoints) or can all handshake traffic be proxied?

— Aditya
Aditya Rao
Senior Engineering Manager, Serving Runtime
aditya@redwood.com

On Mon, Feb 8, 2027 at 3:12 PM Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team,
>
> OrbitStack (contact: Elena Morales) circled back ...
From: Elena Morales <elena@orbitstack.io>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>
Cc: Marketplace Ops <ops@marketplaces.example>, Partner Success <ps@orbitstack.io>
Date: Tue, 9 Feb 2027 09:24:00 -0800
Subject: Re: OrbitStack listing — integration exception & product asks

Hi Hannah (and Redwood team),

Thanks for the quick response. To clarify on our side:

- We cannot have list pricing shown publicly — our customers procure via our enterprise sales motion. The marketplace listing should act as a discovery + redirect channel only.
- For SSO we can provide SAML metadata for a test tenant. We prefer a model where the marketplace triggers the discovery and then redirects the buyer to our SSO initiation endpoint; we will handle the IdP exchange on our end.
- We can provide a short-lived test tenant for integration and legal can sign a standard non-public pricing addendum if required.

Procurement note: if we can show a short pilot path (non-public listing + SSO redirect) we can start pilots in March and go GA in April. That timeline is tight for us.

Happy to hop on a 30-minute technical call if that helps — I can include our engineer (Sanjay) and our procurement lead.

Best,
Elena Morales
Head of Product Partnerships, OrbitStack
elena@orbitstack.io

On Mon, Feb 8, 2027 at 3:12 PM Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team,
>
> OrbitStack (contact: Elena Morales) circled back ...
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 9 Feb 2027 11:10:00 -0800
Subject: Re: OrbitStack listing — integration exception & product asks

Quick product assessment:

- Feasibility: OK for a scoped exception. We have two paths: a) quick 'discovery-only' pipeline that surfaces an entry in the marketplace catalog but routes all clicks to OrbitStack's external workflow (no pricing, no signup) — lower effort; b) full integration that lets OrbitStack toggle a private instance and surface telemetry/data after explicit opt-in — higher effort and compliance checks.

- Proposed MVP: path (a) + SAML redirect hookup. Minimal console UI: toggle per-customer listing visibility (admin only).

- Dependencies & risks: legal sign-off on non-public pricing clause; infra changes to allow marketplace metadata on private deployments; E2E testing for SAML redirect flows (security review).

Estimated timeline for MVP: 6 weeks from greenlight (3 sprints infra+eng, 1 sprint QA, 2 weeks product/ops rollout).

I put a short RFC in the internal doc: https://app.redwood.com/docs/internal/marketplace-private-listing-rfc — please add comments.

Camila Reyes
Product Manager, Marketplace Integrations
camila@redwood.com

On Tue, Feb 9, 2027 at 09:24 AM Elena Morales <elena@orbitstack.io> wrote:
> Hi Hannah (and Redwood team),
>
> Thanks for the quick response...
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Morales <elena@orbitstack.io>, Marketplace Ops <ops@marketplaces.example>
Date: Wed, 10 Feb 2027 11:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: OrbitStack listing — integration exception & product asks

Thanks everyone — summary and next steps: 

Summary:
- OrbitStack requires a discovery-only marketplace listing + SAML redirect to their IdP. Pricing must remain off the public page.
- Product (Camila): MVP = discovery-only + SAML redirect; 6-week ETA.
- Eng (Aditya): 3-5 sprints for scoped exception; extra UI work may add 2 sprints.
- Legal/Sales: OrbitStack will accept a non-public pricing addendum; legal to confirm contract language.

Action items (owners / target dates):
- Aditya: scope ticket with infra details and estimated sprint plan — due 2027-02-15.
- Camila: draft MVP acceptance criteria and console toggle mock — due 2027-02-16.
- Priya (Sales): confirm procurement timeline with OrbitStack and share HubSpot deal (OS-2027-0047) — due 2027-02-12.
- Legal (cc: legal@redwood.com): review non-public pricing addendum and provide redlines — due 2027-02-17.
- Ops/Marketplace: confirm whether listing can be made discovery-only with no public pricing — ops@marketplaces.example.

Elena: can you confirm the test tenant SAML metadata and availability window (they mentioned March pilot)? If yes, Camila and Aditya will start the RFC and scoping work immediately.

I'll follow up with a calendar invite for a 30-minute sync once Aditya posts the initial scope.

Hannah
Hannah Schmitt
Head of Partnerships, Redwood Inference
hannah@redwood.com

Attachments referenced: orbitstack-integration-checklist.xlsx, orbitstack-sso-specs.pdf
Related HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/123456/deal/OS-2027-0047

Subject: Re: OrbitStack listing — integration exception & product asks

Thanks Hannah — quick note from infra/serving runtime perspective:

- Private deployments today do not emit the marketplace metadata (catalog entry, discovery metadata) by default. Adding that is feasible but will require changes across the control plane and the private image/packaging layer.
- The SAML flow you describe (marketplace initiates SSO, then redirect to customer-managed IdP) is doable if we add a pass-through token and a short-lived redirect flow. Main risk: we must not persist any marketplace tokens in the customer's KV store unless explicitly requested.

Estimated rough effort: 3-5 sprint-equivalents for a minimal 'exception' path (engineering + QA), assuming we can reuse our existing SSO bridge. If we need a UI/Console surface for making the private instance discoverable, tack on another 2 sprints.

Questions for OrbitStack before we commit: do they require us to host any part of the redirect sequence (i.e., long-lived callback endpoints) or can all handshake traffic be proxied?

— Aditya
Aditya Rao
Senior Engineering Manager, Serving Runtime
aditya@redwood.com

On Mon, Feb 8, 2027 at 3:12 PM Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team,
>
> OrbitStack (contact: Elena Morales) circled back ...


From: Elena Morales <elena@orbitstack.io>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>
Cc: Marketplace Ops <ops@marketplaces.example>, Partner Success <ps@orbitstack.io>
Date: Tue, 9 Feb 2027 09:24:00 -0800
Subject: Re: OrbitStack listing — integration exception & product asks

Hi Hannah (and Redwood team),

Thanks for the quick response. To clarify on our side:

- We cannot have list pricing shown publicly — our customers procure via our enterprise sales motion. The marketplace listing should act as a discovery + redirect channel only.
- For SSO we can provide SAML metadata for a test tenant. We prefer a model where the marketplace triggers the discovery and then redirects the buyer to our SSO initiation endpoint; we will handle the IdP exchange on our end.
- We can provide a short-lived test tenant for integration and legal can sign a standard non-public pricing addendum if required.

Procurement note: if we can show a short pilot path (non-public listing + SSO redirect) we can start pilots in March and go GA in April. That timeline is tight for us.

Happy to hop on a 30-minute technical call if that helps — I can include our engineer (Sanjay) and our procurement lead.

Best,
Elena Morales
Head of Product Partnerships, OrbitStack
elena@orbitstack.io

On Mon, Feb 8, 2027 at 3:12 PM Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team,
>
> OrbitStack (contact: Elena Morales) circled back ...


From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 9 Feb 2027 11:10:00 -0800
Subject: Re: OrbitStack listing — integration exception & product asks

Quick product assessment:

- Feasibility: OK for a scoped exception. We have two paths: a) quick 'discovery-only' pipeline that surfaces an entry in the marketplace catalog but routes all clicks to OrbitStack's external workflow (no pricing, no signup) — lower effort; b) full integration that lets OrbitStack toggle a private instance and surface telemetry/data after explicit opt-in — higher effort and compliance checks.

- Proposed MVP: path (a) + SAML redirect hookup. Minimal console UI: toggle per-customer listing visibility (admin only).

- Dependencies & risks: legal sign-off on non-public pricing clause; infra changes to allow marketplace metadata on private deployments; E2E testing for SAML redirect flows (security review).

Estimated timeline for MVP: 6 weeks from greenlight (3 sprints infra+eng, 1 sprint QA, 2 weeks product/ops rollout).

I put a short RFC in the internal doc: https://app.redwood.com/docs/internal/marketplace-private-listing-rfc — please add comments.

Camila Reyes
Product Manager, Marketplace Integrations
camila@redwood.com

On Tue, Feb 9, 2027 at 09:24 AM Elena Morales <elena@orbitstack.io> wrote:
> Hi Hannah (and Redwood team),
>
> Thanks for the quick response...


From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Morales <elena@orbitstack.io>, Marketplace Ops <ops@marketplaces.example>
Date: Wed, 10 Feb 2027 11:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: OrbitStack listing — integration exception & product asks

Thanks everyone — summary and next steps: 

Summary:
- OrbitStack requires a discovery-only marketplace listing + SAML redirect to their IdP. Pricing must remain off the public page.
- Product (Camila): MVP = discovery-only + SAML redirect; 6-week ETA.
- Eng (Aditya): 3-5 sprints for scoped exception; extra UI work may add 2 sprints.
- Legal/Sales: OrbitStack will accept a non-public pricing addendum; legal to confirm contract language.

Action items (owners / target dates):
- Aditya: scope ticket with infra details and estimated sprint plan — due 2027-02-15.
- Camila: draft MVP acceptance criteria and console toggle mock — due 2027-02-16.
- Priya (Sales): confirm procurement timeline with OrbitStack and share HubSpot deal (OS-2027-0047) — due 2027-02-12.
- Legal (cc: legal@redwood.com): review non-public pricing addendum and provide redlines — due 2027-02-17.
- Ops/Marketplace: confirm whether listing can be made discovery-only with no public pricing — ops@marketplaces.example.

Elena: can you confirm the test tenant SAML metadata and availability window (they mentioned March pilot)? If yes, Camila and Aditya will start the RFC and scoping work immediately.

I'll follow up with a calendar invite for a 30-minute sync once Aditya posts the initial scope.

Hannah
Hannah Schmitt
Head of Partnerships, Redwood Inference
hannah@redwood.com

Attachments referenced: orbitstack-integration-checklist.xlsx, orbitstack-sso-specs.pdf
Related HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/123456/deal/OS-2027-0047

source.subject: SIG questionnaire — consolidated evidence and access for Aegis Health
messages:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@aegishealth.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.n@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 18 Jan 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: SIG questionnaire — consolidated evidence and access for Aegis Health

Hi Liam / Aegis security team,

Thanks again for the quick review of the SIG questionnaire. Per our last call, I consolidated the artifacts we have that map to the SIG sections into a single Drive folder so you can iterate on what else you need: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aB2cDEfG_SIG_Aegis_Consolidated.

What I uploaded in the top-level folder:
- sig_evidence_index_v1.xlsx (index that maps SIG question IDs to files)
- redwood_kms_policy.pdf (KMS usage and key rotation policy)
- oncall-runbook-sig-section.md (extracted runbook snippets relevant to incident response)
- audit-logs-snapshot-20270117.png (obfuscated screenshot of Cloud audit logs for the retention window)
- architecture-diagram-public.png (redacted network diagram; internal IPs removed)

Notes / asks:
1) Drive permissions: I shared the folder with the emails you provided. If you need org-wide access or prefer a zipped package, tell us and we can export a signed zip.
2) If you need unredacted diagrams for SOC review, we can provide them under a temporary NDA and narrow-scoped access — please confirm who should receive them and the timeframe.
3) We left the runbook snippets as markdown so your reviewers can grep for steps; full oncall playbooks require an additional request (we can deliver by EOD tomorrow).

I attached the index and KMS policy to this email for convenience. Please confirm access and call out any missing SIG IDs from the index and we will prioritize.

Thanks,
Evelyn Hart
Director, GRC Operations
Redwood Inference
attachments: [sig_evidence_index_v1.xlsx, redwood_kms_policy.pdf]

From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@aegishealth.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: Aegis Security Team <security@aegishealth.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Jan 2027 11:03:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: SIG questionnaire — consolidated evidence and access for Aegis Health

Thanks Evelyn — folder opens fine for me. A couple quick clarifications from our side: 

- We need an audit log screenshot that includes the full timestamp range used for retention testing (the current snapshot looks truncated). Can you provide a larger window export (CSV or PNG) and confirm the timezone used?
- For KMS material: is there a summary table of key owners, rotation policy cadence, and whether keys are customer-managed or platform-managed? The PDF is helpful but we prefer a one-page table for procurement.
- On the diagrams: appreciate the redaction. For our internal security reviewers, we will need a version showing subnet CIDR blocks (NOT host IPs) — acceptable if provided with an access expiration.

Michelle from procurement is copied to confirm whether we need the evidence inbound to our secure ShareFile link instead of Drive. Michelle — please advise.

Also tagging the SIG question IDs we care about most: SIG-CR-2.1 (encryption in transit), SIG-CR-3.4 (key rotation), SIG-OP-1.2 (audit logging).

Best,
Liam
Senior Information Security Analyst
Aegis Health

On Mon, Jan 18, 2027 at 09:12 AM Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Liam / Aegis security team,
> 
> Thanks again for the quick review of the SIG questionnaire...

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.n@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@aegishealth.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Jan 2027 13:47:00 -08:00
Subject: Fwd: SIG questionnaire — consolidated evidence and access for Aegis Health

Evelyn — forwarding Liam's notes. Marcus, can you action the audit log export and the subnet-level diagram variant? Details below:

Action items for Marcus:
- Export audit logs for retention test window: full CSV export covering 2026-12-01 through 2027-01-17 in UTC; include column headers and event types. Place CSV in the shared Drive folder and add filename: audit-logs-retention-20261201-20270117.csv.
- Produce a subnet-only architecture diagram (PNG + Visio) that shows CIDR blocks and peering connections but omits host-level addresses. Put both files in the Drive folder and set view access to Aegis reviewers for 14 days.
- Provide a one-page KMS summary table (owners/rotation cadence/managed-by) in XLSX format.

Liam — we can upload directly to ShareFile if procurement prefers; send the secure link and we will push there. Otherwise we'll place everything in Drive and adjust sharing.

Thanks — Priya
Senior Manager, Sales Operations
Redwood Inference

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.n@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@aegishealth.com>, Michelle Park <mpark@aegishealth.com>
Date: Tue, 19 Jan 2027 10:34:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: SIG questionnaire — consolidated evidence and access for Aegis Health

On Tue, Jan 18, 2027 at 01:47 PM Priya Natarajan <priya.n@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Evelyn — forwarding Liam's notes. Marcus, can you action the audit log export and the subnet-level diagram variant?

All items uploaded to the Drive folder (same link Evelyn shared). Files added:
- audit-logs-retention-20261201-20270117.csv (CSV export, UTC timestamps)
- audit-logs-snapshot-full-20270117.png (full-resolution screenshot, marked as redacted for user IDs)
- kms-summary-onepage.xlsx (contains owner, rotation cadence, CMK/PMK column)
- architecture-diagram-cidr.png, architecture-diagram-cidr.vsdx
- oncall-runbook-sig-section.md (full runbook excerpt)
- SIG-evidence-pack_v2.zip (zip of the above with an index)

Permissions: I set the folder to allow the three Aegis emails view access for 14 days. If ShareFile is required, let me know and we'll push the same zip there; ETA for ShareFile push is 2 hours after link is provided.

Notes about redaction: we removed host IPs but preserved CIDR blocks and peering annotations. Audit logs have been sanitized to remove service-account tokens and internal-only principals. If you need unsanitized logs for a notarized audit, we will need a short-form NDA and a locked S3 transfer — ping Evelyn to coordinate.

Marcus Lin
Staff Software Engineer, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference
attachments: [SIG-evidence-pack_v2.zip, kms-summary-onepage.xlsx]

source.subject: Discovery recap + exec panel readiness and stakeholder heatcheck
source.content:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Jane Liu <jane.liu@retailco.com>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@retailco.com>, Anika Rao <anika.rao@retailco.com>
Date: Sun, 20 Dec 2026 09:15:00 -0800
Subject: Discovery recap + exec panel readiness and stakeholder heatcheck

Jane — thanks again for the call earlier. Quick recap and concrete next steps to get the steering/exec panel scheduled and make sure we have the right artifacts for procurement and security to move forward.

Summary of where we landed on the call:
- Goal: get steering committee / exec sponsor comfortable to approve a pilot and sign a small committed capacity order for Q1.
- Economic buyer / sponsor: Jane Liu (CFO) — confirmed.
- Procurement owner: Sam Patel (procurement@retailco.com).
- Security owner: Anika Rao (Head of Security).
- Key decision criteria: cost per token/throughput, data residency & auditability, and measurable latency SLOs for production inference.

Attachments I’m sending: Redwood-Product-Brief.pdf (short), Proposed-SOW-Redwood-Initial.docx (baseline SOW), SOC2-report-2025.pdf. I’ve also attached a suggested 20-minute exec deck (sectioned inside the brief).

Asks / proposed next steps:
1) Steering panel dates: you mentioned Jan 9–12 windows in the mornings PT. Can you confirm a target slot? We’re proposing Jan 10, 09:00–09:30 PT for a 20-minute briefing + 10 minutes Q&A.
2) Procurement checklist: Sam — can you share delivery/PO cadence and the contract owner who will handle redlines? If you have a standard MSA, please send it so our legal can pre-check.
3) Security artifacts: Anika — we’ll prepare a one-page security summary and deliver SOC2 and data flow diagrams. Is there a vendor questionnaire we should fill out? If you prefer, we can complete the questionnaire you send and return within 48 hours.
4) Economic buy-in: Jane — what are the top financial metrics you want to see in the pilot (e.g., cost per inference at target QPS, expected token volume)? We’ll include a short TCO slide.

Proposed internal owners from my side:
- Exec briefing + econ slides: Ben Carter (me)
- Security pre-read and questionnaire: Priya Natarajan (CS)
- Technical SLOs and deployment runbook: Karthik Iyer (Solutions Eng)

If that all looks right I’ll: (a) lock the Jan 10 slot pending your confirmation, (b) turn the SOW into a redline-friendly doc and send to Sam, and (c) get Priya/Karthik to prepare the short pre-reads.

Thanks — appreciate the quick turnaround here, we’ll keep the steering deck tight and focused on the three decision levers you called out.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
+1 415-555-0142

-- Attachments: Redwood-Product-Brief.pdf (9 pages), Proposed-SOW-Redwood-Initial.docx, SOC2-report-2025.pdf --

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Jane Liu <jane.liu@retailco.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@retailco.com>, Anika Rao <anika.rao@retailco.com>
Date: Sun, 20 Dec 2026 10:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Discovery recap + exec panel readiness and stakeholder heatcheck

Ben — thanks for the summary. A few clarifications from our side: we can tentatively hold Jan 10 at 09:00 PT but need a formal calendar invite. Procurement (Sam) will need the SOW and MSAs in advance; Sam, please advise on the PO lead time (we typically need 3–4 weeks for a standard vendor on-boarding).

Security: Anika — we need your SOC2, a short data flow (we push everything through a DMZ and require encryption in transit and at rest), and answers to a short vendor questionnaire. I’ve attached the questionnaire (RetailCo-vendor-questionnaire.xlsx) — would like a completed version back by Dec 23 so security can triage before the panel.

Decision criteria summary for the steering committee:
- Unit economics at projected QPS (base case and stress case)
- Data handling & compliance (SOC2 attestation, access controls, retention windows)
- Operational readiness (runbook, rollback plan, SLA escalation path)

If we get those artifacts by Dec 23 we’ll circulate pre-reads to the steering group.

Jane
Jane Liu
CFO, RetailCo
jane.liu@retailco.com

-- Attachment: RetailCo-vendor-questionnaire.xlsx --

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jane Liu <jane.liu@retailco.com>, Sam Patel <sam.patel@retailco.com>, Anika Rao <anika.rao@retailco.com>
Date: Mon, 21 Dec 2026 08:30:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Discovery recap + exec panel readiness and stakeholder heatcheck — action items for prep

Priya/Karthik — forwarding the thread; please see Jane’s asks (questionnaire due Dec 23, steering panel target Jan 10). Can you each confirm ownership and what you need from me?

Immediate asks:
- Priya: please prepare the one-page security summary + complete the attached questionnaire. Aim: first draft by Dec 22 close of business so I can review and send to Jane.
- Karthik: can you draft a short SLO & deployment slide that covers latency targets (p50/p95), expected burst handling, and a short fallback plan? Also include a short infra diagram showing VPC options and data flow.
- Ben: I will handle the econ slide and lock the calendar after you confirm Jan 10.

Let me know if either of you think we need legal involved before Sam sees the SOW.

Thanks,
Ben

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jane Liu <jane.liu@retailco.com>, Sam Patel <sam.patel@retailco.com>, Anika Rao <anika.rao@retailco.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Dec 2026 11:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Discovery recap + exec panel readiness and stakeholder heatcheck — action items for prep

Ben — I’ll take the questionnaire. Plan:
- Dec 22 EOD: first pass of the vendor questionnaire + one-page security summary (SOC2 highlights, encryption at rest/in-transit, data retention default 30 days for KV cache, audit logging note).
- Dec 23 AM: finalize and send to Jane.

Agenda for the 30-minute steering briefing (proposed):
1) 5 min — Objective & success criteria (Ben)
2) 10 min — Solution overview & infra (Karthik)
3) 10 min — Risk/compliance & pilot safeguards (Priya)
4) 5 min — Commercial ask & next steps (Ben)

I’ll prepare the security slide and attach the SOC2 snapshot and data flow diagram. If Legal input is needed on the SOW, please loop in Legal now; otherwise I’ll wait for Sam’s PO timeline.

Priya
Principal Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.ai

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jane Liu <jane.liu@retailco.com>, Sam Patel <sam.patel@retailco.com>, Anika Rao <anika.rao@retailco.com>
Date: Wed, 23 Dec 2026 14:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Discovery recap + exec panel readiness and stakeholder heatcheck — action items for prep

Ben / Priya — noted. Quick technical notes I’ll include in the slide deck:
- Suggested baseline SLOs: p50 < 60ms, p95 < 200ms for 3-token median responses at expected pilot QPS. We’ll present a stress-case latency table and expected tail latencies at 2x projected QPS.
- KV/prefix caching: we recommend enabling prefix caching for predictable prompts to reduce cost 40–60% for high-repeat queries.
- Deployment options: Hosted API (fastest start), Dedicated pool (predictable latency), Private VPC (for regulated data). I’ll include a simple decision matrix against RetailCo criteria.

I’ll upload the draft slides to the Drive prep folder by EOD today.

Karthik
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.ai

-- End of thread --

source.subject: EchoVerse renewal: payment lane change, PO and net-term update
messages:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@echoverse.com>, Finance AP <ap@echoverse.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: EchoVerse renewal: payment lane change, PO and net-term update

Hi Liam / EchoVerse finance team,

Thanks again for the time yesterday — excited to get EchoVerse onto the Dedicated plan and the additional capacity you confirmed for Q1. Before we finalize paperwork, a couple of quick asks from our billing side so we don't hit manual delays at renewal:

- Please share the PO number you'd like us to reference on invoices for the 12/31 renewal (this will ensure invoices route to the correct ledger).
- Finance requested your preferred net terms (we quoted Net30 previously; I heard you may need Net60 for this entity). Can you confirm?
- If the payor entity changed for the renewal, please attach the requested tax form (W-9 or W-8BEN-E) for the invoice recipient.

I've cc'd Priya (billing ops) to help map the PO to the account and Marcus for the contract amendment. If it helps, I can produce a redlined order form with the Net60 term and proposed PO pre-filled for signature.

Thanks — aiming to have billing and DocuSign wrapped this week so the capacity add shows on the first Jan invoice.

Best,
Jordan Blake
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com]
Attachment: draft-orderform-EV-427.pdf (staged)
From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@echoverse.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Finance AP <ap@echoverse.com>, Sarah Mills <sarah.mills@echoverse.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Dec 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: EchoVerse renewal: payment lane change, PO and net-term update

Jordan — thanks for the recap. Confirming the details from our side: 

- Please reference PO #EV-PO-9813 on all invoices for the renewal and capacity top-up.
- We need Net60 terms on the contract amendment (our treasury team confirms Net60 for renewals > $100k).
- Payor entity is the same (EchoVerse, Inc.), I've attached a completed W-9 for EchoVerse for your records.

Can you send the redlined order form with Net60 and the PO filled in? Once we review I'll loop legal to sign. Also cc'ing Sarah from procurement to track the DocuSign routing.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

— Liam
Liam O'Connor
Head of Platform Partnerships | EchoVerse
Attachment: EchoVerse_W9_2026.pdf
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Dec 2026 08:22:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: EchoVerse renewal: payment lane change, PO and net-term update

Priya / Marcus — forwarding Liam's confirmation. Actions: 

1) Priya: can you map PO EV-PO-9813 to EchoVerse's billing profile and update the account to Net60 for the upcoming renewal? We need the invoice template to show Net60 and the PO on the remittance. Also confirm whether Net60 impacts our expected cash timing for the Jan invoice (flag to Rev Ops if so).

2) Marcus: ready for a short amendment that switches terms to Net60 and references the PO + capacity add? If you can draft a one-page amendment we can send for signature (DocuSign). I'd like the amendment to explicitly note the start date for the increased capacity (2027-01-01) and the invoicing cadence.

Liam attached the W-9 — I've downloaded and attached in case you need to upload to billing.

If you want me to pre-populate the DocuSign envelope, I can do that and include Sarah Mills as the procurement signee.

Thanks — Jordan

[Quoted message]

> Jordan — thanks for the recap. Confirming the details from our side: 
> 
> - Please reference PO #EV-PO-9813 on all invoices for the renewal and capacity top-up.
> - We need Net60 terms on the contract amendment (our treasury team confirms Net60 for renewals > $100k).
> - Payor entity is the same (EchoVerse, Inc.), I've attached a completed W-9 for EchoVerse for your records.
> 
> Can you send the redlined order form with Net60 and the PO filled in? Once we review I'll loop legal to sign. Also cc'ing Sarah from procurement to track the DocuSign routing.
> 
> — Liam
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@echoverse.com>, Finance AP <ap@echoverse.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Sarah Mills <sarah.mills@echoverse.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Dec 2026 16:45:00 -0800
Subject: EchoVerse renewal — PO loaded, amendment sent for signature (EV-PO-9813)

Hi Liam / Sarah — quick update and confirmation: 

- I uploaded PO EV-PO-9813 into our billing system and set EchoVerse's invoice terms to Net60 for the renewal cycle.
- Marcus drafted a short amendment (1 page) to change billing terms to Net60 and add the Q1 capacity increase; I created a DocuSign envelope and added the EchoVerse procurement signee (Sarah) and legal for countersign. Link below.
- Attached: signed PO (EchoVerse_PO_EV-427_signed.pdf) and the W-9 you provided. Once signature completes the first invoice (covering the renewal plus prorated capacity) will follow the Net60 term.

DocuSign link: https://www.docusign.com/ev-427-signature-link
HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234560/deal/EV-2026-427

If you need us to split invoicing (renewal + capacity as separate line items) let me know before we finalize the invoice run next Friday. Otherwise we'll invoice combined and apply the PO to the single invoice.

Thanks — Jordan
Jordan Blake
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Phone: (415) 555-0198
Attachment: EchoVerse_PO_EV-427_signed.pdf, EchoVerse_W9_2026.pdf

source.subject: Clearwater Health — billing path options & formal quote / rate card request
messages:
From: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@clearwaterhealth.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Cc: ProcureOps <procureops@clearwaterhealth.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Billing cadence & rate card — upcoming procurement review

Hi Hannah,

Hope you’re well. We’re preparing for Clearwater’s procurement review next week (decide vendor terms by 2026-12-20). Can you please: 1) share a current hosted API rate card and 2) clarify billing cadence options — specifically whether you support monthly invoicing (Net30/Net45) vs. primary-card billing?

A few follow-ups from our side:
- Do you accept POs and what does your vendor setup require?
- Are there standard discounts for 12-month committed spend vs. month-to-month usage?
- Can you provide a sample invoice (tax line, billing address, remittance instructions) so our finance team can confirm the PO flow?

I’ve attached our vendor setup form for your finance ops to complete. If possible, can we get the rate card + sample invoice by end of day Thursday (2026-12-17)?

Thanks — I’m copying Tom (CFO) and ProcureOps for visibility.

Best,
Alicia Gomez
Head of Procurement
Clearwater Health
attachments: vendor_setup_form.pdf

From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
To: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@clearwaterhealth.com>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Dec 2026 11:04:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Billing cadence & rate card — upcoming procurement review

Hi Alicia — thanks for the note and the vendor form.

Quick summary of options we commonly offer for hosted API customers:
- Card (default): charge on primary card daily/weekly depending on usage settings. Fastest onboarding, no PO required.
- Monthly invoice (standard): invoice generated monthly in arrears; Payment terms: Net30 by default for accounts with approved credit. We can agree Net45 on a case-by-case basis during contracting.
- Net & discounting: we support committed spend discounts (tiered) and one-time onboarding credits for multi‑year commitments.

I’ve attached our billing options doc and the current rate card extract for Hosted API so your team can review specifics (line items for tokens, embeddings, and dedicated capacity). Also attaching a sample invoice that shows tax and remittance fields.

Next steps I propose:
1) We complete your vendor form (Markus / Priya can help).
2) If you want invoicing, confirm billing entity and PO routing; we’ll send a formal quote for signatures and the payment terms section.
3) If Clearwater prefers a committed discount, tell us target monthly spend and term (12/24/36 months) and we’ll return a proposal.

Available for a quick 20-min call tomorrow afternoon if that helps.

Best,
Hannah Schmitt
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference
attachments: Redwood_Hosted_API_Billing_Options.pdf, sample_invoice_SOX.pdf

From: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@clearwaterhealth.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Brennan <tbrennan@clearwaterhealth.com>, ProcureOps <procureops@clearwaterhealth.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Dec 2026 08:23:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Billing cadence & rate card — upcoming procurement review

Thanks Hannah — that helps. A few clarifications from our procurement/finance side:

- We prefer monthly invoicing with Net30 payment terms. Primary-card billing is acceptable for proof-of-concept but procurement requires invoice/PO for production.
- For committed discounts: can you share explicit tier thresholds (e.g., >= $25k/mo => X% off, >= $75k/mo => Y% off) and whether discounts apply to both generation tokens and embeddings?
- Do you offer a small early-payment discount (1-3%) if we can pay Net15?

We completed and re-attached the vendor setup form (ProcureOps typically needs to keep an audit trail).

Would appreciate a short written quote showing: billing cadence Monthly / Net30, accepted payment methods, remittance instructions, and the committed-discount table (if available) — that will let us route to legal/finance.

Best,
Alicia
attachments: vendor_setup_form_filled.pdf

From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
To: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@clearwaterhealth.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 17 Dec 2026 10:45:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Billing cadence & rate card — upcoming procurement review

Hi Alicia — Markus from Sales Ops / Pricing here. Appreciate the details.
