source.subject: Geo-anchoring & Scheduled Purge: confirmation needed for SummitTech Dedicated
messages:
From: Aisha Rana <aisha.rana@summittech.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Dan Cho <dan.cho@summittech.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0500
Subject: Geo-anchoring and purge timeline for our Dedicated instance

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our security & infra team has one final set of questions before we sign the SOW around data residency and deletion for the Dedicated cluster you proposed. In particular: 

- Can you confirm which physical country the SummitTech Dedicated cluster will be pinned to (both primary host and backups)?
- What is Redwood's standard timeline and steps for a tenant-scoped purge (requested by customer)? Please include snapshot, backup, and object-store flows.
- Do you provide a post-purge attestation (signed) and sample logs that show the erase events?

We need this information to close our internal review and provide an acceptance note to procurement. If you can send a short runbook and any artifacts (attestation template/sample logs), we can sign off.

Appreciate a response by EOD tomorrow US Eastern if possible.

Best,
Aisha Rana
Security Program Manager
SummitTech

---
Attachment: none
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Rana <aisha.rana@summittech.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Dan Cho <dan.cho@summittech.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Dec 2026 11:30:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Geo-anchoring and purge timeline for our Dedicated instance

Hi Aisha — thanks for the clear list. Quick answers up front, then I’ll attach the runbook artifact for your review.

1) Country pinning: we will provision your Dedicated pool in the US-East (Virginia) region and anchor tenant data to that region. Backups/snapshots will also be stored in the same legal jurisdiction; cross-region replication is not enabled for this Dedicated contract unless you opt in.

2) Purge timeline & steps (customer-initiated tenant purge):
   - Day 0 (request received): We acknowledge and open a purge ticket, schedule maintenance window if needed.
   - Hours 0–2: Quiesce incoming writes to the tenant and take a final snapshot flagged for retention window zero.
   - Hours 2–6: Execute KV cache flush and remove live tenant objects; initiate snapshot deletion workflow (primary storage) and mark tenant metadata for wipe.
   - Hours 6–24: Verify object-store deletions and start metadata tombstone sweep.
   - Day 2–7: Run forensic verification against artifact indexes and purge any remaining ephemeral caches and worker local state.
   - Up to Day 30: We maintain logical retention for audit purposes only; we can shorten this window by mutual agreement.

3) Attestation & logs: we provide a signed attestation PDF (template attached) and an extract of erase-event logs showing erase-job IDs, timestamps, and operator confirmation. Sample log extract is attached as JSON.

I’ve attached a short "Country Commitment and Purge Runbook" (jurisdiction-commitment.pdf) and a sample erase log (redwood-erase-log-sample.json). Priya is on copy to add technical detail for snapshot/backups and the playbook diagram.

If those artifacts look good, we can produce a formal signed attestation and a link to the deletion evidence at the run of the purge.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer, Customer Solutions
Redwood Inference

Attachments:
- jurisdiction-commitment.pdf (PDF, 2 pages)
- redwood-erase-log-sample.json (JSON, 1.2KB)
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Rana <aisha.rana@summittech.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Dan Cho <dan.cho@summittech.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Dec 2026 10:05:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Geo-anchoring and purge timeline for our Dedicated instance

Hi Aisha — quick technical clarifications to Ben's plan so expectations are aligned. I’ll keep this short and operational.

- Snapshots: we take point-in-time snapshots at the storage layer. For Dedicated customers who request zero-retention purge, we mark the snapshot as "expire-immediate" and trigger storage-provider delete API calls. Note: object-store providers may have internal deletion windows (finalized deletes can vary by provider, we surface the delete timestamp and job id).

- Backups & replicas: by default there is no cross-region replication. If SummitTech later requests an asynchronous geo-replica, that would create additional retention vectors and need explicit removal steps.

- Forensic verification: our purge job produces a 3-part artifact: 1) erase-event log (job id, worker nodes, timestamps), 2) snapshot delete receipts from object-store, 3) a short verification checklist run by ops engineers (hash checks for tombstone markers). We bundle those into the attestation PDF.

- Sample timeline nuance: in practice most tenant purges complete within 24 hours (live objects + metadata), but final sweep/forensic wrap-up can extend to 7 days for complex workloads. If you require a hard SLA (e.g., all artifacts produced within 72 hours), we can add that to the contract as an SLA exception.

Attached is the purge workflow diagram that maps actions to storage and worker components. Also looping in Karthik for any legal/backup replication notes that should be surfaced in the attestation template.

Thanks — Priya
Priya Natarajan
Product Security Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments:
- purge-workflow-diagram.png (PNG, 860KB)
From: Aisha Rana <aisha.rana@summittech.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Dan Cho <dan.cho@summittech.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Dec 2026 14:08:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Geo-anchoring and purge timeline for our Dedicated instance

Ben/Priya — this is perfect and addresses our checklist. SummitTech accepts the proposed country anchoring to US-East and the purge workflow as described. A few final asks: 

1) Please produce the signed attestation PDF (use the attached template) once the SOW is countersigned. We need the attestation to be signed by an authorized Redwood security representative.
2) Please add an SLA clause that the final forensic artifact bundle (erase-event log + object-store receipts + verification checklist) will be delivered within 72 hours of purge request. We will take that as a contractual acceptance criterion.
3) Share a Drive folder link for evidence delivery and the initial sample artifacts you attached earlier. Dan will coordinate procurement and include the attestation requirement in the SOW.

If you can confirm (1) and (2) by EOD tomorrow, we’ll move the SOW to countersignature. Thanks for the quick turn here.

Best,
Aisha Rana
Security Program Manager
SummitTech

---
Included artifacts referenced: jurisdiction-commitment.pdf, redwood-erase-log-sample.json, purge-workflow-diagram.png

2) Please add an SLA clause that the final forensic artifact bundle (erase-event log + object-store receipts + verification checklist) will be delivered within 72 hours of purge request. We will take that as a contractual acceptance criterion.
3) Share a Drive folder link for evidence delivery and the initial sample artifacts you attached earlier. Dan will coordinate procurement and include the attestation requirement in the SOW.

If you can confirm (1) and (2) by EOD tomorrow, we’ll move the SOW to countersignature. Thanks for the quick turn here.

Best,
Aisha Rana
Security Program Manager
SummitTech

---
Included artifacts referenced: jurisdiction-commitment.pdf, redwood-erase-log-sample.json, purge-workflow-diagram.png
text:
From: Aisha Rana <aisha.rana@summittech.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Dan Cho <dan.cho@summittech.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0500
Subject: Geo-anchoring and purge timeline for our Dedicated instance

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our security & infra team has one final set of questions before we sign the SOW around data residency and deletion for the Dedicated cluster you proposed. In particular: 

- Can you confirm which physical country the SummitTech Dedicated cluster will be pinned to (both primary host and backups)?
- What is Redwood's standard timeline and steps for a tenant-scoped purge (requested by customer)? Please include snapshot, backup, and object-store flows.
- Do you provide a post-purge attestation (signed) and sample logs that show the erase events?

We need this information to close our internal review and provide an acceptance note to procurement. If you can send a short runbook and any artifacts (attestation template/sample logs), we can sign off.

Appreciate a response by EOD tomorrow US Eastern if possible.

Best,
Aisha Rana
Security Program Manager
SummitTech

---
Attachment: none


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Rana <aisha.rana@summittech.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Dan Cho <dan.cho@summittech.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Dec 2026 11:30:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Geo-anchoring and purge timeline for our Dedicated instance

Hi Aisha — thanks for the clear list. Quick answers up front, then I’ll attach the runbook artifact for your review.

1) Country pinning: we will provision your Dedicated pool in the US-East (Virginia) region and anchor tenant data to that region. Backups/snapshots will also be stored in the same legal jurisdiction; cross-region replication is not enabled for this Dedicated contract unless you opt in.

2) Purge timeline & steps (customer-initiated tenant purge):
   - Day 0 (request received): We acknowledge and open a purge ticket, schedule maintenance window if needed.
   - Hours 0–2: Quiesce incoming writes to the tenant and take a final snapshot flagged for retention window zero.
   - Hours 2–6: Execute KV cache flush and remove live tenant objects; initiate snapshot deletion workflow (primary storage) and mark tenant metadata for wipe.
   - Hours 6–24: Verify object-store deletions and start metadata tombstone sweep.
   - Day 2–7: Run forensic verification against artifact indexes and purge any remaining ephemeral caches and worker local state.
   - Up to Day 30: We maintain logical retention for audit purposes only; we can shorten this window by mutual agreement.

3) Attestation & logs: we provide a signed attestation PDF (template attached) and an extract of erase-event logs showing erase-job IDs, timestamps, and operator confirmation. Sample log extract is attached as JSON.

I’ve attached a short "Country Commitment and Purge Runbook" (jurisdiction-commitment.pdf) and a sample erase log (redwood-erase-log-sample.json). Priya is on copy to add technical detail for snapshot/backups and the playbook diagram.

If those artifacts look good, we can produce a formal signed attestation and a link to the deletion evidence at the run of the purge.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer, Customer Solutions
Redwood Inference

Attachments:
- jurisdiction-commitment.pdf (PDF, 2 pages)
- redwood-erase-log-sample.json (JSON, 1.2KB)


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Rana <aisha.rana@summittech.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Dan Cho <dan.cho@summittech.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Dec 2026 10:05:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Geo-anchoring and purge timeline for our Dedicated instance

Hi Aisha — quick technical clarifications to Ben's plan so expectations are aligned. I’ll keep this short and operational.

- Snapshots: we take point-in-time snapshots at the storage layer. For Dedicated customers who request zero-retention purge, we mark the snapshot as "expire-immediate" and trigger storage-provider delete API calls. Note: object-store providers may have internal deletion windows (finalized deletes can vary by provider, we surface the delete timestamp and job id).

- Backups & replicas: by default there is no cross-region replication. If SummitTech later requests an asynchronous geo-replica, that would create additional retention vectors and need explicit removal steps.

- Forensic verification: our purge job produces a 3-part artifact: 1) erase-event log (job id, worker nodes, timestamps), 2) snapshot delete receipts from object-store, 3) a short verification checklist run by ops engineers (hash checks for tombstone markers). We bundle those into the attestation PDF.

- Sample timeline nuance: in practice most tenant purges complete within 24 hours (live objects + metadata), but final sweep/forensic wrap-up can extend to 7 days for complex workloads. If you require a hard SLA (e.g., all artifacts produced within 72 hours), we can add that to the contract as an SLA exception.

Attached is the purge workflow diagram that maps actions to storage and worker components. Also looping in Karthik for any legal/backup replication notes that should be surfaced in the attestation template.

Thanks — Priya
Priya Natarajan
Product Security Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments:
- purge-workflow-diagram.png (PNG, 860KB)


From: Aisha Rana <aisha.rana@summittech.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Dan Cho <dan.cho@summittech.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Dec 2026 14:08:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Geo-anchoring and purge timeline for our Dedicated instance

Ben/Priya — this is perfect and addresses our checklist. SummitTech accepts the proposed country anchoring to US-East and the purge workflow as described. A few final asks: 

1) Please produce the signed attestation PDF (use the attached template) once the SOW is countersigned. We need the attestation to be signed by an authorized Redwood security representative.
2) Please add an SLA clause that the final forensic artifact bundle (erase-event log + object-store receipts + verification checklist) will be delivered within 72 hours of purge request. We will take that as a contractual acceptance criterion.
3) Share a Drive folder link for evidence delivery and the initial sample artifacts you attached earlier. Dan will coordinate procurement and include the attestation requirement in the SOW.

If you can confirm (1) and (2) by EOD tomorrow, we’ll move the SOW to countersignature. Thanks for the quick turn here.

Best,
Aisha Rana
Security Program Manager
SummitTech

source.subject: Kickoff: Renewal & Expansion MAP — Crestview Analytics
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Lucy Williams <lucy@crestview.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Kickoff: Renewal & Expansion MAP — Crestview Analytics

Hi Lucy —

Thanks again for the conversation last week. As discussed, wed like to start a short Mutual Action Plan (MAP) to align on the renewal window, the evaluation items for the additional inference capacity, and an expanded commercial proposal that covers a new model tier for your forecasting pipelines.

Objective for this kickoff:
- Confirm the decision-makers and procurement timeline
- Share usage baseline and target uplift for Q1
- Agree on evaluation criteria (latency, cost per 10k tokens, inference throughput)
- Set close date and next milestones (contract/PO, security review)

Proposed close timeline:
- MAP draft & roles: this week (Dec 14–17)
- Evaluation & short POC: Dec 20–Jan 14
- Contract & procurement sign-off: target Jan 21

Attachments: Im attaching a short MAP draft and the usage export from November so we have a baseline. Please confirm the right stakeholders to loop in from your side (legal/procurement/engineering).

Immediate asks:
1) Please confirm the primary approver for budget and the procurement contact.
2) If the dates above look tight, suggest a preferred close date.
3) Any compliance paperwork we should surface now (SOC2 evidence, IP addendum)?

Ill follow up with a calendar invite that captures milestones and owners. Thanks — looking forward to partnering on this expansion.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0142

Attachments: crestview-map-draft-20261214.pdf; crestview-usage-report-nov-2026.csv

From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
Cc: Lucy Williams <lucy@crestview.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Dec 2026 10:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Kickoff: Renewal & Expansion MAP — Crestview Analytics

On Tue, Dec 14, 2026 at 09:12 AM Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Lucy —
> 
> Thanks again for the conversation last week. As discussed, wed like to start a short Mutual Action Plan (MAP) to align on the renewal window, the evaluation items for the additional inference capacity, and an expanded commercial proposal that covers a new model tier for your forecasting pipelines.

Thanks Ben — quick operational note from my side. Ill own the evaluation coordination and will book the weekly syncs. Proposed owners:
- Redwood evaluation lead: Priya Natarajan (me) — coordinates POC, runs throughput tests
- Redwood commercial lead: Ben Carter — pricing/contract discussions
- Customer success lead: Monica Patel — adoption & usage uplift plan
- Solution architect (technical): Rishi Malhotra — integration/latency tuning

Ive attached a stakeholder roster template and a one-page evaluation checklist that mirrors the MAP. I will send calendar invitations for:
- MAP kickoff (30m) — Thu Dec 16 10:00 AM PT
- Weekly evaluation syncs (30m) — Tuesdays 9:00 AM PT, starting Dec 21

Action items for Crestview before the kickoff:
- Share procurement contact and any PO windows
- Provide sample payloads for throughput testing (anonymized)
- Flag any regulatory constraints for data handling

See attached: stakeholder-roster-crestview.xlsx

Thanks — Priya
Priya Natarajan
Evaluation Lead | Redwood Inference
priya@redwood.com

Attachments: stakeholder-roster-crestview.xlsx

From: Lucy Williams <lucy@crestview.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Dec 2026 11:27:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Kickoff: Renewal & Expansion MAP — Crestview Analytics

Thanks Ben and Priya — appreciate the clear plan.

Confirming two things from Crestview:
1) Procurement lead is Mark Green (procurement@crestview.ai) — Ive CCd him on a separate note (hell join the kickoff if available).
2) Were supportive of the MAP timeline but our internal budget committee meets the week of Jan 18 which makes the Jan 21 target tight. Would it be acceptable to target Jan 28 for contract execution? That gives us one full committee window and a little buffer for legal redlines.

We will upload sample payloads to the secure S3 link Priya requested by end of day Wed Dec 15. No SOC2 addendum required beyond the standard evidence you provide; Ill flag anything else after the kickoff.

Looking forward to the kickoff on Thu.

Best,
Lucy Williams
Head of Data Platforms | Crestview Analytics
lucy@crestview.ai | +1 617-555-0199

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Lucy Williams <lucy@crestview.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>, Mark Green <procurement@crestview.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Dec 2026 15:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Kickoff: Renewal & Expansion MAP — Crestview Analytics

Lucy — thanks for the quick turnaround and the procurement contact. Jan 28 is fine; well reflect that in the MAP milestones.

A couple of follow-ups on the expansion side so were aligned going into evaluation:
- Upsell suggestion: introduce a dedicated inference pool (Reserved XPU) plus a burst policy for end-of-month reporting spikes — well quantify expected cost delta in the proposal.
- Evaluation metrics: well surface a short table in the kickoff that maps each metric (latency p50/p95, tokens/sec, cost per 10k tokens) to pass/fail thresholds for a successful uplift.

Ill update the MAP doc and send the calendar invites Priya mentioned. If Mark or Lucy want a quick pre-kickoff with procurement/legal, we can schedule 15 minutes tomorrow.

Thanks — Ben
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com

Attachments referenced: crestview-map-draft-20261214.pdf; crestview-usage-report-nov-2026.csv

source.subject: Demo window locked pending vendor portal clearance & exec travel
source.content:
From: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Myers <laura@aurorasys.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 8 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Demo timing -- blocked by your portal & exec travel

Hi Laura,

Thanks for confirming interest — excited to show Aurora Systems how Redwood could cut inference cost for your search augmentation pipeline. I pulled a demo window on Dec 15 for the team, but procurement flagged that your vendor portal onboarding (and the NDA/procurement questionnaire) needs to complete before we can run the production-facing walkthrough.

Could you confirm status of the vendor portal invite? Marcus noted the portal requires an SSO assertion and a signed DPA to provision test credentials. If it makes sense, we can do a narrow internal tech brief (no live data) on Dec 15 and then run the full walkthrough once procurement clears the portal.

Thanks,
Irene

--
Irene Choi
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

-------------------------
From: Laura Myers <laura@aurorasys.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@aurorasys.com
Date: Wed, 9 Dec 2026 10:01:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Demo timing -- blocked by your portal & exec travel

Hi Irene,

Appreciate the heads up. We did receive the vendor portal email but our security team is asking for a couple more artifacts (SOC 2 cert PDF, and the vendor onboarding checklist completed) before they flip the portal switch. I'll attach the checklist and loop in procurement separately.

Separately, our VP of Product — who was supposed to join the Dec 15 demo — is traveling and out through Dec 18. We can do the internal tech brief on the 15th (no problem) but the executive stakeholders prefer to see the full demo with production examples after the 18th.

I'll push the SOC 2 and checklist today; can you send over a short stub of the demo agenda so we can decide whether an internal brief covers our needs on the 15th?

Best,
Laura

Attachment: VendorOnboardingChecklist.xlsx (stub)

-------------------------
From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
To: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Myers <laura@aurorasys.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 08:44:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Demo timing -- blocked by your portal & exec travel

On Wed, Dec 9, 2026 at 10:01 AM Laura Myers <laura@aurorasys.com> wrote:
> Our VP of Product — who was supposed to join the Dec 15 demo — is traveling and out through Dec 18.

Thanks for the update. Irene — suggestion: run a scoped technical pre-brief on Dec 15 that focuses on architecture, model selection, and integration hooks (no customer data). Then book the executive-facing demo for Dec 21 or 22 so Aurora's VP and procurement are present and the portal has time to be cleared.

Action items for Redwood:
- Send a 25-minute tech-brief agenda by EOD Dec 10.
- Provide SOC 2 pointer and short answers for the checklist items Laura's team asked about (we have a SOC 2 link and a standard checklist response doc).
- Prep a fall-back plan: we can demo a redacted dataset if the portal isn't fully provisioned, but clear that governance won't be validated until portal access is live.

Marcus
Senior Solutions Architect

source.subject: Customer DPA redline: telemetry & subprocessor clarifications needed
messages:
From: Claire Dawson <claire@lumenai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Markus Hall <m.hall@lumenai.com>
Date: 2026-12-06T09:18:00-08:00
Subject: DPA redline — telemetry, subprocessors, training carveouts

Hi Ben,

Thanks for sending the DPA draft. Markus and I scanned the changes; we attached our redline (LumenAI_DPA_Redline_v3.pdf). A couple of points we need clarified before legal proceeds:

- Telemetry: your team’s proposed definitions use "operational telemetry" vs "analytic telemetry." We need to understand what fields are included under each bucket and the retention windows. Our security policy prohibits retention of customer prompt/response pairs beyond 30 days without explicit waiver.
- Subprocessors: the subprocessor_matrix.csv only lists generic roles. Can you confirm named vendors for telemetry/metrics, usage analytics, and model hosting? Do you require any customer data for model improvement or training?
- Training/data use carveout: the redline currently has a broad clause allowing "aggregate" model improvement. We need specifics on whether prompt/response content could be used to fine-tune models and whether LumenAI can opt-out per workspace.

We're trying to keep this moving — can you escalate internally to Security/Product/Legal so we can get a definitive yes/no on: (1) telemetry retention ceiling, (2) explicit non-training guarantee or opt-out flow, and (3) named subprocessors for telemetry/analytics?

Thanks,
Claire

--
Claire Dawson
Head of Contracts, LumenAI
claire@lumenai.com
+1 415-555-1212
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Claire Dawson <claire@lumenai.com>
Date: 2026-12-06T10:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: DPA redline — telemetry, subprocessors, training carveouts

Claire — thanks for the redline and the clear asks. I've looped in our Legal (Olga), Privacy (Priya) and Product (Karthik) internally to get firm answers. Quick notes for you while we triage:

- Telemetry fields: by default we collect event-level metrics (latency, error rates), token-level counts, and anonymized usage histograms. We do not persist full prompt/response pairs by default in the hosted product; when customers enable debug logs we retain request/response content for troubleshooting.
- Retention: our standard hosted retention is 90 days for operational telemetry, 365 days for aggregated metrics. We understand this may be longer than LumenAI’s policy; we can propose a contract carveout to 30 days if required for the account but need Product/Security signoff on the operational impact.
- Training: Redwood does not train models on customer prompt/response data by default. We can include explicit contractual language that customer content will not be used for model training unless customer gives an opt-in.

I'll get back to you with approvals and suggested contract language by EOD. Attaching the files you sent into our thread for internal review.

Ben

--
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0199
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: 2026-12-06T11:04:00-08:00
Subject: Fwd: DPA redline — telemetry, subprocessors, training carveouts — need signoff

Team — forwarding LumenAI's redline and their questions (attached). Key asks summarized below:

1) Telemetry: they want ability to limit retention to 30 days for prompt/response content and detailed field-level definition of telemetry buckets. Our standard: 90d operational content, 365d aggregated metrics.
2) Subprocessors: they want named vendors for telemetry/analytics/model hosting. We currently list categories; they need named vendors for audit.
3) Training: they require explicit contractual non-training of customer content (opt-in model if we want to use data).

Impact/ask:
- Product: confirm whether we can support a 30-day retention option per customer on hosted Redwood and any runtime/observability features that would be impacted (debug logs, traceability). Also need to know if "no-training" guarantees are feasible without engineering changes.
- Privacy/Security: provide recommended contractual language for the non-training guarantee, and confirm whether any subprocessors need to be redacted or annotated (e.g., Observair for metrics, ModelHub for hosted model caching).
- Legal (Olga): if you can propose redlines we should push back with, ideal language for both opt-out and opt-in flows, and whether an exception for ephemeral debugging session storage is acceptable.

Attachments: LumenAI_DPA_Redline_v3.pdf, subprocessor_matrix.csv (from customer).

Target: I'd like to send a consolidated response + proposed contract text back to Claire by 3pm PST. Can you confirm availability to review/approve by 2pm?

Thanks — Ben
From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-06T13:06:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: DPA redline — telemetry, subprocessors, training carveouts — need signoff

Ben / all — quick internal response after syncing with Privacy/Security:

1) Telemetry retention
- Legal is comfortable with offering a contract option for a 30-day retention ceiling for request/response content on hosted accounts, provided Product confirms operational feasibility. Proposed clause: "Redwood will, upon customer request, configure hosted account telemetry retention for request and response content to a maximum of 30 days. Operational telemetry and aggregated metrics may be retained longer as anonymized aggregates."
- We should carve out explicit language for debug logs: "Debug logs containing full request/response content may be retained for up to 90 days for troubleshooting when enabled by the customer; such logs will be accessible only to authorized personnel under documented access controls."

2) Named subprocessors
- Security confirms the following named vendors may be disclosed: Observair (metrics), ModelCache Inc. (model caching), CloudRun GPUs LLC (managed hosting). We will provide the subprocessor CSV with names and contact points. Note: some vendor agreements contain confidentiality clauses — we must not include vendor contract rates or internal terms.

3) Training/data use
- Privacy recommends the following binding language: "Redwood will not use Customer Content to train, improve, or retrain models absent the Customer's explicit, written opt-in. Aggregated, de-identified metrics that do not contain Customer Content may be used for product analytics and performance tuning."
- Product note: need confirmation that feature flags exist to ensure no training pipeline consumes customer content by default.

Operational impact & decision path:
- Priya: please confirm whether the opt-out/opt-in control is implementable within the current privacy controls; if not, we should include a technical milestone in the contract to deliver the control within X days post-signature.
- Karthik: confirm whether a per-customer retention override to 30d is feasible and any perf implications for debugging.

If Product/Security agree, Legal can prepare a proposed DPA amendment and redline to send back to LumenAI.

Olga

--
Olga Petrov
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
olga.petrov@redwood.com
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-06T14:12:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: DPA redline — telemetry, subprocessors, training carveouts — need signoff

Quick confirmation from Privacy:
- Non-training guarantee: approved. We can contractually guarantee "no training" without immediate engineering changes by ensuring data never flows into model-training pipelines for hosted customers by default. We'll add a delivery milestone for a UI-based opt-in for customers who want to contribute data; timing TBD (recommend 60 days after signature).
- 30-day retention for request/response content: feasible as a configuration; however, debug logs currently bypass retention policies when enabled. Product should provide a plan to make debug logs respect the per-customer retention setting or require explicit customer-level override.

Karthik — need your confirmation on the debug logs and whether a 30-day per-customer retention will affect our ability to investigate incidents.

Priya

Subject: DPA redline — telemetry, subprocessors, training carveouts

Hi Ben,

Thanks for sending the DPA draft. Markus and I scanned the changes; we attached our redline (LumenAI_DPA_Redline_v3.pdf). A couple of points we need clarified before legal proceeds:

- Telemetry: your team’s proposed definitions use "operational telemetry" vs "analytic telemetry." We need to understand what fields are included under each bucket and the retention windows. Our security policy prohibits retention of customer prompt/response pairs beyond 30 days without explicit waiver.
- Subprocessors: the subprocessor_matrix.csv only lists generic roles. Can you confirm named vendors for telemetry/metrics, usage analytics, and model hosting? Do you require any customer data for model improvement or training?
- Training/data use carveout: the redline currently has a broad clause allowing "aggregate" model improvement. We need specifics on whether prompt/response content could be used to fine-tune models and whether LumenAI can opt-out per workspace.

We're trying to keep this moving — can you escalate internally to Security/Product/Legal so we can get a definitive yes/no on: (1) telemetry retention ceiling, (2) explicit non-training guarantee or opt-out flow, and (3) named subprocessors for telemetry/analytics?

Thanks,
Claire

--

claire@lumenai.com

Claire — thanks for the redline and the clear asks. I've looped in our Legal (Olga), Privacy (Priya) and Product (Karthik) internally to get firm answers. Quick notes for you while we triage:

- Telemetry fields: by default we collect event-level metrics (latency, error rates), token-level counts, and anonymized usage histograms. We do not persist full prompt/response pairs by default in the hosted product; when customers enable debug logs we retain request/response content for troubleshooting.
- Retention: our standard hosted retention is 90 days for operational telemetry, 365 days for aggregated metrics. We understand this may be longer than LumenAI’s policy; we can propose a contract carveout to 30 days if required for the account but need Product/Security signoff on the operational impact.
- Training: Redwood does not train models on customer prompt/response data by default. We can include explicit contractual language that customer content will not be used for model training unless customer gives an opt-in.

I'll get back to you with approvals and suggested contract language by EOD. Attaching the files you sent into our thread for internal review.

Ben

Olga

source.subject: SOC 2 renewal: inventory of evidence, log retention cost options and capacity considerations
messages:
From: Maya Singh <security@heliopay.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.example>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.p@heliopay.com>
Date: 2026-11-29T15:12:00Z
Subject: SOC 2 renewal — retention window & evidence delivery options

Ben —

As we move toward renewal (Dec 31), our audit team asked for two things we want to clarify before signing: a) confirmation of your log retention window (we currently see the contract default at 90 days) and whether that can be extended to 365 days for our Prod audit streams, and b) a simple mechanism to pull historic ingestion logs in bulk for the last 12 months (CSV or NDJSON preferred).

Also, want to understand if expanding retention to 365 will require an amendment or if it can be handled via the renewal terms (we may also increase committed capacity if pricing is reasonable). Can you send an estimated cost delta & timeline for evidence export?

Thanks —
Maya
Maya Singh
Head of Security Operations
HelioPay

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.example>
To: Maya Singh <security@heliopay.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.p@heliopay.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.example>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.example>
Date: 2026-11-30T09:04:00Z
Subject: Re: SOC 2 renewal — retention window & evidence delivery options

Maya —

Thanks for the clear ask. Short answer: we can support a 365-day retention for audit logs and provide a bulk export in NDJSON or gzipped CSV. A few quick notes:
- Retention change: requires a term amendment on the renewal so we can adjust retention and audit SLAs (legal needs to sign off).
- Export: ops can prepare a one-time bulk export (we’ll provide a file manifest and checksums). We typically ship via a signed S3 presigned URL or a secure Drive link.
- Cost: I’ll attach a first-pass delta (storage + egress + engineering export effort) and an option that includes a modest discount if you increase committed capacity by 20% for the next 12 months.

Looping in Priya (legal) and Karthik (platform) to confirm process and timing. Proposed next steps:
1) Karthik to confirm export cadence & ETA for a full 12-month export (we aim 5 business days after sign-off).
2) Priya to confirm amendment language and whether it can be bundled into the renewal or needs a stand-alone SOW.
3) I’ll send a consolidated quote and DocuSign link for the amendment + optional capacity uplift.

Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.example
+1 (415) 555-0182

Attachments: SOC2-evidence-index.csv (index of items we can export)
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.example>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.example>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.example>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.example>
Date: 2026-11-30T11:17:00Z
Subject: Re: SOC 2 renewal — retention window & evidence delivery options

Ben / team —

Quick ops read: we can produce a 12-month ingestion-log export in NDJSON gzipped chunks. Estimate: ~48 hours of work to stage and validate, then 1 business day to generate presigned URLs. Storage delta calculation (rough):
- HelioPay current monthly ingestion ~4 TB (compressed).
- Extra 9 months retention (moving from 90->365) ~ additional 18 TB stored on object storage (approx).
- Monthly storage delta ~ $1,250 (S3 style pricing); egress for one-time export ~ $450.
- If they commit +20% capacity (GPU/throughput), we can absorb export engineering time and apply a 15% discount on storage delta.

We’ll need: the HelioPay tenant id, the exact retention start date they expect, and a confirmation on export format (NDJSON recommended). I’ll attach a small log sample we’ll generate for QA.

title: SOC 2 renewal: inventory of evidence, log retention cost options and capacity considerations
preview:
From: Maya Singh <security@heliopay.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.example>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.p@heliopay.com>
Date: 2026-11-29T15:12:00Z
Subject: SOC 2 renewal — retention window & evidence delivery options

Ben —

As we move toward renewal (Dec 31), our audit team asked for two things we want to clarify before signing: a) confirmation of your log retention window (we currently see the contract default at 90 days) and whether that can be extended to 365 days for our Prod audit streams, and b) a simple mechanism to pull historic ingestion logs in bulk for the last 12 months (CSV or NDJSON preferred).

Also, want to understand if expanding retention to 365 will require an amendment or if it can be handled via the renewal terms (we may also increase committed capacity if pricing is reasonable). Can you send an estimated cost delta & timeline for evidence export?

Thanks —
Maya
Maya Singh
Head of Security Operations
HelioPay



From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.example>
To: Maya Singh <security@heliopay.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.p@heliopay.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.example>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.example>
Date: 2026-11-30T09:04:00Z
Subject: Re: SOC 2 renewal — retention window & evidence delivery options

Maya —

Thanks for the clear ask. Short answer: we can support a 365-day retention for audit logs and provide a bulk export in NDJSON or gzipped CSV. A few quick notes:
- Retention change: requires a term amendment on the renewal so we can adjust retention and audit SLAs (legal needs to sign off).
- Export: ops can prepare a one-time bulk export (we’ll provide a file manifest and checksums). We typically ship via a signed S3 presigned URL or a secure Drive link.
- Cost: I’ll attach a first-pass delta (storage + egress + engineering export effort) and an option that includes a modest discount if you increase committed capacity by 20% for the next 12 months.

Looping in Priya (legal) and Karthik (platform) to confirm process and timing. Proposed next steps:
1) Karthik to confirm export cadence & ETA for a full 12-month export (we aim 5 business days after sign-off).
2) Priya to confirm amendment language and whether it can be bundled into the renewal or needs a stand-alone SOW.
3) I’ll send a consolidated quote and DocuSign link for the amendment + optional capacity uplift.

Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.example
+1 (415) 555-0182

Attachments: SOC2-evidence-index.csv (index of items we can export)

We’ll need: the HelioPay tenant id, the exact retention start date they expect, and a confirmation on export format (NDJSON recommended). I’ll attach a small log sample we’ll generate for QA.

Karthik
Principal SRE, Redwood Inference

From: Clara Jensen <clara.jensen@cleardata-analytics.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Cc: ap@cleardata-analytics.com
Date: Thu, 3 Dec 2026 14:35:00 -0800
Subject: Redwood — Bank details and onboarding docs (ClearData Analytics)

Hi Grace,

Thanks for the warm welcome — excited to get Redwood set up as a vendor. Per our conversation earlier this week, please find our banking information and completed W-9 attached. We will initiate micro-deposits from the originating account ending in 4221 once you confirm the preferred remittance email.

Bank: First Federal Commercial
Routing (ABA): 121000358
Account name: ClearData Analytics Inc.
Account number: 4891237654
Account type: Checking
SWIFT: FFCBUS33 (for wires)

Remittance email for EDI/auto-remit: ap@cleardata-analytics.com
Payment terms: Net 30 (per PO)
Tax: W-9 attached

Attachments (stubs): ClearData_BANK_Details.pdf (banking details, voided check), ClearData_W9.pdf

Let us know if you need anything else to start the vendor setup. We can also send a DocuSign confirmation for the banking details if required.

Best,
Clara Jensen
Head of Finance Operations
ClearData Analytics
clara.jensen@cleardata-analytics.com
+1 (415) 555-3221

From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
To: Clara Jensen <clara.jensen@cleardata-analytics.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 3 Dec 2026 15:08:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Redwood — Bank details and onboarding docs (ClearData Analytics)

Thanks Clara — received both attachments. Quick confirmations before I route to finance: 

- Is ap@cleardata-analytics.com the address that will receive remittance advices (PDFs/EDI) and where we should send auto-remit notifications?
- We require either a signed ACH authorization or DocuSign confirmation for bank details before Marcus can schedule the vendor in our ERP. If you can send the DocuSign link that is ideal.
- We will validate micro-deposits; please expect two small deposits (under $1.00) within 1–2 business days from Redwood Inference (orig acct ending 8810).

Priya — copying you to ensure procurement has the W-9 on file and to confirm PO flow.

I'll forward all docs to Marcus for ledger setup once we have either the DocuSign confirmation or the micro-deposits verified.

Thanks,
Grace O'Connor
Vendor & Procurement Operations
Redwood Inference
grace.oconnor@redwood.ai
+1 (650) 555-0178

Subject: Fwd: Redwood — Bank details and onboarding docs (ClearData Analytics)

Marcus — forwarding ClearData's bank pack and W-9 (attached). Vendor has provided ap@cleardata-analytics.com as their remittance address and can do a DocuSign confirmation if needed.

Action items for finance: 
- Create vendor record: ClearData Analytics Inc. (TIN on W-9 attached).
- Hold final payment until micro-deposits are verified OR after we receive DocuSign bank confirmation.
- Assign vendor id and share the ERP vendor id back to procurement so POs can be issued (we prefer to have vendor id before issuing bigger POs).

Attachments forwarded: ClearData_BANK_Details.pdf, ClearData_W9.pdf

Thanks — appreciate a quick turnaround on this as we have two small POs pending.

—Grace

source.subject: Proposal: Renewal + Growth Accelerator for Synthica Health
messages:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
To: Emma Lane <emma.lane@synthicahealth.com>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@synthicahealth.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 09:15:00 -0800
Subject: Proposal: Renewal + Growth Accelerator for Synthica Health

Hi Emma / Tom,

Thanks again for the time last week — great discussion about how Synthica plans to ramp inference across clinical workflows next year. Attached is a first-pass proposal that balances predictable economics with runway for burst growth. High-level summary below; full numbers are in the attached quote (quote-v2.xlsx) and SLA appendix.

Summary (two-path approach)
- Option A — Co-term Annual (preferred): 12% uplift on current committed units, annual co-term, 25% surge buffer credits that can be consumed during spikes, quarterly true-up. Includes 1% latency SLA credit if latency > target for 2 consecutive weeks.
- Option B — Quarterly True-up (flex): 6% uplift applied immediately, monthly true-up with overage priced at list per-token. No surge credits but offers a one-time onboarding credit to offset ramp.

Why this? Synthica wants predictable unit economics for product planning but needs headroom for unpredictable spikes. Option A gives the finance team a stable amortization path and the product teams a burst buffer; Option B minimizes upfront commitment but has higher overage surface.

Next steps I suggest: pick preferred path and confirm whether legal can accept the standard SLA credit language (attached). If you prefer, I can convert the preferred option into the DocuSign envelope for signature.

Attachments: quote-v2.xlsx, SLA-appendix.pdf

Best,
Jordan
Jordan Blake | Account Executive
Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 (206) 555-0142



From: Emma Lane <emma.lane@synthicahealth.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@synthicahealth.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 10:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposal: Renewal + Growth Accelerator for Synthica Health

Jordan —

Thanks for the quick turnaround and the clear options. A few clarifying Qs before we loop in procurement:
1) On Option A, how are surge credits invoiced / reconciled? Do unused credits roll into the next quarter?
2) For Option B, can the onboarding credit be spread across the first two invoices rather than a single upfront credit?
3) Our finance director wants the ability to convert to annual at any point during the year without penalty — is that possible and what repricing would apply?

We like the idea of the surge buffer but procurement will push back on a large upfront uplift unless we can see explicit amortization on the invoices.

Can you also add a breakout of the expected invoice impact for Q1 under both options? That would help our CFO.

Thanks,
Emma
Emma Lane | Head of Platform
Synthica Health
emma.lane@synthicahealth.com

On Mon, Nov 30, 2026 at 9:15 AM Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com> wrote:
> [original proposal clipped]

From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 11:05:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Proposal: Renewal + Growth Accelerator for Synthica Health — need pricing options and invoice breakout

Team — forwarding Synthica's clarifying questions (below). Need your best playbook and a quick invoice breakout for Q1 under both Option A and Option B. Key asks from customer:
- Define surge credit reconciliation behavior (do unused credits roll?)
- Split onboarding credit across first two invoices (if possible)
- Ability to convert from quarterly true-up to annual mid-term with clear repricing

My initial thought: offer Option A with credits that expire quarterly (to preserve revenue recognition) but allow a one-time conversion to annual with repricing at the then-current list less a conversion discount (we'd need legal to ok). For Option B, agree to spread the onboarding credit 50/50 across first two invoices.

Please suggest: 1) recommended concession level (max uplift discount we can offer), 2) whether SLA credit 1% is acceptable to include, 3) margins — what floor can we accept?

I'm on a call at 11:30 but can sync after 1pm PT.

Thanks,
Jordan

[Quoted thread]
From: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 02 Dec 2026 08:40:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Proposal: Renewal + Growth Accelerator for Synthica Health — need pricing options and invoice breakout

Jordan —

Good notes. My recommendations below so we can move quickly:

1) Surge credits: make them quarterly-expiring credits tied to the committed uplift. Unused credits do not roll; this keeps accounting simple and preserves urgency to consume. Reconciliation happens at quarter close with a line-item credit or invoice adjustment. That aligns with how we handle other health customers.

2) Onboarding credit: agree to 50/50 split across the first two invoices for Option B only. For Option A, onboarding credit is applied up front as a one-time implementation concession (we can limit to 60% of the onboarding credit in year 1 and the remainder prorated into year 2).

3) Mid-term conversion: allow conversion from quarterly to annual one time with repricing at the current published list less a conversion credit (we should cap the conversion credit to 5% to protect margin). Legal will need to confirm but I think the language is workable.

4) SLA credit: acceptable at 1% for latency SLA if we add a 2-week cure window and caps per quarter (max 3% credit across all SLA failures). That protects us from blowouts.

5) Concession ceiling: don't go below a blended effective uplift of 7% on year-1 committed units. If the customer insists on lower, we should push for a 12-month minimum commitment increase or revenue-protecting clause.

Ben / Priya — please produce a Q1 invoice breakout for both paths showing (a) cashflow impact and (b) GAAP recognition differences. Include tax and sample invoice lines. Attach both files and I'll sign off.

Naomi Feldman
Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
naomi_feldman@redwood.com

From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
To: Emma Lane <emma.lane@synthicahealth.com>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@synthicahealth.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 03 Dec 2026 16:40:00 -0800
Subject: Revised Offer: Renewal + Growth Accelerator (two options)

Emma / Tom —

Appreciate the quick clarification requests; we've incorporated them into a revised offer and attached a clean quote (quote-v3.xlsx). Summary follows — pick Option A or B and I will issue a DocuSign for signature.

Option A — Annual Co-term (recommended)
- 12% uplift on committed units (applies immediately on contract start)
- Quarterly-expiring surge credits equal to 25% of uplift units; unused credits expire at quarter close and are reconciled as a line-item credit
- Quarterly true-up and invoice amortization breakout included in attached Q1 invoice sample
- SLA: 1% service credit for latency breaches after a 2-week cure window; capped at 3% total per quarter
- Conversion: one-time mid-year conversion to annual allowed; repriced at then-current list with a 5% conversion credit cap
- Implementation: one-time 40% onboarding credit applied to first invoice

Option B — Quarterly True-up (flex)
- 6% uplift applied immediately
- No surge credits; overage billed at list per-token rates
- Onboarding credit of $20k split 50/50 across first two invoices
- Conversion allowed (see terms), subject to repricing

Next steps and links
- Attached: quote-v3.xlsx (detailed line items, Q1 invoice sample), SLA-appendix.pdf
- DocuSign link: https://docusign.com/embedded/ABC123-43219 (I can reissue if you prefer a different signer order)
- HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/43219

If you want us to preserve cashflow for accounting, we can provide an invoice amortization schedule for the CFO — Ben and Priya have prepared Q1 and I can share on request. If this looks good I’ll loop legal to finalize the contract language and send the DocuSign envelope.

Thanks and happy to hop on a short call to walk the CFO through the invoice sample.

Best,
Jordan
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
jordan_blake@redwood.com
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0193

Attachments: quote-v3.xlsx, SLA-appendix.pdf

Why this? Synthica wants predictable unit economics for product planning but needs headroom for unpredictable spikes. Option A gives the finance team a stable amortization path and the product teams a burst buffer; Option B minimizes upfront commitment but has higher overage surface.

Next steps I suggest: pick preferred path and confirm whether legal can accept the standard SLA credit language (attached). If you prefer, I can convert the preferred option into the DocuSign envelope for signature.

Attachments: quote-v2.xlsx, SLA-appendix.pdf

Best,
Jordan
Jordan Blake | Account Executive
Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 (206) 555-0142





From: Emma Lane <emma.lane@synthicahealth.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@synthicahealth.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 10:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposal: Renewal + Growth Accelerator for Synthica Health

Jordan —

Thanks for the quick turnaround and the clear options. A few clarifying Qs before we loop in procurement:
1) On Option A, how are surge credits invoiced / reconciled? Do unused credits roll into the next quarter?
2) For Option B, can the onboarding credit be spread across the first two invoices rather than a single upfront credit?
3) Our finance director wants the ability to convert to annual at any point during the year without penalty — is that possible and what repricing would apply?

We like the idea of the surge buffer but procurement will push back on a large upfront uplift unless we can see explicit amortization on the invoices.

Can you also add a breakout of the expected invoice impact for Q1 under both options? That would help our CFO.

Thanks,
Emma
Emma Lane | Head of Platform
Synthica Health
emma.lane@synthicahealth.com

On Mon, Nov 30, 2026 at 9:15 AM Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com> wrote:
> [original proposal clipped]



From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 11:05:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Proposal: Renewal + Growth Accelerator for Synthica Health — need pricing options and invoice breakout

Team — forwarding Synthica's clarifying questions (below). Need your best playbook and a quick invoice breakout for Q1 under both Option A and Option B. Key asks from customer:
- Define surge credit reconciliation behavior (do unused credits roll?)
- Split onboarding credit across first two invoices (if possible)
- Ability to convert from quarterly true-up to annual mid-term with clear repricing

My initial thought: offer Option A with credits that expire quarterly (to preserve revenue recognition) but allow a one-time conversion to annual with repricing at the then-current list less a conversion discount (we'd need legal to ok). For Option B, agree to spread the onboarding credit 50/50 across first two invoices.

Please suggest: 1) recommended concession level (max uplift discount we can offer), 2) whether SLA credit 1% is acceptable to include, 3) margins — what floor can we accept?

I'm on a call at 11:30 but can sync after 1pm PT.

Thanks,
Jordan

[Quoted thread]


From: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 02 Dec 2026 08:40:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Proposal: Renewal + Growth Accelerator for Synthica Health — need pricing options and invoice breakout

Jordan —

Good notes. My recommendations below so we can move quickly:

1) Surge credits: make them quarterly-expiring credits tied to the committed uplift. Unused credits do not roll; this keeps accounting simple and preserves urgency to consume. Reconciliation happens at quarter close with a line-item credit or invoice adjustment. That aligns with how we handle other health customers.

2) Onboarding credit: agree to 50/50 split across the first two invoices for Option B only. For Option A, onboarding credit is applied up front as a one-time implementation concession (we can limit to 60% of the onboarding credit in year 1 and the remainder prorated into year 2).

3) Mid-term conversion: allow conversion from quarterly to annual one time with repricing at the current published list less a conversion credit (we should cap the conversion credit to 5% to protect margin). Legal will need to confirm but I think the language is workable.

4) SLA credit: acceptable at 1% for latency SLA if we add a 2-week cure window and caps per quarter (max 3% credit across all SLA failures). That protects us from blowouts.

5) Concession ceiling: don't go below a blended effective uplift of 7% on year-1 committed units. If the customer insists on lower, we should push for a 12-month minimum commitment increase or revenue-protecting clause.

Ben / Priya — please produce a Q1 invoice breakout for both paths showing (a) cashflow impact and (b) GAAP recognition differences. Include tax and sample invoice lines. Attach both files and I'll sign off.

Naomi Feldman
Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
naomi_feldman@redwood.com



From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
To: Emma Lane <emma.lane@synthicahealth.com>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@synthicahealth.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 03 Dec 2026 16:40:00 -0800
Subject: Revised Offer: Renewal + Growth Accelerator (two options)

Emma / Tom —

Appreciate the quick clarification requests; we've incorporated them into a revised offer and attached a clean quote (quote-v3.xlsx). Summary follows — pick Option A or B and I will issue a DocuSign for signature.

Option A — Annual Co-term (recommended)
- 12% uplift on committed units (applies immediately on contract start)
- Quarterly-expiring surge credits equal to 25% of uplift units; unused credits expire at quarter close and are reconciled as a line-item credit
- Quarterly true-up and invoice amortization breakout included in attached Q1 invoice sample
- SLA: 1% service credit for latency breaches after a 2-week cure window; capped at 3% total per quarter
- Conversion: one-time mid-year conversion to annual allowed; repriced at then-current list with a 5% conversion credit cap
- Implementation: one-time 40% onboarding credit applied to first invoice

Option B — Quarterly True-up (flex)
- 6% uplift applied immediately
- No surge credits; overage billed at list per-token rates
- Onboarding credit of $20k split 50/50 across first two invoices
- Conversion allowed (see terms), subject to repricing

Next steps and links
- Attached: quote-v3.xlsx (detailed line items, Q1 invoice sample), SLA-appendix.pdf
- DocuSign link: https://docusign.com/embedded/ABC123-43219 (I can reissue if you prefer a different signer order)
- HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/43219

If you want us to preserve cashflow for accounting, we can provide an invoice amortization schedule for the CFO — Ben and Priya have prepared Q1 and I can share on request. If this looks good I’ll loop legal to finalize the contract language and send the DocuSign envelope.

Thanks and happy to hop on a short call to walk the CFO through the invoice sample.

Best,
Jordan
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
jordan_blake@redwood.com
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0193

Attachments: quote-v3.xlsx, SLA-appendix.pdf

source.subject: Proposal: move to staggered multi-year term with quarterly trueups
source.body:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Sam Patel <sam.patel@atlaslogix.com>, Laura Chen <laura.chen@atlaslogix.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Proposal: move to staggered multi-year term with quarterly trueups

Hi Sam / Laura,

Thanks again for the time last week — we appreciated the walkthrough of AtlasLogix's forecasted token growth and the Q3 spike drivers. To reduce your unit cost while keeping the flexibility you asked for, attached is a proposal that moves AtlasLogix from a single-year prepay to a 2-year staggered commitment with quarterly trueups and a burst buffer. Key bullets from the attachment:

- Year 1: 20% uplift on current annual baseline, quarterly trueups to align for spikes
- Year 2: 40% uplift with an option to convert unused committed tokens to carryover credits
- Early termination protection: 60-day notice with limited prorated refund for unused committed credit in exceptional cases
- Pricing: step-down unit pricing at 25% uplift band and 50% uplift band; detailed matrix in page 3

I attached a short SOW and a usage extract for Q3 so you can validate the numbers. If you want, we can align the contract to be co-termed with your other vendor agreements (we can do an enforced co-term date in the amendment).

Next steps I propose:
1) Quick sync tomorrow (30 min) to walk the numbers and confirm the baseline;
2) We produce an updated SOW (if needed) and send DocuSign;
3) Execute and schedule onboarding for burst lanes.

If that timeline works I’ll loop in RevOps to get DocuSign ready.

Thanks,
Jordan Blake
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0199

Attachments: AtlasLogix_Renewal_Proposal_Q4.pdf, SOW_AtlasLogix_Q1-27.docx, usage_report_Q3.csv

---
From: Sam Patel <sam.patel@atlaslogix.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Chen <laura.chen@atlaslogix.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 12:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposal: move to staggered multi-year term with quarterly trueups

Jordan — thanks for the proposal. A few quick clarifying points from our side before we lock anything down:

1) Can you confirm whether unused committed tokens in Year 1 can be applied to Year 2 vs only being carried as credits? We want predictability if our usage dips after a big acquisition in Q1.
2) How does the quarterly trueup work operationally? Are we billed immediately for overages or will trueups reconcile and apply to our burst buffer?
3) Any SLA credits tied to sustained latency or availability degradation? We need to map that to our internal vendor scorecard.

If you can address 1–3 and share an updated SOW with the conversion option spelled out, we’ll be ready to move.

—Sam
Sam Patel
VP of Platform | AtlasLogix
sam.patel@atlaslogix.com

---
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Sam Patel <sam.patel@atlaslogix.com>, Laura Chen <laura.chen@atlaslogix.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Dec 2026 08:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposal: move to staggered multi-year term with quarterly trueups

Sam — great questions, answered inline below and updated language is reflected in the attached SOW (SOW_AtlasLogix_Q1-27.docx):

1) Conversion of unused tokens: We offer a convert-or-carry policy. At the end of Year 1 you may elect to convert up to 50% of unused committed tokens into a locked Year 2 uplift (effectively moving those units forward into the Year 2 committed baseline at the Year 2 rate). Any remainder becomes carryover credits valid for 12 months. This is an opt-in election at your trueup date.

2) Quarterly trueups: Operationally we reconcile usage vs committed at the end of each quarter. If you exceed committed in the quarter, we first apply any available burst buffer (applied immediately), then reconcile excess against carryover credits. Any outstanding overage after credits will be invoiced in the following billing cycle. We can also configure a recurring webhook to push quarterly trueup summaries to your finance mailbox.

3) SLA credits: We include a 99.5% availability target for Dedicated customers on this contract tier; we’ll include a schedule of SLA credits in the amendment. For hosted customers we can include a latency SLO with a credit schedule tied to 95th/99th percentile breach windows.

I’ve proposed a short internal runbook for RevOps below so we don’t miss co-term alignment and billing cadence.

If that looks good, I’ll ask RevOps to prepare DocuSign and we can aim to sign by EOW.

Jordan

---
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Dec 2026 10:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposal: move to staggered multi-year term with quarterly trueups

Looping in pricing nuance: Jordan — per the matrix, confirm that the step-down bands are applied on actual billed units (post-trueup) rather than committed tokens. Also add a clause that any carryover credits credited from Year 1 will be valued at the Year 1 effective unit rate for simplicity.

Priya — can you draft the amendment language for the conversion election (opt-in at Year 1 trueup) and add the co-term block we discussed last quarter?

—Marissa
VP, Sales Strategy | Redwood Inference

---
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Sam Patel <sam.patel@atlaslogix.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 2 Dec 2026 09:36:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposal: move to staggered multi-year term with quarterly trueups

Sam / Laura — attaching an updated SOW and the proposal PDF with the conversion and carryover language clarified (see attachments). I also added the co-term amendment block and a short billing cadence example table so finance can model cashflow. DocuSign link is ready: https://www.docusign.com/sign/XYZ-Atlas-8342

Attachments: SOW_AtlasLogix_Q1-27.docx, AtlasLogix_Renewal_Proposal_Q4.pdf

Let me know a preferred signer and we’ll route the DocuSign envelope.

Thanks,
Priya Natarajan
Senior Account Manager | Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com

---
From: Sam Patel <sam.patel@atlaslogix.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 2 Dec 2026 16:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposal: move to staggered multi-year term with quarterly trueups

Thanks — that addresses our questions. Laura will be signer but I’ll confirm her availability for Friday. Please hold the DocuSign envelope until I confirm the signer name (Laura prefers to review the billing cadence table with our FP&A first).

Once signed we’ll want a kickoff with your Onboarding team to validate burst lanes and webhook delivery.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.
—Sam


Forwarding history and quotes preserved above.

Subject: Re: Proposal: move to staggered multi-year term with quarterly trueups

Thanks — that addresses our questions. Laura will be signer but I’ll confirm her availability for Friday. Please hold the DocuSign envelope until I confirm the signer name (Laura prefers to review the billing cadence table with our FP&A first).

Once signed we’ll want a kickoff with your Onboarding team to validate burst lanes and webhook delivery.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.
—Sam


Forwarding history and quotes preserved above.

messages:
From: Alex Turner <alex.turner@finex.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: security@finex.com
Date: Sun, 29 Nov 2026 16:14:00 +0000
Subject: Request: VM cadence + SLA + CVE closure evidence (SOC2)

Hi Camila,

We're prepping for our SOC 2 Readiness review and need a compact evidence packet from Redwood that covers:
- Scan cadence for public-facing infra and customer-facing services (what scanners, frequency).
- SLA commitments / targets for CVE remediation by severity (P1/P2/P3) and an exception process.
- A short attestation that CVE-2026-1234 (noted in our intake) has been triaged and closed, with dates and owners.

Can you send a one-page attestation + the latest scan export and a CSV with remediation timestamps? Our auditor wants this by EOD PST Tuesday (2026-12-01).

Thanks,
Alex Turner
Sr. InfoSec Manager
Finex Bank

--
Alex Turner
Finex Bank | security@finex.com

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Turner <alex.turner@finex.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 29 Nov 2026 17:02:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Request: VM cadence + SLA + CVE closure evidence (SOC2)

Hi Alex — thanks for the heads-up. We can pull together the artifact set and a short attestation. Quick notes before I assemble the packet:

1) We operate two scan rhythms: authenticated baseline scans across services (weekly) and external/CI pipeline scans for edge services (daily). We also run monthly supply-chain dependency scans.
2) Our internal SLA targets are: P1 (critical): fix or mitigation within 7 days; P2 (high): 30 days; P3 (medium/low): 90 days. Exceptions are logged and timeboxed with engineering and CS notified.
3) For CVE-2026-1234 specifically we committed an emergency patch deploy on 2026-11-18 and completed post-patch validation on 11-22; final closure notes and owners will be included.

I'm looping in Priya (VM lead) for timestamps and the remediation CSV. Marissa: can you confirm the exact wording you'd like on the attestation header for SOC 2?

I'll aim to deliver the packet + attestation and attachments by EOD PST Tuesday. If you need anything sooner, tell me which doc to prioritize.

Regards,
Camila Reyes
Security & Compliance Ops
Redwood Inference

Attachments: (none in this reply)

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 09:25:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Request: VM cadence + SLA + CVE closure evidence (SOC2)

Camila / Marissa —

I pulled the remediation ledger and scan exports. Summary below (I also uploaded files to the shared Drive link):

- Scanners: Trivy (container deps), Nessus (external network), Internal authenticated scanner (agent) for servers.
- Cadence: External edge (daily), Authenticated functional scans (weekly), Dependency SBOM/OSS scans (monthly).
- SLA by severity (applies to customer-impacting services):
  * Critical (P1): 7 days (mitigation or rollback + hotfix), owner assigned to oncall eng/SEC-ops.
  * High (P2): 30 days (target remediation + test), tracked in Jira.
  * Medium/Low (P3): 90 days.
- Exceptions: We create an "Exception Record" ticket that includes risk acceptance, compensating controls, and a timebox. We route exceptions to platform leads + compliance and log them in the evidence ledger.

CVE-2026-1234 timeline (from remediation_log_202611.csv):
- 2026-11-12 02:14Z: Internal detection (fuzzing + external report).
- 2026-11-12 05:40Z: Triage assigned (owner: eng-ops/sre-7).
- 2026-11-18 14:10Z: Emergency patch released to canary.
- 2026-11-19 03:55Z: Canary validation passed; started progressive rollout.
- 2026-11-22 08:20Z: Post-deploy validation complete; remediation verified.
- 2026-11-23 11:00Z: Closure ticket created and attached to the remediation ledger.

Files uploaded: redwood_scan_20261129.pdf (exported external scan), remediation_log_202611.csv (timestamped ledger), cve-2026-1234-closure-statement.pdf (one-page closure summary).

If you want additional attestations (signed by security leadership), tell me and I'll route to legal for the signature block.

—Priya
Priya Natarajan
Vulnerability Management Lead
Redwood Inference

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Turner <alex.turner@finex.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 1 Dec 2026 17:02:00 +0000
Subject: Finex Bank: VM evidence packet and attestation (delivered)

Alex —

Attached: redwood_scan_20261129.pdf, remediation_log_202611.csv, cve-2026-1234-closure-statement.pdf. Below is the attestation text for your SOC 2 review: 

---
Redwood Inference Vulnerability Management Attestation (excerpt)
Date: 2026-12-01

Redwood Inference maintains documented vulnerability scanning and remediation processes across production and customer-facing assets. Scan cadence: external edge services scanned daily; authenticated functional scans run weekly; dependency and SBOM scans run monthly.

Our operational SLA targets are: Critical (P1) — remediation or mitigant within 7 days; High (P2) — 30 days; Medium/Low (P3) — 90 days. Exceptions are timeboxed and recorded in our exception ledger; compensating controls are documented with approvals.

CVE-2026-1234 (customer-reported): triaged on 2026-11-12; emergency patch released 2026-11-18; progressive rollout validated by 2026-11-22; closure verified on 2026-11-23. The remediation ledger and scan exports are attached for audit.

We confirm that the attached artifacts are true exports from our vulnerability management systems and that the timelines above reflect completed remediation activities.

Signed,
Camila Reyes
Security & Compliance Ops
Redwood Inference
---

Attachments: redwood_scan_20261129.pdf (PDF, 5.8MB), remediation_log_202611.csv (CSV, 42KB), cve-2026-1234-closure-statement.pdf (PDF, 0.4MB)

Let me know if the auditor wants a signature from our CISO or an expanded evidence bundle; we can provide a longer ledger export and Jira links under NDA.

Best,
Camila
(end of thread)
