source.subject: Recap + next steps: NovaCorp trial readiness & responsibility census
messages:
From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dana Liao <dana.liao@novacorp.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 27 Nov 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Recap + next steps: NovaCorp trial readiness & responsibility census

Hi Dana —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we can start the trial without surprises. I focused this on concrete acceptance criteria, who owns what, and a minimal timeline to get infra and procurement aligned.

Summary (one-liner): run a 2-week sandbox trial of the 1.3B inference profile against NovaCorp’s anonymized support logs; pass = latency < 200ms p95 on 256-token median requests AND no data exfil concerns from security scan.

Success criteria (final acceptance tests):
- Performance: p95 latency <= 200ms for 256-token median requests with continuous batching enabled (measured on Redwood Dedicated test pool).
- Functional: model achieves >= 0.78 F1 on your 200-case label set for routing recommendations.
- Security: SOC2 evidence checklist items 3.1–3.4 reviewed and no unresolved high-risk items.
- Ops: deployment playbook executed end-to-end (whitelist, KV cache seeding, rollback plan).

Owners (responsibility census):
- Infra (provisioning, VPC peering, whitelists): Marcus Lin (Redwood) -> Marcus will own test pool prep and temporary credentials.
- Application integration (routing, schema mapping): Marissa Cole (Redwood) -> app-side adapters and metrics hooks.
- Security & compliance (evidence review, data handling): Vanessa Ortiz (Redwood) -> runs SOC2 checklist and coordinate with your security contact.
- Procurement & contracting (SLA paperwork, invoice): Priya Natarajan (Redwood) -> commercial touchpoint for trial T&Cs and any PO.

Immediate actions I propose:
1) Shared MAP (attached novacorp-map-v1.xlsx) — please confirm owners and fill missing emails by Mon 11/30.
2) Marcus to provision test pool and share ephemeral creds — target provisioned by Wed 12/2.
3) NovaCorp security to reply with the single POC email for the SOC2 evidence exchange (we referenced soc2-evidence-request-form.pdf).
4) Kickoff demo + connectivity check on Tue 12/7 (30 min) — propose 10:00 PT.

Attachments: novacorp-map-v1.xlsx; soc2-evidence-request-form.pdf

Happy to adjust timelines if you have constraints — calling out what I think we need to move fast.

Thanks,
Priyom
Priyom Das | Senior AE, Redwood Inference
pri8yom.das@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0127
From: Dana Liao <dana.liao@novacorp.com>
To: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Markus Vogel <markus.v@novacorp.com>, procurement@novacorp.com
Date: Fri, 27 Nov 2026 16:18:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Recap + next steps: NovaCorp trial readiness & responsibility census

Priyom —

Thanks for the thorough recap. This matches what we discussed; a few notes from our side:

- Timeline: 12/7 kickoff is okay but our security lead is out the week of 11/30. Can we shift the provisioning deadline by one business day (provision by Thu 12/3) so they have time to review the SOC2 form?
- Security contact: I’m copying Markus (markus.v@novacorp.com) who will coordinate our internal security and provide the POC email.
- Procurement: I’ve looped in procurement@novacorp.com — they’ll want the standard trial agreement and an estimated cost note for recurring use.

We’ll review the MAP and populate missing owner emails by Monday.

On the success criteria — the perf target looks reasonable but we need to confirm the measurement harness; will you share the test harness or do we integrate the sample client?

Quick ask: can you also attach the list of temporary ports/IPs you expect to use so our network team can start a whitelist?

Thanks,
Dana Liao
Head of ML Ops, NovaCorp

> On Nov 27, 2026, at 14:05, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
>
> [original message trimmed]
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dana Liao <dana.liao@novacorp.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 08:07:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Recap + next steps: NovaCorp trial readiness & responsibility census

Dana —

Following up with the implementation side notes and a small owner census attachment (owner-census.csv). A few clarifications:

- Provisioning: Marcus will spin up a Dedicated test pool (no multi-tenant mixing) and share ephemeral API key + client sample by 12/03 EOD PT. Marcus — can you confirm the VM sizes and expected IPs?
- Measurement harness: Redwood will provide a lightweight JAR and a Python client with the load script (we will host a short README in the Drive link). That’s what we’ll use to measure p95. If you prefer to run the harness inside your environment and report telemetry, we can accept signed results (we’ll share the expected metrics schema).
- Whitelist: expected outbound ports 443, and inbound ephemeral IPs provided by Marcus. I’ll paste the initial IP block below once Marcus confirms.
- Procurement: Priya will send the standard trial MSA (no long-term commitment, trial acceptance = pass criteria above). Procurement@novacorp: please respond with any PO needs; our finance team can issue a one-time invoice if needed.

Owner census (high level):
- Marcus Lin (Infra) — provisioning, ephemeral creds, IPs
- Marissa Cole (App) — adapter + telemetry hooks + test dataset ingestion
- Vanessa Ortiz (Security) — SOC2 checklist, secure creds handoff, encryption verification
- Priya Natarajan (Commercial) — trial MSA, PO coordination, billing

Attachments: owner-census.csv (simple CSV with 'role,owner,email,notes')

We’ll make the IP list available by EOD Wed after Marcus finishes the pool.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer | Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dana Liao <dana.liao@novacorp.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 09:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Recap + next steps: NovaCorp trial readiness & responsibility census

Hi all —

Thanks for the quick updates. From a compliance perspective a couple of practical items before kickoff:

1) Evidence exchange: Dana/Markus — please confirm the single security POC (email) we should use for the SOC2 packet. We’ll transmit the form and request specific control artifacts (I’ve listed them in soc2-evidence-request-form.pdf).
2) Data handling: trial will run only on anonymized support logs; please ensure a single scrubbed dataset is provided. If you need a script, Marissa can run the scrub locally and send an example.
3) Network: Marcus — once you post the IPs we’ll validate TLS cert chains and request your firewall team to whitelist the ephemeral creds host.

I agree with the shifted provisioning target (12/3) and the kickoff on 12/7. Priyom — please hold the 10:00 PT slot; we’ll use that time for a short connectivity check and responsibility handoff.

Next steps for NovaCorp:
- Markus: confirm security POC + expected IPs (by 12/2)
- Dana: confirm the scrubbed dataset owner and timeline (by 12/1)
- Procurement@novacorp: confirm whether a PO is required for the trial (Procurement reply)

I’ll follow up with the SOC2 checklist items we’ll want completed pre-kickoff.

Cheers,
Vanessa Ortiz
Head of Security Partnerships, Redwood Inference

title: Recap + next steps: NovaCorp trial readiness & responsibility census
preview:
From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dana Liao <dana.liao@novacorp.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 27 Nov 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Recap + next steps: NovaCorp trial readiness & responsibility census

Hi Dana —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we can start the trial without surprises. I focused this on concrete acceptance criteria, who owns what, and a minimal timeline to get infra and procurement aligned.

Summary (one-liner): run a 2-week sandbox trial of the 1.3B inference profile against NovaCorp’s anonymized support logs; pass = latency < 200ms p95 on 256-token median requests AND no data exfil concerns from security scan.

Success criteria (final acceptance tests):
- Performance: p95 latency <= 200ms for 256-token median requests with continuous batching enabled (measured on Redwood Dedicated test pool).
- Functional: model achieves >= 0.78 F1 on your 200-case label set for routing recommendations.
- Security: SOC2 evidence checklist items 3.1–3.4 reviewed and no unresolved high-risk items.
- Ops: deployment playbook executed end-to-end (whitelist, KV cache seeding, rollback plan).

Owners (responsibility census):
- Infra (provisioning, VPC peering, whitelists): Marcus Lin (Redwood) -> Marcus will own test pool prep and temporary credentials.
- Application integration (routing, schema mapping): Marissa Cole (Redwood) -> app-side adapters and metrics hooks.
- Security & compliance (evidence review, data handling): Vanessa Ortiz (Redwood) -> runs SOC2 checklist and coordinate with your security contact.
- Procurement & contracting (SLA paperwork, invoice): Priya Natarajan (Redwood) -> commercial touchpoint for trial T&Cs and any PO.

Immediate actions I propose:
1) Shared MAP (attached novacorp-map-v1.xlsx) — please confirm owners and fill missing emails by Mon 11/30.
2) Marcus to provision test pool and share ephemeral creds — target provisioned by Wed 12/2.
3) NovaCorp security to reply with the single POC email for the SOC2 evidence exchange (we referenced soc2-evidence-request-form.pdf).
4) Kickoff demo + connectivity check on Tue 12/7 (30 min) — propose 10:00 PT.

Attachments: novacorp-map-v1.xlsx; soc2-evidence-request-form.pdf

Happy to adjust timelines if you have constraints — calling out what I think we need to move fast.

Thanks,
Priyom
Priyom Das | Senior AE, Redwood Inference
pri8yom.das@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0127


From: Dana Liao <dana.liao@novacorp.com>
To: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Markus Vogel <markus.v@novacorp.com>, procurement@novacorp.com
Date: Fri, 27 Nov 2026 16:18:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Recap + next steps: NovaCorp trial readiness & responsibility census

Priyom —

Thanks for the thorough recap. This matches what we discussed; a few notes from our side:

- Timeline: 12/7 kickoff is okay but our security lead is out the week of 11/30. Can we shift the provisioning deadline by one business day (provision by Thu 12/3) so they have time to review the SOC2 form?
- Security contact: I’m copying Markus (markus.v@novacorp.com) who will coordinate our internal security and provide the POC email.
- Procurement: I’ve looped in procurement@novacorp.com — they’ll want the standard trial agreement and an estimated cost note for recurring use.

We’ll review the MAP and populate missing owner emails by Monday.

On the success criteria — the perf target looks reasonable but we need to confirm the measurement harness; will you share the test harness or do we integrate the sample client?

Quick ask: can you also attach the list of temporary ports/IPs you expect to use so our network team can start a whitelist?

Thanks,
Dana Liao
Head of ML Ops, NovaCorp

> On Nov 27, 2026, at 14:05, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
>
> [original message trimmed]


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dana Liao <dana.liao@novacorp.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 08:07:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Recap + next steps: NovaCorp trial readiness & responsibility census

Dana —

Following up with the implementation side notes and a small owner census attachment (owner-census.csv). A few clarifications:

- Provisioning: Marcus will spin up a Dedicated test pool (no multi-tenant mixing) and share ephemeral API key + client sample by 12/03 EOD PT. Marcus — can you confirm the VM sizes and expected IPs?
- Measurement harness: Redwood will provide a lightweight JAR and a Python client with the load script (we will host a short README in the Drive link). That’s what we’ll use to measure p95. If you prefer to run the harness inside your environment and report telemetry, we can accept signed results (we’ll share the expected metrics schema).
- Whitelist: expected outbound ports 443, and inbound ephemeral IPs provided by Marcus. I’ll paste the initial IP block below once Marcus confirms.
- Procurement: Priya will send the standard trial MSA (no long-term commitment, trial acceptance = pass criteria above). Procurement@novacorp: please respond with any PO needs; our finance team can issue a one-time invoice if needed.

Owner census (high level):
- Marcus Lin (Infra) — provisioning, ephemeral creds, IPs
- Marissa Cole (App) — adapter + telemetry hooks + test dataset ingestion
- Vanessa Ortiz (Security) — SOC2 checklist, secure creds handoff, encryption verification
- Priya Natarajan (Commercial) — trial MSA, PO coordination, billing

Attachments: owner-census.csv (simple CSV with 'role,owner,email,notes')

We’ll make the IP list available by EOD Wed after Marcus finishes the pool.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer | Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dana Liao <dana.liao@novacorp.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 09:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Recap + next steps: NovaCorp trial readiness & responsibility census

Hi all —

Thanks for the quick updates. From a compliance perspective a couple of practical items before kickoff:

1) Evidence exchange: Dana/Markus — please confirm the single security POC (email) we should use for the SOC2 packet. We’ll transmit the form and request specific control artifacts (I’ve listed them in soc2-evidence-request-form.pdf).
2) Data handling: trial will run only on anonymized support logs; please ensure a single scrubbed dataset is provided. If you need a script, Marissa can run the scrub locally and send an example.
3) Network: Marcus — once you post the IPs we’ll validate TLS cert chains and request your firewall team to whitelist the ephemeral creds host.

I agree with the shifted provisioning target (12/3) and the kickoff on 12/7. Priyom — please hold the 10:00 PT slot; we’ll use that time for a short connectivity check and responsibility handoff.

Next steps for NovaCorp:
- Markus: confirm security POC + expected IPs (by 12/2)
- Dana: confirm the scrubbed dataset owner and timeline (by 12/1)
- Procurement@novacorp: confirm whether a PO is required for the trial (Procurement reply)

I’ll follow up with the SOC2 checklist items we’ll want completed pre-kickoff.

Cheers,
Vanessa Ortiz
Head of Security Partnerships, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Activation sprint: account gating & environment readiness for Lumacare private deployment
messages:
From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
To: Harriet Cole <harriet@lumacare.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-28T14:22:00Z
Subject: Lumacare private deploy — pre-kickoff checklist and account gating

Hi Harriet,

Thanks again for the sync today. As discussed, we want to run a short, focused activation sprint next week to get the private deployment into a validated staging state before production cutover. To keep things tight I've attached a concise checklist and the network diagram we sketched.

Key asks for Lumacare to confirm before the sprint:
- Service account + minimum IAM permissions for the provisioner (see checklist item 2)
- Egress IP allocation and NAT path for telemetry + health-check endpoints
- KMS access patterns (whether you want Redwood to use a customer-managed key or our transient key)
- SOC 2 evidence request owner for the audit artifacts we may need to collect during onboarding

Can you confirm who's owning each item on your side? If Procurement needs a separate thread/DocuSign for the MSA addendum, loop them in and we'll accelerate.

Proposed sprint kickoff: Mon Dec 1 10:00 AM PT — 60 minutes. Ben and I will lead the infra validation steps; Monica will handle the deployment runbook handoff.

Thanks,

Dylan Brooks
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
+1 415-555-0123

Attachments: redwood-private-onboard-checklist-v3.pdf, lumacare-network-diagram-v1.pdf

From: Harriet Cole <harriet@lumacare.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rajiv Menon <rajiv@lumacare.com>, Procurement <contracts@lumacare.com>
Date: 2026-11-29T09:10:00Z
Subject: Re: Lumacare private deploy — pre-kickoff checklist and account gating

Hi Dylan — thanks for the clear list and the materials. I've assigned items internally:
- IAM/service account: Rajiv (own)
- Egress IPs & NAT: Infrastructure team (I'll confirm public/peering options by EOD Monday)
- KMS preference: We prefer customer-managed keys (KMS) — security wants HSM-backed where available
- SOC 2 evidence owner: Procurement will coordinate (cc'd) but I'll be the primary contact for technical questions

We can do the kickoff Mon Dec 1 at 10am PT. Procurement (contracts@lumacare.com) — if you need any changes to the addendum, please advise; we can sign a short SOW for activation work.

Quick question: for the KMS flow, do you require any cross-account IAM roles from our side or is Redwood able to pull keys via a restricted KMS grant? If we need to create roles I'll get Rajiv to provision them ahead of the sprint.

—Harriet
Harriet Cole
Head of IT Operations, Lumacare Health
harriet@lumacare.com

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Harriet Cole <harriet@lumacare.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-30T13:05:00Z
Subject: Re: Lumacare private deploy — pre-kickoff checklist and account gating

Harriet — great to hear. Quick notes ahead of the kickoff:

1) KMS: We support both models. For customer-managed keys we typically ask for either a 1) restricted cross-account role that allows our provisioning service to Create/Decrypt CMKs, or 2) a KMS grant scoped to the key for the bootstrap window. If Lumacare prefers the grant approach we'll send the exact grant statement and lifetime TTL.

2) Network: If you go with VPC peering, please add the prefix list for the Redwood control plane (attached in the diagram) and allow outbound TCP 443 for health-check and telemetry endpoints. If NAT + egress IPs are used, provide four IPs for the canary range and two for fallback.

3) Service accounts: Please include the service account email in the onboarding form (see checklist page 2) and grant iam.serviceAccounts.actAs to the role the provisioner uses.

4) Evidence/Artifacts: Procurement — we will need a contact to receive SOC 2 evidence requests. We can stage automated evidence collection once access is validated.

Attached: activation-map-lumacare.docx (high level MAP with timeboxes). We'll capture the sprint runbook during the kickoff and leave 15 minutes for smoke validation.

See you Monday — sending calendar invite now.

Ben Carter
Director, Customer Success (Onboarding), Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

---
On Fri, Nov 29, 2026 at 09:10 Harriet Cole <harriet@lumacare.com> wrote:
> Hi Dylan — thanks for the clear list and the materials. I've assigned items internally:
> - IAM/service account: Rajiv (own)
> - Egress IPs & NAT: Infrastructure team (I'll confirm public/peering options by EOD Monday)
> - KMS preference: We prefer customer-managed keys (KMS) — security wants HSM-backed where available
> - SOC 2 evidence owner: Procurement will coordinate (cc'd) but I'll be the primary contact for technical questions
> 
> We can do the kickoff Mon Dec 1 at 10am PT. Procurement (contracts@lumacare.com) — if you need any changes to the addendum, please advise; we can sign a short SOW for activation work.
> 
> —Harriet

From: Rajiv Menon <rajiv@lumacare.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Harriet Cole <harriet@lumacare.com>, Procurement <contracts@lumacare.com>
Date: 2026-11-30T16:03:00Z
Subject: Re: Lumacare private deploy — pre-kickoff checklist and account gating

Hi all,

Confirmed on our side: I'll provision the service account and share the email by close of day. On KMS — security prefers a KMS grant for the bootstrap period, not a permanent cross-account role. Please send the grant template and lifetime you recommend.

I've reviewed the network diagram and will validate the four canary IPs with our NAT owner; we may be able to reserve two immediately and two as fallback but will aim to confirm before Tue.

Looking forward to the kickoff. For the DocuSign / SOW, Procurement — please advise if you want Redwood to draft the short activation addendum and we can route it for signature.

Rajiv Menon
Senior Cloud Engineer, Lumacare Health
rajiv@lumacare.com

> On Nov 30, 2026, at 13:05, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> 
> > Harriet — great to hear. Quick notes ahead of the kickoff:
> > ...

I've reviewed the network diagram and will validate the four canary IPs with our NAT owner; we may be able to reserve two immediately and two as fallback but will aim to confirm before Tue.

Looking forward to the kickoff. For the DocuSign / SOW, Procurement — please advise if you want Redwood to draft the short activation addendum and we can route it for signature.

Rajiv Menon
Senior Cloud Engineer, Lumacare Health
rajiv@lumacare.com

text:
From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
To: Harriet Cole <harriet@lumacare.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-28T14:22:00Z
Subject: Lumacare private deploy — pre-kickoff checklist and account gating

Hi Harriet,

Thanks again for the sync today. As discussed, we want to run a short, focused activation sprint next week to get the private deployment into a validated staging state before production cutover. To keep things tight I've attached a concise checklist and the network diagram we sketched.

Key asks for Lumacare to confirm before the sprint:
- Service account + minimum IAM permissions for the provisioner (see checklist item 2)
- Egress IP allocation and NAT path for telemetry + health-check endpoints
- KMS access patterns (whether you want Redwood to use a customer-managed key or our transient key)
- SOC 2 evidence request owner for the audit artifacts we may need to collect during onboarding

Can you confirm who's owning each item on your side? If Procurement needs a separate thread/DocuSign for the MSA addendum, loop them in and we'll accelerate.

Proposed sprint kickoff: Mon Dec 1 10:00 AM PT — 60 minutes. Ben and I will lead the infra validation steps; Monica will handle the deployment runbook handoff.

Thanks,

Dylan Brooks
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
+1 415-555-0123

Attachments: redwood-private-onboard-checklist-v3.pdf, lumacare-network-diagram-v1.pdf

From: Harriet Cole <harriet@lumacare.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rajiv Menon <rajiv@lumacare.com>, Procurement <contracts@lumacare.com>
Date: 2026-11-29T09:10:00Z
Subject: Re: Lumacare private deploy — pre-kickoff checklist and account gating

Hi Dylan — thanks for the clear list and the materials. I've assigned items internally:
- IAM/service account: Rajiv (own)
- Egress IPs & NAT: Infrastructure team (I'll confirm public/peering options by EOD Monday)
- KMS preference: We prefer customer-managed keys (KMS) — security wants HSM-backed where available
- SOC 2 evidence owner: Procurement will coordinate (cc'd) but I'll be the primary contact for technical questions

We can do the kickoff Mon Dec 1 at 10am PT. Procurement (contracts@lumacare.com) — if you need any changes to the addendum, please advise; we can sign a short SOW for activation work.

Quick question: for the KMS flow, do you require any cross-account IAM roles from our side or is Redwood able to pull keys via a restricted KMS grant? If we need to create roles I'll get Rajiv to provision them ahead of the sprint.

—Harriet
Harriet Cole
Head of IT Operations, Lumacare Health
harriet@lumacare.com

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Harriet Cole <harriet@lumacare.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-30T13:05:00Z
Subject: Re: Lumacare private deploy — pre-kickoff checklist and account gating

Harriet — great to hear. Quick notes ahead of the kickoff:

1) KMS: We support both models. For customer-managed keys we typically ask for either a 1) restricted cross-account role that allows our provisioning service to Create/Decrypt CMKs, or 2) a KMS grant scoped to the key for the bootstrap window. If Lumacare prefers the grant approach we'll send the exact grant statement and lifetime TTL.

2) Network: If you go with VPC peering, please add the prefix list for the Redwood control plane (attached in the diagram) and allow outbound TCP 443 for health-check and telemetry endpoints. If NAT + egress IPs are used, provide four IPs for the canary range and two for fallback.

3) Service accounts: Please include the service account email in the onboarding form (see checklist page 2) and grant iam.serviceAccounts.actAs to the role the provisioner uses.

4) Evidence/Artifacts: Procurement — we will need a contact to receive SOC 2 evidence requests. We can stage automated evidence collection once access is validated.

Attached: activation-map-lumacare.docx (high level MAP with timeboxes). We'll capture the sprint runbook during the kickoff and leave 15 minutes for smoke validation.

See you Monday — sending calendar invite now.

Ben Carter
Director, Customer Success (Onboarding), Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

source.subject: PrimeFare: sustained token backlog & reserved throughput gap — exec + runbook
source.content:
From: Michael Baxter <michael.baxter@primefare.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sarah Holt <sarah.holt@primefare.com>
Date: Sat, 28 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Spike in 5xx and latency on our dedicated pool (PrimeFare Prod)

David —

We started seeing elevated 5xx rates and a 3x tail latency increase on our dedicated cluster around 08:45 PT. The issue is sustained for the last 30 minutes and it's impacting checkout flows (time-to-confirm). Attached are the logs and a short sample trace. Can you confirm this is coming from the reserved pool and whether you can route us to a fallback model? We have a Black Friday test tomorrow and are very concerned.

Thanks,
Michael
Michael Baxter
Head of Platform, PrimeFare
---

On Sat, Nov 28, 2026 at 09:30 AM David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai> wrote:
To: Michael Baxter <michael.baxter@primefare.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 28 Nov 2026 09:30:12 -0800
Subject: Re: Spike in 5xx and latency on our dedicated pool (PrimeFare Prod)

Michael — thanks for the quick heads-up and for sending logs. We're triaging now. Initial notes:
- I can confirm the requests are hitting your reserved capacity and are queueing at the head of the NVMe-backed KV cache layer.
- Ops is checking kernel queuing and model-cold-start rates.

Immediate plan (short):
1) Enable routing to a quantized model variant we host (same family, -q4_0) to reduce per-token GPU time (expected latency drop ~30%).
2) Evict long-lived KV entries for PrimeFare to unstuck the prefix backlog (safe for 60s window).
3) Approve a short burst up to an estimated $12k to buy extra headroom while autoscale catches up.

Marcus / Priya — please confirm feasibility and ETA for steps 1–2; Naomi, cc'ed, will coordinate customer comms and any credit conversation.

David
VP, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

On Sat, Nov 28, 2026 at 10:05 AM Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai> wrote:
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 28 Nov 2026 10:05:45 -0800
Subject: Re: Spike in 5xx and latency on our dedicated pool (PrimeFare Prod)

David — quick ops readout:
- Root signal: sustained high QPS from a handful of hot endpoints combined with a model variant that has larger KV state per session. The dedicated pool is hitting NVMe bandwidth saturation; token interleaving is increasing stall times.
- We see a backlog of prefix entries (~120k entries) pinned to PrimeFare's worker set. Prefix eviction will reduce queue depth.
- Autoscale did try to add nodes but failed due to shortfalls in reserved-slot allocation (your reservation maxed for the hour).

Actions I'm enacting now (ETA ~8–12m):
- Trigger KV eviction (safe window) and prioritise in-queue requests based on latency SLO.
- Route new requests to -q4_0 fallback model for PrimeFare. I will drop the live model weight to 70% and route 30% to fallback to monitor quality.
- Attach: svc-qps-graph.png (QPS and queue depth).

If fallback reduces error rate we can expand routing. If queueing persists, we'll need a temporary cross-tenant burst (ops alloc) — requires exec approval since it uses non-reserved headroom.

Marcus
Senior SRE, Serving Runtime

On Sat, Nov 28, 2026 at 11:00 AM Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai> wrote:
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 28 Nov 2026 11:00:01 -0800
Subject: Re: Spike in 5xx and latency on our dedicated pool (PrimeFare Prod)

Details / runbook I'm applying: 
1) Routing change: update routing table for RouteID PF-3247 to point to quantized variant (model-redwood-7b-q4_0) and set initial split 70/30 (live/fallback).
2) KV eviction: issue eviction with TTL=60s to clear stale prefixes. Monitoring hooks will check for cache miss amplification.
3) Batch tuning: lower max batch size for PF endpoints from 64->40 to reduce stall variance in the shared headroom window.
4) Observability: enable extra token-level tracing for 30 minutes (sampling 5%) to validate no semantic regression.

Estimated impact: expected tail-latency reduction of 25–35% and 5xx reduction to baseline within ~12–20 minutes if backlog clears.
Estimated operator time: 25m for changes and 1–2h of monitoring.

Cost note: short burst of 4–6 GPUs estimated at ~$10–14k for 2h depending on region; attached proposed-burst-costs.xlsx.

Priya
Principal Engineer, Serving Runtime

On Sat, Nov 28, 2026 at 11:20 AM Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai> wrote:
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Michael Baxter <michael.baxter@primefare.com>, Sarah Holt <sarah.holt@primefare.com>
Date: Sat, 28 Nov 2026 11:20:10 -0800
Subject: Re: Spike in 5xx and latency on our dedicated pool (PrimeFare Prod)

Michael / Sarah — thank you for flagging. We're enacting a mitigation path now that should reduce errors quickly.
Short message for customers: 
"We’ve identified a prefix-caching backlog on your reserved cluster causing elevated errors and tail latency. We’ve routed a portion of your traffic to a quantized fallback, evicted stale cache entries, and are monitoring. Expected stabilization in 15–30 minutes. We’ll follow up with a post-incident summary and proposed credits for impact window"

Customer ask: Michael — would you like us to increase the fallback routing to 50% immediately (higher latency gain but more model variance) or keep the gradual 30% while we validate? Also, I’ll need approval from David/Jonas to provision cross-tenant burst credit if we need >2h.

Naomi Feldman
Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference

On Sun, Nov 29, 2026 at 08:15 AM Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai> wrote:
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 29 Nov 2026 08:15:00 -0800
Subject: Re: PrimeFare: sustained token backlog & reserved throughput gap — exec + runbook

Team — quick leadership note: I reviewed the ops notes and cost estimate. Approve a provisional cross-tenant burst up to $15,000 to avoid customer outage for the next 4 hours. Please document exact spend and revert if we go beyond.

Two asks from me: 
- Naomi: send the customer-facing status update and include a promise for a post-incident credit discussion (we should aim for a 10–15% credit of the impacted window).
- David / Priya: prepare a short postmortem with three recommended product changes (e.g., per-reservation KV quota, earlier autoscale thresholds, and a routing knob for immediate fallback).

Jonas Weber
VP, Engineering Operations

On Sun, Nov 29, 2026 at 18:42 PM David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai> wrote:
To: Michael Baxter <michael.baxter@primefare.com>, Sarah Holt <sarah.holt@primefare.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 29 Nov 2026 18:42:30 -0800
Subject: Re: PrimeFare: sustained token backlog & reserved throughput gap — exec + runbook

Michael / Sarah — final status for this window and next steps: 
- What we changed: route 40% of incoming requests to the quantized fallback (model-redwood-7b-q4_0), evicted stale KV prefixes, lowered batch size for your endpoints.
- Outcome: tail latency reduced by ~28%, 5xx rate dropped to ~0.6% from 4.2% during peak. Queue depth returned to baseline after ~18 minutes.
- Authorization: Leadership approved a provisional burst of up to $15k; we used ~ $9.1k (see attached).

Next steps / commitments: 
1) Postmortem to follow by Tue EOD with root cause and three product fixes.
2) We’ll credit PrimeFare for the impact window — Naomi will propose 12% credit and follow with accounting and a DocuSign amendment if required.
3) Engineering recommendation: allow per-reservation KV quotas and an emergency fallback routing toggle (internal) — Priya will draft specs.

Appreciate your patience — we’ll set a briefing with our CTO if you want deeper technical coverage.

David Kim
VP, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

Attachments referenced above: primefare-error-logs-1128.zip, svc-qps-graph.png, proposed-burst-costs.xlsx

title: PrimeFare: sustained token backlog & reserved throughput gap — exec + runbook

Cc: finance@crestpoint.co
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 08:41:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Bank/Ship-to change for Redwood PO# 78421

I reviewed the signed bank change form; it's mostly complete but the form is missing the printed name of the bank signatory on page 2 and a company stamp we usually require for an ACH routing change. Priya, is AP able to accept the doc as-is if we get an email from the CFO confirming the account? If not, we'll request the missing fields and ask them to re-sign.

Operational asks: 
- Can procurement update the ship-to on PO 78421 now, or do we need procurement change control approval? (If the vendor change affects freight terms we may need a quick ops signoff.)
- If AP needs a notarized replacement, Samir, can you provide that or a confirmation from your bank?

If procurement can do the PO address change, please do so and tag the ticket to K-78421 so I can coordinate the wire routing after the document set is finalized.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Manager, Vendor Payments | Redwood Inference
From: Samir Patel <samir.patel@crestpoint.co>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Crestpoint Logistics Billing <finance@crestpoint.co>
Date: Mon, 29 Nov 2026 16:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Bank/Ship-to change for Redwood PO# 78421

Hi Karthik / Irene — thanks for the review and sorry for the missing field. I've attached an updated bank_change_form that includes the printed name and title for the signatory, plus a scanned bank letter confirming the routing and account ending in 4321. We've also included our company stamp.

To be explicit: Crestpoint confirms the effective date is 2026-12-01 and that billing@crestpoint.co is the AP contact for future invoices. If procurement can patch the ship-to on PO 78421 to the Crestpoint Crossdock #4 address, we will update our routing with carriers this afternoon.

Samir Patel

Priya, is AP able to accept the doc as-is if we get an email from the CFO confirming the account? If not, we'll request the missing fields and ask them to re-sign.

Operational asks: 
- Can procurement update the ship-to on PO 78421 now, or do we need procurement change control approval? (If the vendor change affects freight terms we may need a quick ops signoff.)
- If AP needs a notarized replacement, Samir, can you provide that or a confirmation from your bank?

If procurement can do the PO address change, please do so and tag the ticket to K-78421 so I can coordinate the wire routing after the document set is finalized.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Manager, Vendor Payments | Redwood Inference

Thanks again for signing — really excited to kick this off. Attached is a first-pass activation charter and a lightweight mutual action plan (MAP) that lays out the sprint-0 scope, acceptance criteria, and owner suggestions. Key things to call out up-front:

- Goal: pilot Redwood inference for the EHR summarization flow (internal-facing, HIPAA consented) with a goal to hit 95% reliability under the agreed latency SLO during pilot.
- Scope for sprint-0 (2 weeks): network & VPC connectivity validation, model selection + baseline perf run, and a first integration via our hosted API.
- Acceptance: successful tokenized generation at test rate (see MAP tab "acceptance") + infra sign-off from Crossroads infra.

Please confirm that the network contacts below are correct and that the proposed dates in the MAP work for you. I’ve CC’d Priya (implementation lead) and Lauren (CS manager) from our side — they’ll take over the detailed scheduling and runbook creation once we align on the sprint-0 window.

source.subject: Clarify non-learning warranty, ephemeral retention, and erase proof
messages:
From: Aisha Kapoor <aisha.kapoor@finedge.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: FinEdge Legal <legal@finedge.com>
Date: 2026-11-20T15:07:00-05:00
Subject: Question: Do you ever use our data for model training? Clarify retention windows

Connor —

Thanks for the demo yesterday. Before we move forward with a pilot we need a clear written answer for our security team and procurement: does Redwood ever use customer request/response or telemetry data to train models (including offline training, fine-tuning, or aggregate analytics pipelines)?

Specifically we need:
- A plain-English statement we can put in our contract (short one-liner).
- Retention timelines for the different categories (message payloads, telemetry/metrics, debug snapshots, backups).
- Whether ephemeral caches (KV cache, prefix cache) are ever persisted to backups or included in logs that could be used for training.
- The deletion/eviction process if we request erasure and the approximate time-to-completion.

If you have a short playbook or checklist we can attach to our security questionnaire that would be ideal. The legal team is expecting us to produce evidence (audit extract or log) showing deletion when exercised.

Thanks,
Aisha Kapoor
Director, Security & Privacy
FinEdge

--
Attachment: sample-security-questionnaire.pdf (stub)

From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Kapoor <aisha.kapoor@finedge.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-21T09:34:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Question: Do you ever use our data for model training? Clarify retention windows

Hi Aisha —

Short answer for contract: “Redwood Inference does not use customer-provided content for model training unless explicitly agreed in writing.”

Expanded details below (will follow up with a playbook and a short attestation):

1) Scope — what we mean by "not used for training":
- We do not route production request/response pairs, customer prompts, or derived outputs into our model training/fine-tuning pipelines for hosted customers by default.
- Telemetry used for service health (latency/error rates, aggregated metrics) is aggregated and anonymized; it is not material used for training model weights.

2) Retention categories & typical TTLs (operational defaults):
- Request/response payloads: retained by default for 30 days for troubleshooting and replay; can be shortened to customer-requested TTL via contract.
- KV/prefix caches: treated as ephemeral in-memory structures; KV cache snapshots are not persisted to long-term backups in normal operation. If a snapshot is taken for debugging, it is stored encrypted, limited access, and expires within 7 days unless escalation occurs.
- Debug snapshots & support traces: retained for up to 90 days when created for incident investigation; we can tombstone those earlier on request.
- Backups & snapshots of underlying volumes: standard backup retention is 90 days; we segregate customer namespaces and provide deletion evidence when required.

3) Guarantees & controls:
- No-training flag: Customers are given a tenant-level flag that prevents any routing of their data into training or research pipelines. This flag is enforced at the ingestion layer and independently audited.
- KMS-bound encryption: customer data is encrypted at rest with KMS; keys for private deployments are customer-managed.
- Access controls: debug snapshots require break-glass and SRE approval; we maintain an approval log.

4) Erasure process (how we delete on request):
- Request intake -> scope confirmation (24 business hours) -> execution: eviction from live stores and caches (within 48 hours) -> delete from hot backups & tombstone long-term snapshots (target 7–30 days depending on backup cycle) -> provide signed audit extract confirming operations (within 10 business days).
- Edge cases: if data has been exported to a customer-managed region or long-term archival (with customer agreement), deletion timing depends on that store's lifecycle.

We maintain a short playbook and an attestation PDF that we can sign (attachment: redwood-erase-playbook-v2.pdf). I’ve cc'd Priya (product) and Mina (security) to validate the wording and ensure we can produce the audit artifacts you requested.

Connor
Head of Customer Solutions
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment: redwood-erase-playbook-v2.pdf (stub)

From: Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Kapoor <aisha.kapoor@finedge.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-25T11:02:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Question: Do you ever use our data for model training? Clarify retention windows

Hi Aisha —

Adding a few operational clarifications from security/compliance: 

- Evidence we can deliver:
  * Signed non-training attestation (DocuSign) tied to your account ID.
  * Extracted audit log entries showing the tenant-level "no-train" flag state (timestamped).
  * Redacted S3 lifecycle report and backup-delete job logs for the deletion window you exercise.

- For SOC2 / third-party review requests we can provide:
  * A scope-limited log extract and an executive summary of controls — we cannot share internal hostnames or PII beyond the scope.

- A few pragmatic notes on retention limits:
  * While we default to 30 days for payloads, we can reduce that to 0 days (no persistence) for the pilot environment with configuration changes and an SLA addendum.
  * Snapshot deletion is gated by the backup schedule; we will schedule an out-of-cycle deletion if you need faster purge (we own the change request and provide confirmation).

Action items I propose:
- Connor: share the short attestation and playbook (we already have the draft attached).
- Aisha/Rajesh: tell us which evidence items you need for procurement and a target erasure SL (e.g., 48 hours / 7 days).
- Priya (product): scope the config change to set payload TTL=0 for the pilot and confirm any downstream operational impacts.

Regards,
Mina Haddad
Senior Security PM
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment: retention-matrix-2026.xlsx (stub); redwood-data-flow-diagram.png (stub)

From: Aisha Kapoor <aisha.kapoor@finedge.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: Rajesh Patel <rajesh.patel@rajesh.patel@finedge.com>, FinEdge Legal <legal@finedge.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-28T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Question: Do you ever use our data for model training? Clarify retention windows

Connor / Mina —

title: Clarify non-learning warranty, ephemeral retention, and erase proof
preview:
From: Aisha Kapoor <aisha.kapoor@finedge.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: FinEdge Legal <legal@finedge.com>
Date: 2026-11-20T15:07:00-05:00
Subject: Question: Do you ever use our data for model training? Clarify retention windows

Connor —

Thanks for the demo yesterday. Before we move forward with a pilot we need a clear written answer for our security team and procurement: does Redwood ever use customer request/response or telemetry data to train models (including offline training, fine-tuning, or aggregate analytics pipelines)?

Specifically we need:
- A plain-English statement we can put in our contract (short one-liner).
- Retention timelines for the different categories (message payloads, telemetry/metrics, debug snapshots, backups).
- Whether ephemeral caches (KV cache, prefix cache) are ever persisted to backups or included in logs that could be used for training.
- The deletion/eviction process if we request erasure and the approximate time-to-completion.

If you have a short playbook or checklist we can attach to our security questionnaire that would be ideal. The legal team is expecting us to produce evidence (audit extract or log) showing deletion when exercised.

Thanks,
Aisha Kapoor
Director, Security & Privacy
FinEdge

--
Attachment: sample-security-questionnaire.pdf (stub)

From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Kapoor <aisha.kapoor@finedge.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-21T09:34:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Question: Do you ever use our data for model training? Clarify retention windows

Hi Aisha —

Short answer for contract: “Redwood Inference does not use customer-provided content for model training unless explicitly agreed in writing.”

Expanded details below (will follow up with a playbook and a short attestation):

1) Scope — what we mean by \"not used for training\":
- We do not route production request/response pairs, customer prompts, or derived outputs into our model training/fine-tuning pipelines for hosted customers by default.
- Telemetry used for service health (latency/error rates, aggregated metrics) is aggregated and anonymized; it is not material used for training model weights.

2) Retention categories & typical TTLs (operational defaults):
- Request/response payloads: retained by default for 30 days for troubleshooting and replay; can be shortened to customer-requested TTL via contract.
- KV/prefix caches: treated as ephemeral in-memory structures; KV cache snapshots are not persisted to long-term backups in normal operation. If a snapshot is taken for debugging, it is stored encrypted, limited access, and expires within 7 days unless escalation occurs.
- Debug snapshots & support traces: retained for up to 90 days when created for incident investigation; we can tombstone those earlier on request.
- Backups & snapshots of underlying volumes: standard backup retention is 90 days; we segregate customer namespaces and provide deletion evidence when required.

3) Guarantees & controls:
- No-training flag: Customers are given a tenant-level flag that prevents any routing of their data into training or research pipelines. This flag is enforced at the ingestion layer and independently audited.
- KMS-bound encryption: customer data is encrypted at rest with KMS; keys for private deployments are customer-managed.
- Access controls: debug snapshots require break-glass and SRE approval; we maintain an approval log.

4) Erasure process (how we delete on request):
- Request intake -> scope confirmation (24 business hours) -> execution: eviction from live stores and caches (within 48 hours) -> delete from hot backups & tombstone long-term snapshots (target 7–30 days depending on backup cycle) -> provide signed audit extract confirming operations (within 10 business days).
- Edge cases: if data has been exported to a customer-managed region or long-term archival (with customer agreement), deletion timing depends on that store's lifecycle.

We maintain a short playbook and an attestation PDF that we can sign (attachment: redwood-erase-playbook-v2.pdf). I’ve cc'd Priya (product) and Mina (security) to validate the wording and ensure we can produce the audit artifacts you requested.

Connor
Head of Customer Solutions
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment: redwood-erase-playbook-v2.pdf (stub)


From: Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Kapoor <aisha.kapoor@finedge.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-25T11:02:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Question: Do you ever use our data for model training? Clarify retention windows

Hi Aisha —

Adding a few operational clarifications from security/compliance: 

- Evidence we can deliver:
  * Signed non-training attestation (DocuSign) tied to your account ID.
  * Extracted audit log entries showing the tenant-level "no-train" flag state (timestamped).
  * Redacted S3 lifecycle report and backup-delete job logs for the deletion window you exercise.

- For SOC2 / third-party review requests we can provide:
  * A scope-limited log extract and an executive summary of controls — we cannot share internal hostnames or PII beyond the scope.

- A few pragmatic notes on retention limits:
  * While we default to 30 days for payloads, we can reduce that to 0 days (no persistence) for the pilot environment with configuration changes and an SLA addendum.
  * Snapshot deletion is gated by the backup schedule; we will schedule an out-of-cycle deletion if you need faster purge (we own the change request and provide confirmation).

Action items I propose:
- Connor: share the short attestation and playbook (we already have the draft attached).
- Aisha/Rajesh: tell us which evidence items you need for procurement and a target erasure SL (e.g., 48 hours / 7 days).
- Priya (product): scope the config change to set payload TTL=0 for the pilot and confirm any downstream operational impacts.

Regards,
Mina Haddad
Senior Security PM
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment: retention-matrix-2026.xlsx (stub); redwood-data-flow-diagram.png (stub)

source.subject: Activation gating for AWS Private Offers: buyer banding & subscription tiers
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: 2026-11-25T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Team —

Following the call this morning with AcmeCo and Carlos from AWS, I sketched a lightweight banding approach we can use across private offers to accelerate buyer approvals and co-sell activation. Attached: po_banding_matrix_v1.xlsx and a short activation playbook draft.

Key proposal (high-level):
- Band A: <$100k ARR equivalent, auto-approval via Seller Ops + one finance item (invoice cadence).
- Band B: $100k–$500k, requires seller manager + finance quick review (24–48h) and AWS partner signoff for co-sell.
- Band C: >$500k, full procurement evidence pack + legal/finance signoff and DocuSign.

Questions I want us to decide on in the next 48 hours:
1) Exact thresholds (USD / ARR equivalent) — are the breakpoints above reasonable?
2) Minimal evidence per band (what must live in the HubSpot deal to trigger activation).
3) Owner for automated gating (PR/Partner Ops vs Seller Ops) and whether we route to finance first for Band B.

Action items: please review the attached matrix and the playbook. Vanessa, can you confirm AWS co-sell expectations? Marcus, can you own the gating automation checklist? Priya, can you draft the evidence template for finance?

Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachments: po_banding_matrix_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), aws_activation_playbook_draft.pdf (pdf)

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-25T11:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Ben — great framing. A few notes from AWS partner side after syncing with Carlos: 
- AWS wants a clear activation SLA for co-sell lead assignment — recommend 72h for Band A/B to assign partner rep and 5 business days for Band C.
- For Band B, a one-pager (commercial summary + contact list) + confirmation of buyer identity is usually sufficient for initial co-sell allocation.
- Band C will need the procurement packet (SCA, billing contact, PO window) before we push co-sell resources.

I'll loop Carlos into the next doc review. I like the automated gating idea; we should be careful not to increase friction for high-velocity SMB deals.

Vanessa Ortiz
Global Marketplace Lead | Redwood Inference
vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-25T13:20:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Thanks — reviewed the matrix. Technical notes for gating automation:
- HubSpot deal properties we can use as triggers: "Estimated ARR", "PO Expected Value", "Primary Billing Contact", "Procurement Contact Email".
- For Band A, implement an automated workflow that sets "PO Activation Ready" when Estimated ARR < 100000 and Billing Contact verified.
- For Band B, add a two-step: auto-notify finance with one-click "Approve" in HubSpot that sets a 48h timer. If finance doesn't approve, escalate to Seller Ops.

I'll draft the checklist in Confluence and link it in the playbook — will aim to have the automation spec by Wednesday (2026-11-28).

Marcus Lin
Head of Seller Ops | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com

From: Lisa Tran <lisa.tran@acmeco.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-26T09:07:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Ben / Vanessa — thanks for the clear summary. A couple of quick points from AcmeCo procurement: 
- We prefer Band B behavior: we can move to a 12-month usage commitment with quarterly invoicing; finance will need a short evidence pack (signed SOW or summary email + procurement contact).
- For Band C (if we expand later) procurement will request our legal team to review SLA and data retention.

Happy to review the one-pager once you have it.

—Lisa
Lisa Tran
Head of Procurement | AcmeCo
lisa.tran@acmeco.com

-----Original Message-----
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Sent: Wednesday, November 25, 2026 9:12 AM
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>; Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>; Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Subject: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

(Quoted above)

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-26T11:02:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Thanks, all. Draft evidence template for finance attached (I pasted it into the activation playbook and updated the spreadsheet):
- Required for Band A: billing contact + signed order email.
- Required for Band B: one-pager, procurement contact, billing cadence, summary SOW or summary email, CFO name.
- Required for Band C: standard evidence pack (SCA, legal review summary, PO term sheet).

I'll put the template in HubSpot as a required checklist item so workflow can verify presence of fields before flagging "PO Activation Ready".

Priya Natarajan
Finance Ops Manager | Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Lisa Tran <lisa.tran@acmeco.com>, Carlos Mendes <carlos.mendes@aws-partner.amazon.com>
Date: 2026-11-27T15:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Quick status update and next steps: 
- Marcus will publish the gating automation spec by 2026-11-28.
- Priya has added the finance checklist to the playbook (see attached pdf).
- Vanessa / Carlos: please confirm the 72h SLA for Band A/B assignment; if OK we will add it to the HubSpot SLA automation and communicate to AWS.
- Lisa: when you have a minute, would you confirm that a one-pager + confirmation email will be sufficient for your procurement team for Band B?

If we align on these items, I'll push the HubSpot workflow and create the internal Seller Ops runbook for onboarding private offers.

Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

Attachments: aws_activation_playbook_draft.pdf

text:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: 2026-11-25T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Team —

Following the call this morning with AcmeCo and Carlos from AWS, I sketched a lightweight banding approach we can use across private offers to accelerate buyer approvals and co-sell activation. Attached: po_banding_matrix_v1.xlsx and a short activation playbook draft.

Key proposal (high-level):
- Band A: <$100k ARR equivalent, auto-approval via Seller Ops + one finance item (invoice cadence).
- Band B: $100k–$500k, requires seller manager + finance quick review (24–48h) and AWS partner signoff for co-sell.
- Band C: >$500k, full procurement evidence pack + legal/finance signoff and DocuSign.

Questions I want us to decide on in the next 48 hours:
1) Exact thresholds (USD / ARR equivalent) — are the breakpoints above reasonable?
2) Minimal evidence per band (what must live in the HubSpot deal to trigger activation).
3) Owner for automated gating (PR/Partner Ops vs Seller Ops) and whether we route to finance first for Band B.

Action items: please review the attached matrix and the playbook. Vanessa, can you confirm AWS co-sell expectations? Marcus, can you own the gating automation checklist? Priya, can you draft the evidence template for finance?

Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachments: po_banding_matrix_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), aws_activation_playbook_draft.pdf (pdf)


From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-25T11:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Ben — great framing. A few notes from AWS partner side after syncing with Carlos: 
- AWS wants a clear activation SLA for co-sell lead assignment — recommend 72h for Band A/B to assign partner rep and 5 business days for Band C.
- For Band B, a one-pager (commercial summary + contact list) + confirmation of buyer identity is usually sufficient for initial co-sell allocation.
- Band C will need the procurement packet (SCA, billing contact, PO window) before we push co-sell resources.

I'll loop Carlos into the next doc review. I like the automated gating idea; we should be careful not to increase friction for high-velocity SMB deals.

Vanessa Ortiz
Global Marketplace Lead | Redwood Inference
vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-25T13:20:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Thanks — reviewed the matrix. Technical notes for gating automation:
- HubSpot deal properties we can use as triggers: \"Estimated ARR\", \"PO Expected Value\", \"Primary Billing Contact\", \"Procurement Contact Email\".
- For Band A, implement an automated workflow that sets \"PO Activation Ready\" when Estimated ARR < 100000 and Billing Contact verified.
- For Band B, add a two-step: auto-notify finance with one-click \"Approve\" in HubSpot that sets a 48h timer. If finance doesn't approve, escalate to Seller Ops.

I'll draft the checklist in Confluence and link it in the playbook — will aim to have the automation spec by Wednesday (2026-11-28).

Marcus Lin
Head of Seller Ops | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com


From: Lisa Tran <lisa.tran@acmeco.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-26T09:07:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Ben / Vanessa — thanks for the clear summary. A couple of quick points from AcmeCo procurement: 
- We prefer Band B behavior: we can move to a 12-month usage commitment with quarterly invoicing; finance will need a short evidence pack (signed SOW or summary email + procurement contact).
- For Band C (if we expand later) procurement will request our legal team to review SLA and data retention.

Happy to review the one-pager once you have it.

—Lisa
Lisa Tran

source.subject: Reserved capacity pricing sensitivity and approvals for NimbusAI Q4 commit
messages:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Sara Cho <sara.cho@nimbusai.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Initial sizing & pricing sensitivity for reserved commit (DEAL-7892)

Sara —

Thanks again for the time today. Per our discussion, attached is a first-pass capacity model (nimbus_capacity_model_v3.xlsx) and a short options deck (redwood_commit_term_options.pdf) that outline 3 commitment tenors we typically offer and how they map to GPU unit economics.

Key asks from our side to finalize an internal approval:
- Confirm expected Q1 sustained concurrent throughput (vGPU-hours/day) used in the model
- Confirm whether you need an on-prem or VPC private deployment option (this affects isolation premium)
- Indicate target commit tenor (12 / 24 / 36 months) and any flexibility on minimum monthly spend

High level: for a 24-month commit at the sizing in the attached, the headline discount to list is ~28% vs on-demand. That assumes current GPU ASPs. If GPU market pricing (spot/contract) moves >15% vs our forecast, we will need a pricing re-opener or banded adjustments — I included a sensitivity matrix (pricing_sensitivity_matrix.csv).

Looping in Karthik and Marcus for the detailed discount/approval path.

Thanks,
Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
---
Attachment stubs: nimbus_capacity_model_v3.xlsx (spreadsheet), redwood_commit_term_options.pdf (pdf), pricing_sensitivity_matrix.csv (csv)

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Sara Cho <sara.cho@nimbusai.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Initial sizing & pricing sensitivity for reserved commit (DEAL-7892)

Folks —

I reviewed the attached model. Two things to call out from finance/ops perspective:

1) Sensitivity buckets (from pricing_sensitivity_matrix.csv)
   - Base case (0–5% GPU price movement): economics hold; approval can be delegated to Sales Director (> $300k ARR)
   - Moderate movement (5–15%): requires VP Finance sign-off and a 90-day price cadence review clause in the contract
   - Significant movement (>15%): needs Exec Committee approval and either a price-pass-through clause or an indexation mechanism tied to our GPU supplier benchmark

2) Term vs. discount tradeoffs (rough):
   - 12-month: ~18% discount at that sizing; minimal approval friction
   - 24-month: ~28% discount but requires VP Finance approval if not capped by a market-movement clause
   - 36-month: ~33–36% discount, higher capex exposure for Redwood if GPU prices increase; Exec approval likely

Quick recommendation: propose a 24-month commit with a 5–12% cap band on GPU supplier variance (indexation) and a mutualtermination clause if GPU costs exceed +20% for 3 consecutive months — that reduces Redwood downside while giving Nimbus a predictable price.

I'll draft a short approval memo if you want to route to VP Finance.

—Marcus
Marcus Lin, Head of Finance — Dedicated Capacity
Redwood Inference

Attachment note: Marcus references an internal approval memo template but none attached in this thread.

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Cho <sara.cho@nimbusai.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 15:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Initial sizing & pricing sensitivity for reserved commit (DEAL-7892)

Team / Sara —

Thanks Marcus, that aligns with what I expected. A couple of practical notes from Sales/legal:
- For deals that cross the VP Finance threshold, we should include a standard 6–8 week review/approval window in the mutual action plan so Nimbus knows timing implications.
- Discounts beyond the standard tier will need a Director Sales approval + written sign-off from Sales Ops. I can cover Director approval for up to 30% off list for a 24-month if we get the indexation clause in place.
- If Nimbus prefers a fixed-dollar cap rather than indexation, we can model a blended floor/ceiling and include a GPU-market-contingent escrow arrangement (we've done this with two other recent customers).

Next steps I suggest we document in a short MAP: sizing confirmation, desired term, legal clause option (indexation vs fixed cap), and the internal approval owners + ETA. Jordan — can you update the MAP and share?

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer, Senior Sales Manager
Redwood Inference

From: Sara Cho <sara.cho@nimbusai.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: Aaron Feld <aaron@cloudbroker.io>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 08:21:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Initial sizing & pricing sensitivity for reserved commit (DEAL-7892)

Jordan / Karthik / Marcus —

Thanks for the model and the clear options. A few clarifying Qs from Nimbus procurement:
1) Can you show the delta to list broken out by GPU family? Nimbus wants to see NVL48 vs A100-equivalent buckets separately.
2) For the indexation clause, will Redwood accept a +/− band tied to the GPU supplier index with quarterly resets? We prefer fixed quarterly cadence.
3) On the mutual termination: what does Redwood consider "cost pass-through" operationally? We need a simple definition in the contract so the legal team can sign off quickly.

We are leaning toward a 24-month commit but need those clarifications before we present internally. Also copying our channel partner (CloudBroker) to coordinate any multi-cloud routing requirements.

Sara Cho
Head of Procurement, NimbusAI
procurement@nimbusai.com

Attachment: Nimbus provided an internal schedule doc (not attached here)

From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Sara Cho <sara.cho@nimbusai.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 09:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Initial sizing & pricing sensitivity for reserved commit (DEAL-7892)

Sara —

Great questions. Quick answers and next steps below:

1) Delta by GPU family: I attached a breakout sheet in the model (tab: "gpu_family_delta"). We'll export a separate PDF table and send it today (expected by EOD PST).
2) Indexation mechanics: Redwood is comfortable with a quarterly reset band tied to our GPU supplier benchmark. Marcus, per your buckets can we make the band +/- 10% for quarterly resets and a 20% trigger for executive review?
3) Cost pass-through definition: propose this language for the MAP and later contract: "Cost Pass-Through: If Redwood's direct GPU procurement cost (as measured against Redwood's selected supplier benchmark) increases by >20% for three consecutive months, Redwood may: (a) propose a proportional price adjustment to the customer; or (b) offer equivalent capacity substitution or a one-time credit to mitigate impact. Any adjustment will require VP Finance notification and 30 days' notice to the customer."

Action items: I will —
- Export the GPU-family delta PDF for Nimbus (EOD PST today)
- Update the MAP: sizing confirmation, term = 24 months (pending Nimbus sign-off), clause option = quarterly indexation band +/-10% with 20% exec trigger, approval owners and expected timelines

If that sounds good I'll queue the VP Finance memo for Marcus to sign and the Director Sales approval path for Karthik.

Thanks,
Jordan
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 415 555 0123
---
Attachments referenced: exported_gpu_family_delta.pdf (will be sent), updated_MAP_v2.docx (will be uploaded to Drive)
