- If Nimbus prefers a fixed-dollar cap rather than indexation, we can model a blended floor/ceiling and include a GPU-market-contingent escrow arrangement (we've done this with two other recent customers).

Next steps I suggest we document in a short MAP: sizing confirmation, desired term, legal clause option (indexation vs fixed cap), and the internal approval owners + ETA. Jordan — can you update the MAP and share?

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer, Senior Sales Manager
Redwood Inference

From: Sara Cho <sara.cho@nimbusai.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: Aaron Feld <aaron@cloudbroker.io>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 08:21:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Initial sizing & pricing sensitivity for reserved commit (DEAL-7892)

Jordan / Karthik / Marcus —

Thanks for the model and the clear options. A few clarifying Qs from Nimbus procurement:
1) Can you show the delta to list broken out by GPU family? Nimbus wants to see NVL48 vs A100-equivalent buckets separately.
2) For the indexation clause, will Redwood accept a +/− band tied to the GPU supplier index with quarterly resets? We prefer fixed quarterly cadence.
3) On the mutual termination: what does Redwood consider "cost pass-through" operationally? We need a simple definition in the contract so the legal team can sign off quickly.

We are leaning toward a 24-month commit but need those clarifications before we present internally. Also copying our channel partner (CloudBroker) to coordinate any multi-cloud routing requirements.

Sara Cho
Head of Procurement, NimbusAI
procurement@nimbusai.com

Attachment: Nimbus provided an internal schedule doc (not attached here)

From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Sara Cho <sara.cho@nimbusai.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 09:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Initial sizing & pricing sensitivity for reserved commit (DEAL-7892)

Sara —

Great questions. Quick answers and next steps below:

1) Delta by GPU family: I attached a breakout sheet in the model (tab: "gpu_family_delta"). We'll export a separate PDF table and send it today (expected by EOD PST).
2) Indexation mechanics: Redwood is comfortable with a quarterly reset band tied to our GPU supplier benchmark. Marcus, per your buckets can we make the band +/- 10% for quarterly resets and a 20% trigger for executive review?
3) Cost pass-through definition: propose this language for the MAP and later contract: "Cost Pass-Through: If Redwood's direct GPU procurement cost (as measured against Redwood's selected supplier benchmark) increases by >20% for three consecutive months, Redwood may: (a) propose a proportional price adjustment to the customer; or (b) offer equivalent capacity substitution or a one-time credit to mitigate impact. Any adjustment will require VP Finance notification and 30 days' notice to the customer."

Action items: I will —
- Export the GPU-family delta PDF for Nimbus (EOD PST today)
- Update the MAP: sizing confirmation, term = 24 months (pending Nimbus sign-off), clause option = quarterly indexation band +/-10% with 20% exec trigger, approval owners and expected timelines

If that sounds good I'll queue the VP Finance memo for Marcus to sign and the Director Sales approval path for Karthik.

Thanks,
Jordan
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 415 555 0123
---
Attachments referenced: exported_gpu_family_delta.pdf (will be sent), updated_MAP_v2.docx (will be uploaded to Drive)

source.subject: Dependent care: part-time return schedule for Priya Natarajan
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 09:14:00 -0800
Subject: Dependent care: proposed part-time RTW for Priya
Attachments: medical_note_priya_20261117.pdf; proposed_flex_schedule.xlsx

Hi Monica and Laura,

Following up on Priya's parental leave wrap-up — Priya is planning to return to work the week of Dec 7 but needs a temporary dependent-care accommodation for the first 6 weeks. Attached is a medical note (requested by Benefits) and a proposed part-time schedule that Priya and I sketched (proposed_flex_schedule.xlsx).

High-level ask:
- Approve a 60% FTE schedule (Mon/Tue/Wed mornings + Friday remote mornings) for Dec 7–Jan 15 as a trial.
- Confirm payroll/benefits implications and timing for HR to set the reduced hours.
- Share any required forms (flex agreement, updated timecard expectations).

Laura — I want to ensure coverage plan is workable; I proposed that Aaron picks up two recurring tasks and we reassign the weekly status meeting to Thurs when Priya is remote. I’m happy to own the transition and weekly check-ins.

Monica — can Benefits confirm whether this needs a formal accommodation plan vs. standard parental transition? Also tagging Benefits team is fine if you want them on the thread.

Thanks —
Ben

Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com
(415) 555-0187

---
Forwarded attachment stubs: medical_note_priya_20261117.pdf (PDF, 2 pages)
From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Benefits Team <benefits@healthease.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 12:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Dependent care: proposed part-time RTW for Priya
Attachments: flex_agreement_template.docx

Thanks for the context, Ben — and hi Priya.

We can support a temporary reduced schedule as a dependent-care transition; this would be treated as a temporary flexible work arrangement rather than a formal ADA accommodation based on the documentation provided. A couple of next steps from HR/Benefits: 

1) Please review the attached flex agreement template and confirm changes you want (Priya). Once you sign, Benefits will enter the adjusted FTE into payroll effective the start date.
2) Benefits needs the exact start date and whether Priya expects any intermittent schedule changes (e.g., doctor visits) so we can set time-off codes.
3) Payroll implications: a 60% FTE will pro-rate salary and benefits contributions for the period. Benefits team — please confirm whether parental leave pay-top up impacts the first pay period back.

We typically do a 4-week trial for schedule changes, then review and either extend or transition back. Given Priya's role, I agree a 6-week trial is reasonable if the manager signs off on coverage.

Available times for a quick 20-min sync: Thu 11/19 10:30am or Mon 11/23 2:00pm. I can invite Benefits if helpful.

Regards,
Monica Patel
People Operations Partner, Redwood Inference
monica@redwood.com

---
Attachment stub: flex_agreement_template.docx
From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 09:07:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Dependent care: proposed part-time RTW for Priya

Thanks — and welcome back in advance, Priya.

I reviewed the proposed schedule and the coverage plan Ben suggested looks solid. Operational notes: 
- Aaron can take ownership of the weekly sync and the pipeline cleanup items. I’ll pair him with Ben for two weeks after Priya returns to transfer context.
- We’ll move the client-facing handoffs to Thurs afternoon when Priya is remote to avoid overlap on high-priority mornings.
- I support a 6-week trial starting Dec 7, with a check-in the week of Dec 28 to evaluate capacity and any blockers.

Priya — if that works for you, please confirm the exact date you expect to start and sign Monica's template. I’m comfortable endorsing this temporarily and will provide written sign-off once HR updates payroll.

Also, Priya, if you anticipate needing any intermittent time off for doctor/childcare appointments, flag them now so we can set expectations with the team.

Best,
Laura
Laura Bennett
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference
laura@redwood.com

---
On Wed, Nov 18, 2026 at 12:02 PM Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com> wrote:
> We can support a temporary reduced schedule as a dependent-care transition...
From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 15:06:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Dependent care: proposed part-time RTW for Priya
Attachments: signed_flex_agreement_priya_20261124.pdf

Hi all — thank you for coordinating and for the support. I reviewed the template and signed (attached signed_flex_agreement_priya_20261124.pdf). I confirm the trial 60% FTE schedule starting Dec 7, 2026 and will plan for the Dec 28 check-in.

I may need to step away for two afternoons in the first two weeks for pediatric appointments — I’ll give as much notice as possible and will coordinate asynchronous handoffs for any client items. Happy to join the sync Mon 11/23 2pm if we want to finalize admin items.

Appreciate the flexibility —
Priya
Priya Natarajan
Product Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
priya@redwood.com

---
Quoted: 
On Thu, Nov 19, 2026 at 09:07 AM Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com> wrote:
> I reviewed the proposed schedule and the coverage plan Ben suggested looks solid...

source.subject: MAP Kickoff: AWS Marketplace Listing — Roles & Timeline
messages:
From: Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>
To: Lara Costa <lara.costa@optimacloud.io>, Alicia Gomez <agomez@amazon.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: MAP Kickoff: AWS Marketplace Listing — Roles & Timeline

Hi Lara / Alicia —

Thanks again for agreeing to run the marketplace listing with us. To make sure we keep the procurement and technical eval on schedule, I put together a short Mutual Action Plan (MAP) outline and attached an evaluation matrix and the AWS marketplace checklist.

High-level goal: ship OptimaCloud listing (Redwood inference integration) to AWS Marketplace, go-live target: 2027-01-15.

Proposed milestones (rough):
- Week 1 (Nov 20–27): Kickoff, intake forms, access & account IDs
- Week 2 (Nov 30–Dec 4): Security/compliance questionnaire (SOC2 evidence), technical review
- Week 3 (Dec 7–11): AWS listing package prep, legal & pricing alignment
- Week 4–5 (Dec 14–23): AWS listing submission & review cycle
- Jan 2027: Final approval & go-live prep, marketplace listing published

Role suggestions (please confirm or update):
- Redwood: Diego (MAP owner / primary ops), Marissa (AWS submission lead), Priya (security owner), Marcus (runtime/perf lead), Rishi (commercial/pricing).
- OptimaCloud: Lara (partner lead), Samir (partner engineering) — please add others.
- AWS: Alicia (Marketplace onboarding).

Immediate asks: 
1) Lara — can you share OptimaCloud’s AWS seller account ID and the primary partner ops contact?
2) Alicia — any specific gating items we should surface up-front for the accelerated timeline?
3) Team (Marissa / Priya) — I’ve attached the evaluation matrix — please flag missing items and own the checklist rows you’ll take.

I propose a 30-minute weekly sync starting next Tue at 10:00 PT to keep momentum — if that works for everyone, I’ll send a calendar invite.

Attachments: optima_evaluation_matrix.xlsx; aws_checklist.pdf

Thanks —
Diego
Diego Fernandez
Senior Partner Solutions Manager | Redwood Inference
From: Lara Costa <lara.costa@optimacloud.io>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.patel@optimacloud.io>, Alicia Gomez <agomez@amazon.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: MAP Kickoff: AWS Marketplace Listing — Roles & Timeline

Thanks Diego — great to kick this off.

Confirming OptimaCloud will be the seller/ISV on the listing. Our AWS Seller ID: 987654321012. Primary partner ops: Samir Patel (cc’d). Our engineering lead for the integration will be Jordan Hughes (I’ve included Jordan here at his Acme Health email for visibility — Jordan is our early adopter point of contact).

We can meet Tuesdays at 10:00 PT. The proposed timeline looks reasonable but we’ll need a short buffer for legal redlines. We’ll start pulling together our SOC2 summary and an initial set of test prompts for the evaluation matrix.

Action items from OptimaCloud:
- Share pre-signed test credentials (Samir)
- Complete our side of the evaluation matrix by Wed (Nov 24)
- Designate pricing approver (I’ll confirm by EOD Monday)

Appreciate the clear plan — we’ll sync on Tuesday.

— Lara
Lara Costa
Head of Partnerships | OptimaCloud
From: Alicia Gomez <agomez@amazon.com>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>, Lara Costa <lara.costa@optimacloud.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 13:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: MAP Kickoff: AWS Marketplace Listing — Roles & Timeline

Hi all —

Thanks for the overview. A couple quick notes from Marketplace onboarding:
- Seller ID received (thanks, Lara). Please also provide the AWS account alias and the legal entity name associated with the Seller ID.
- Security artifacts we need up-front: SOC2 report (summary), data flow diagram, and a contact for the security questionnaire. If Redwood will host any integration components, please provide the contract/policy owner.
- Typical review cadence: once we have the submission package, expect 7–10 business days for initial review, then iterative changes. If you can get us the submission by Dec 16 we can target a Jan 6–12 approval window.

I’ll add the AWS marketplace checklist to the shared doc (link: https://aws.amazon.com/marketplace/onboarding/).

If anyone expects longer legal review cycles, flag now and we’ll adjust the MAP.

— Alicia
Alicia Gomez
Partner Onboarding Manager — AWS Marketplace
From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
Cc: Lara Costa <lara.costa@optimacloud.io>, Alicia Gomez <agomez@amazon.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 09:22:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: MAP Kickoff: AWS Marketplace Listing — Roles & Timeline

Quick ownership notes from Redwood side:
- Marissa Cole: AWS submission lead — I’ll own the seller portal upload and point-of-contact for AWS reviews.
- Priya Natarajan: Security & compliance — I’ll coordinate SOC2 artifacts and complete the security questionnaire.
- Marcus Lin: Runtime & performance — I’ll run the evaluation prompts and provide latency/cost profiles; will organize a perf smoke test with OptimaCloud.
- Rishi Malhotra: Commercials & pricing — I’ll draft the listing pricing and confirm the contract path (opt-in SaaS vs. AMI).

Marissa: I’ll send the calendar invite for Tuesdays at 10:00 PT (recurring for 6 weeks). Please add agenda: intake ID collection, security kickoff, perf plan, listing submission checklist.

Marissa
From: Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>
To: Lara Costa <lara.costa@optimacloud.io>, Alicia Gomez <agomez@amazon.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 15:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: MAP Kickoff: AWS Marketplace Listing — Roles & Timeline

Thanks everyone — calendar invite sent for Tuesdays 10:00 PT starting Nov 30 (30 min).

Immediate next steps (by EOD Wed Nov 24):
- Lara / Samir: share test credentials + confirm pricing approver (attachment: optima_evaluation_matrix.xlsx updated).
- Priya: begin security questionnaire draft and request SOC2 summary from OptimaCloud.
- Marcus: send perf test plan and an initial list of prompts from the evaluation matrix.
- Marissa: prepare portal access checklist and verify Seller ID details with AWS.
- Alicia: confirm expected review SLA for Dec submissions (noted 7–10 business days).

We’ll use the shared doc for tracking (link above) and update the MAP after this week’s sync.

Appreciate the quick turnaround — let’s keep it tight so we can hit the Jan target.

Best,
Diego

source.subject: Transparent terms: short customer snippet + expanded FAQ for data movement & subprocessors (hosted API / Console)
messages:
From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-21T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Short customer snippet + expanded FAQ for data movement & subprocessors

Priya — quick ask from the hosted API sales page workstream. We need two deliverables for the public Console/Hosted API privacy area: a micro snippet (40–60 words) that appears on the Hosted API signup flow, and an expanded FAQ (3–4 short Q&A items) to live on the Console privacy panel.

Key constraints:
- Keep language plain and customer-facing (no legalese).
- Call out subprocessors and where people can find the subprocessor list.
- State high-level data transfer practices and point to the DPA for details.
- Avoid promising anything our infra can't do (e.g., don’t say data stays only in the EU unless eligible accounts are called out).

Can you draft both variants by EOD today? Attached is a rough one-pager with background and examples (draft_snippets.docx). Marcus — tagging you for any performance or console copy constraints.

Thanks,
Laura
Laura Bennett
Director, Product Marketing
Redwood Inference
From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-21T11:30:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Short customer snippet + expanded FAQ for data movement & subprocessors

Laura — thanks, see drafts below. I kept two lengths and made the FAQ answer-focused (customers usually skim). I'm not a lawyer; Kim, can you review and flag anything that must be tighter?

--- Micro snippet (for signup / microcopy) ---
"Redwood processes requests to deliver models and features. We may engage trusted subprocessors (e.g., cloud providers) and transfer data to locations needed to serve requests. See our DPA and subprocessor list for details and controls — you can request region-restricted deployments if your contract requires it."

--- Expanded FAQ (Console privacy panel) ---
Q: Who processes my request data?
A: Redwood and our subprocessors (for example, cloud hosting providers and monitoring services) process request data to run the service and keep it reliable. You can view the full list of subprocessors here: https://redwood.example.com/subprocessors/list

Q: Will my data cross borders?
A: Depending on routing and capacity, request data may be processed outside your region to provide low latency and reliability. We use contractual safeguards (DPA clauses and standard contractual clauses where applicable) and technical measures to protect data in transit and at rest. See our DPA for details: https://redwood.example.com/legal/dpa-view

Q: Can I require data to remain in a specific country or region?
A: Yes — for eligible enterprise plans we offer region-restricted deployments. Speak with your sales/contact to confirm options and any additional controls required (e.g., Dedicated or Private deployments).

Q: How do you notify me about subprocessor changes?
A: We publish an up-to-date subprocessor list and provide notice for material changes. For contractual customers we can surface notice through the Console and via your account contact.

Attachments: draft_snippets.docx

Notes/questions for legal: - Should we replace language like "may be processed" with a stronger construction? - Do we need to list specific transfer mechanisms (SCCs, adequacy, etc.) in the FAQ?

Thanks,
Priya
Priya Natarajan
Senior Manager, Product Content
Redwood Inference
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-22T10:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Short customer snippet + expanded FAQ for data movement & subprocessors

Thanks for the clear drafts, Priya — these are close. A few legal redlines and guidance attached (redlines_privacy.docx). High-level comments: 
- Prefer first-person active voice: use "We may transfer" rather than passive constructions.
- For the micro snippet, avoid "transfer data to locations needed" — that could be read as unconstrained transfers. Suggest "transfer data to locations necessary to provide the service; we rely on contractual and technical safeguards."
- For the FAQ, include a single-sentence note about DPA availability ("A Data Processing Addendum is available and governs transfers and subprocessors for contractual customers") and point to where to request it.
- Please do not promise automatic region-only processing for Hosted in the microcopy. Use "region-restricted deployments are available on Dedicated/Private or by agreement."
- Confirm whether we should name specific transfer mechanisms (SCCs, UK Addendum). My preference is to reference "contractual safeguards (e.g., DPA and SCCs)" rather than enumerate them in the public FAQ.

If you make the edits above, legal is happy to sign off quickly. Also: can you add a short Console tooltip for the subprocessor link explaining what a subprocessor is (one sentence)?

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Security
Redwood Inference
From: Jenna Moore <jenna@acme.example.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-23T16:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Short customer snippet + expanded FAQ for data movement & subprocessors

Thanks for sharing these drafts — helpful. A couple of asks from Acme's security team: 
- Be explicit that transfers to the US may occur and confirm we use contractual safeguards (SCCs / adequacy where applicable).
- Can you surface the SOC 2 Type II report or a SOC 2 summary in the Console (or provide a link to request it)?
- We also want an easy way to download the current subprocessor list (CSV) from the Console.

If you can fold a short mention of SOC2 availability and the CSV subprocessor export into the FAQ answers, that would cover most of the team's immediate needs. Happy to review another round.

Best,
Jenna Moore
Head of Technical Procurement
Acme Corp

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-22T10:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Short customer snippet + expanded FAQ for data movement & subprocessors

Thanks for the clear drafts, Priya — these are close. A few legal redlines and guidance attached (redlines_privacy.docx). High-level comments: 
- Prefer first-person active voice: use "We may transfer" rather than passive constructions.
- For the micro snippet, avoid "transfer data to locations needed" — that could be read as unconstrained transfers. Suggest "transfer data to locations necessary to provide the service; we rely on contractual and technical safeguards."
- For the FAQ, include a single-sentence note about DPA availability ("A Data Processing Addendum is available and governs transfers and subprocessors for contractual customers") and point to where to request it.
- Please do not promise automatic region-only processing for Hosted in the microcopy. Use "region-restricted deployments are available on Dedicated/Private or by agreement."
- Confirm whether we should name specific transfer mechanisms (SCCs, UK Addendum). My preference is to reference "contractual safeguards (e.g., DPA and SCCs)" rather than enumerate them in the public FAQ.

If you make the edits above, legal is happy to sign off quickly. Also: can you add a short Console tooltip for the subprocessor link explaining what a subprocessor is (one sentence)?

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Security
Redwood Inference


From: Jenna Moore <jenna@acme.example.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-23T16:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Short customer snippet + expanded FAQ for data movement & subprocessors

Thanks for sharing these drafts — helpful. A couple of asks from Acme's security team: 
- Be explicit that transfers to the US may occur and confirm we use contractual safeguards (SCCs / adequacy where applicable).
- Can you surface the SOC 2 Type II report or a SOC 2 summary in the Console (or provide a link to request it)?
- We also want an easy way to download the current subprocessor list (CSV) from the Console.

If you can fold a short mention of SOC2 availability and the CSV subprocessor export into the FAQ answers, that would cover most of the team's immediate needs. Happy to review another round.

Best,
Jenna Moore
Head of Technical Procurement
Acme Corp

source.subject: CryptoEdge: tailored data destruction playbook & SIG evidence
messages:
From: Samantha Liu <samantha@cryptoedge.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Mark Reeves <procurement@cryptoedge.io>
Date: Sat, 21 Nov 2026 09:14:00 -0500
Subject: Request: tailored destruction playbook + SIG/CAIQ evidence for Dedicated tenant

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the product demo last week — CryptoEdge is excited about Redwood Private for our inference workloads. Before we move to a PO, the security team has two closely related asks we need to resolve: a tailored data destruction (proof-of-delete) playbook for tenant-scoped logs/artifacts, and the SIG/CAIQ evidence mappings that explain how that playbook meets standard control objectives.

Specifically we need:
- A documented playbook that describes the steps Redwood will take to delete tenant session logs, ephemeral KV cache snapshots, and any diagnostic dumps for a dedicated Private deployment (we run in a VPC with customer-managed KMS).
- A sample proof-of-delete export from a non-prod tenant that shows the notification manifest, deletion timestamps, and checksum evidence (PDF or CSV).
- SIG/CAIQ cells or attachments that tie those destruction steps to the relevant control IDs (data lifecycle, retention, and forensics).

To be clear: CryptoEdge requires the ability to demonstrate to our internal auditors that we can obtain customer-scoped proof-of-delete for logs older than 90 days and for ephemeral caches. We also want to confirm behavior when a legal hold is asserted (how deletion is paused and how evidence is preserved).

Attached is our SIG template (CryptoEdge_SIG_Request_20261121.pdf) — it highlights the cells we care about. Can your team confirm an expected deliverable list and an estimated timetable? Ideally we would like artifacts by next Wednesday (2026-11-24) if feasible.

Thanks,
Samantha Liu
Head of Security, CryptoEdge
Samantha.Liu@cryptoedge.io

--
Attachment: CryptoEdge_SIG_Request_20261121.pdf (application/pdf)
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Liu <samantha@cryptoedge.io>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>, Mark Reeves <procurement@cryptoedge.io>
Date: Sat, 21 Nov 2026 11:02:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Request: tailored destruction playbook + SIG/CAIQ evidence for Dedicated tenant

Samantha — thanks for the clear ask and the template. Looping in Priya (security lead for Private) and Neha (compliance/evidence) to scope deliverables and timeline.

Summary of what we will deliver (first pass):
1) A tailored "Tenant Destruction Playbook" describing steps for: identification of artifacts (logs, KV snapshots, diagnostic bundles), deletion orchestration, KMS key handling for objects, and manifest/checksum generation.
2) A sample proof-of-delete export from a staging tenant: CSV manifest + generated SHA256 checksums + deletion timestamps.
3) SIG/CAIQ mappings (we will populate the CryptoEdge template you attached) and callouts to SOC 2 procedures where relevant.

Clarifying questions so we scope correctly:
- Are you asking for proof-of-delete for all tenants globally, or only for the dedicated tenant we showed? (staging vs. production)
- Do you currently have any active legal holds or investigation flags that would prevent deletion for historical data?

Target timeline: Priya/Neha — can you confirm whether a 3-business-day turnaround for items (1)-(3) is realistic from your end? If not, we should propose a short meeting to prioritize cells in the SIG template.

I attached our working SIG responses workbook (redwood_sig_responses_v1.xlsx) — this is preliminary and will be updated.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

--
Attachment: redwood_sig_responses_v1.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)
From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Liu <samantha@cryptoedge.io>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 09:28:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: tailored destruction playbook + SIG/CAIQ evidence for Dedicated tenant

Thanks for the context, Samantha — and thanks Ben for looping us in. Quick runbook / proposed approach below; I copied the parts we will need from your template into action items.

High-level approach (what we'll produce):
- Playbook doc (PDF + editable Google Doc): scope, roles/responsibilities, triggers for deletion (age-based, customer request, on-termination), legal-hold overrides, verification steps, and recovery/retention exceptions. Will include specific steps for tenant-scoped Redwood Private artifacts (session logs, KV cache snapshots, model-serving debug bundles).
- Proof-of-delete export: a structured manifest (CSV) that contains object id, object type, tenant id, generated checksum, deletion timestamp, deletion operator id, and a signed notice (HMAC) produced by our evidence pipeline.
- SIG/CAIQ mapping: populate the CryptoEdge template and annotate with our SOC 2 procedure references and sample artifacts.

Technical clarifications needed from CryptoEdge:
1) Tenant identifiers: please provide the tenant id(s) you want included in the sample export (staging tenant vs. prod).
2) Legal hold process: confirm who (email) would assert a hold and the expected timeframe to lift it — we need to include hold-handling in the playbook.
3) Export format preference (CSV + ZIP of manifests, or PDF summary + CSV) — we can produce both, but staging export size/time differ.

Notes on timeline and constraints:
- We can produce a draft playbook and populated SIG cells within 3 business days if CryptoEdge can provide tenant ids and confirm there are no active legal holds.
- The proof-of-delete export will be generated from our staging cluster; because of sampling limitations we will provide a mini-export that is representative (not exhaustive), plus instructions for how a full export would be generated in prod.
- For KMS-managed objects we will include the KMS key metadata and demonstrate how key-cycling is handled, but final key-rotation artifacts will require coordination with your KMS team.

Proposed next step: 30-minute sync to confirm tenant ids and legal hold procedures. I have availability Wednesday 11/24 10:00-10:30 PT or Thursday 11/25 09:00-09:30 PT. Let me know what works and we'll send a calendar invite.

On Sat, Nov 21, 2026 at 11:02 AM Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Samantha — thanks for the clear ask and the template. Looping in Priya (security lead for Private) and Neha (compliance/evidence) to scope deliverables and timeline.
>
> Summary of what we will deliver (first pass):
> 1) A tailored "Tenant Destruction Playbook" describing steps for: identification of artifacts (logs, KV snapshots, diagnostic bundles), deletion orchestration, KMS key handling for objects, and manifest/checksum generation.
> 2) A sample proof-of-delete export from a staging tenant: CSV manifest + generated SHA256 checksums + deletion timestamps.
> 3) SIG/CAIQ mappings (we will populate the CryptoEdge template you attached) and callouts to SOC 2 procedures where relevant.
>
> Best,
> Ben
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Liu <samantha@cryptoedge.io>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>, Mark Reeves <procurement@cryptoedge.io>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 14:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: tailored destruction playbook + SIG/CAIQ evidence for Dedicated tenant

Samantha — great, thanks Priya for the plan.

We can target the following:
- Draft playbook + editable doc by end of day Wednesday 11/24 (will add legal-hold text once CryptoEdge confirms point of contact).
- Sample proof-of-delete manifest (mini-export) by Thursday 11/25 EOD PT. The mini-export will include three representative object types and checksums; full export instructions included.
- Populated SIG template v2 delivered with cross-references to SOC 2 controls by Thursday 11/25.

Samantha/Mark — please reply with the tenant id(s) you want included in the mini-export and confirm whether Mark or someone on procurement should be the legal-hold contact. I will send a calendar invite for the 30-minute sync on Wednesday 11/24 10:00 PT unless you ask to reschedule.

Thanks — looking forward to closing this out quickly.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

On Mon, Nov 23, 2026 at 09:28 AM Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks for the context, Samantha — and thanks Ben for looping us in. Quick runbook / proposed approach below; I copied the parts we will need from your template into action items.

> - Proof-of-delete export: a structured manifest (CSV) that contains object id, object type, tenant id, generated checksum, deletion timestamp, deletion operator id, and a signed notice (HMAC) produced by our evidence pipeline.
> - SIG/CAIQ mapping: populate the CryptoEdge template and annotate with our SOC 2 procedure references and sample artifacts.
>
> Proposed next step: 30-minute sync to confirm tenant ids and legal hold procedures. I have availability Wednesday 11/24 10:00-10:30 PT or Thursday 11/25 09:00-09:30 PT. Let me know what works and we'll send a calendar invite.

On Mon, Nov 23, 2026 at 09:28 AM Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks for the context, Samantha — and thanks Ben for looping us in. Quick runbook / proposed approach below; I copied the parts we will need from your template into action items.
>
> High-level approach (what we'll produce):
> - Playbook doc (PDF + editable Google Doc): scope, roles/responsibilities, triggers for deletion (age-based, customer request, on-termination), legal-hold overrides, verification steps, and recovery/retention exceptions.
> - Proof-of-delete export: a structured manifest (CSV) that contains object id, object type, tenant id, generated checksum, deletion timestamp, deletion operator id, and a signed notice (HMAC) produced by our evidence pipeline.
> - SIG/CAIQ mapping: populate the CryptoEdge template and annotate with our SOC 2 procedure references and sample artifacts.
>
> Proposed next step: 30-minute sync to confirm tenant ids and legal hold procedures. I have availability Wednesday 11/24 10:00-10:30 PT or Thursday 11/25 09:00-09:30 PT. Let me know what works and we'll send a calendar invite.

--
Attachments referenced: log-export-sample.csv (text/csv)

Subject: SOC 2 / SIG: Onboarding & Offboarding artifacts request

Hi Maya / Fjord team,

Thanks for the heads up on the assessment scope. We can definitely compile the employee onboarding/offboarding artifacts you requested. To confirm the exact deliverables you need from Redwood:

- Background check policy + a sample of completed (redacted) checks covering hires and applicable contractors
- Training completion roster for Security & Data Handling for the last 12 months
- Onboarding checklist matrix showing role-based access grants and timing (HRIS + Okta evidence)
- Offboarding / deprovisioning tickets and timeline examples (SSO, GCP, internal apps)
- Recent access review exports (last 12 months) and owner attestation logs

Can you confirm whether you need full PII in samples (we will redact where required) and whether contractor onboarding (1099 / vendor contractors) is in-scope? We aim to get a consolidated package to you by Tue Nov 24 EOD PST if that works.

Jordan Blake

Redwood Inference
(jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com)

- PII: redact SSNs and DOBs; we need name, role, hire date, verification that check completed.
- For deprovisioning we specifically want at least two ticket examples showing the ticket lifecycle and timestamps (created -> revoked -> verified).
- Access review: export of Okta (or similar) admin reports for the last 12 months is sufficient; CSV is preferred.

source.subject: Staging hang during connector test — request for trial runway + credits
messages:
From: Devon Wu <devon@cortexly.ai>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.inference>
Cc: Aisha Kumar <aisha.k@cortexly.ai>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 16:12:00 -0800
Subject: Staging hang during connector test — need extra runway

Hi Priya —

Thanks again for the walkthrough yesterday. We started a full connector validation on our staging cluster last night and hit a reproducible hang when the event stream rebalance happens (~30s into the job). Our engs have been debugging but it’s holding up the final POC checklist items (tokenized prompts + live reranking). We expect it’s a configuration edge-case on our side, but we need more time to validate and would like to request a 30-day extension on the trial window plus a small sandbox credit top-up so we can run multiple long-running validation passes.

Attached are a small snippet of the staging logs and a screenshot of the UI hang. If you can approve an extension now, that buys our team time to confirm the fix and complete the final demo next month.

Request: 30-day trial extension + additional $2k sandbox credits (estimated to cover 3x long validation runs).

Appreciate any help — happy to jump on a quick call.

—Devon
Lead Platform Engineer
Cortexly
From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.inference>
To: Devon Wu <devon@cortexly.ai>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.inference>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.inference>
Date: Sat, 21 Nov 2026 09:08:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Staging hang during connector test — need extra runway

Hi Devon —

Thanks for the quick details and for attaching the logs — that helps. Sorry to hear about the hang during the rebalance step; the symptoms line up with a known SDK backoff mis-configuration we’ve seen on a specific client-side consumer group pattern.

I’m looping in Connor (sales ops) and Kim (account lead) to review for an expedited trial extension and sandbox top-up. Connor — can you advise on standard approval cadence and whether we can do a 30-day extension + $2k sandbox credit as a one-off? If not, what would you recommend as an immediate bridge?

If you like, we can provision the sandbox top-up now and roll it into a follow-up manual review. Also happy to set up an eng-to-eng session with our runtime team this week to accelerate the root-cause.

Thanks,
Priya
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.inference>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.inference>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.inference>
Date: Sat, 21 Nov 2026 10:41:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Staging hang during connector test — need extra runway

Priya / Kim —

Quick review: Cortexly is in the mid-market POC funnel, healthy engagement, product-led trial started 2026-10-04. They ran 2 demo sessions and were scheduled to finish validation this week. Given the outage is a non-platform bug (appears client-side config), I recommend a 30-day extension with a provisional $1,500 sandbox credit to keep momentum. Our playbook allows the credit if CS or AE escalates and documents the root cause + next-step plan.

If PD / finance will need more justification, we should capture: (1) concise timeline of the blocker, (2) planned eng-to-eng session date, (3) success criteria for final demo. I can provision the provisional credit now and flag for post-mortem approval.

I'll set the credit to the Cortexly sandbox and extend the trial window. Priya — please confirm you want the provisional approach or hold for leadership sign-off.

Connor
Sales Operations
From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.inference>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.inference>, Devon Wu <devon@cortexly.ai>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.inference>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 08:27:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Staging hang during connector test — need extra runway

Connor — please go ahead with the provisional credit & extension. Devon — we’ll provision a 30-day extension plus $1,500 in sandbox credits right away to keep your validation running. Can you confirm the eng-to-eng time that works for you next Wednesday (11/24) 10:00am PT or Thursday (11/25) 2:00pm PT? Our runtime engineer (Kim) will join and we’ll walk through the rebalance timeline so we can reproduce and land a fix quickly.

Action items: 
- Connor: provision 30-day extension + $1.5k sandbox credits to Cortexly account (provisional).
- Devon: confirm which eng-to-eng slot works and attach full log bundle if available.
- Priya/Kim: run the post-provision validation and capture success criteria.

Thanks everyone — we’ll aim to keep the demo window on schedule.

—Priya
From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.inference>
To: Devon Wu <devon@cortexly.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.inference>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.inference>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Staging hang during connector test — need extra runway

Devon —

Done: I provisioned a 30-day trial extension (new expiry 2026-12-22) and $1,500 in sandbox credits to your Cortexly trial account. Credits are visible in the console under Billing > Sandbox Credits. This is marked provisional pending a short post-mortem: please reply to this thread after the eng-to-eng session with the replication steps and final demo date.

Attachments: staging-log-20261120.txt, screenshot-hang-20261120.png

If you’d like us to increase to the full $2k, we can fast-track after the post-mortem; just submit the replication notes following the session.

Thanks — Connor
Sales Operations
Redwood Inference

We started a full connector validation on our staging cluster last night and hit a reproducible hang when the event stream rebalance happens (~30s into the job). Our engs have been debugging but it’s holding up the final POC checklist items (tokenized prompts + live reranking). We expect it’s a configuration edge-case on our side, but we need more time to validate and would like to request a 30-day extension on the trial window plus a small sandbox credit top-up so we can run multiple long-running validation passes.

Attached are a small snippet of the staging logs and a screenshot of the UI hang. If you can approve an extension now, that buys our team time to confirm the fix and complete the final demo next month.

Request: 30-day trial extension + additional $2k sandbox credits (estimated to cover 3x long validation runs).

Appreciate any help — happy to jump on a quick call.

—Devon
Lead Platform Engineer
Cortexly

Thanks,
Priya
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Our runtime engineer (Kim) will join and we’ll walk through the rebalance timeline so we can reproduce and land a fix quickly.

Action items: 
- Connor: provision 30-day extension + $1.5k sandbox credits to Cortexly account (provisional).
- Devon: confirm which eng-to-eng slot works and attach full log bundle if available.

source.subject: Consent window and escrowed quote for press & logo (MSA/order form)
messages:
From: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@healthsync.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: quote + logo usage & proposed timing for announcement

Hi Irene —

Thanks again for the excellent onboarding session last week. We're planning to announce our integration with Redwood the week of Dec 6 and would like to include a 1–2 sentence customer quote and the Redwood logo in our press materials. A few quick Qs:

- Are we allowed to publish the quote as-is, or do you need to review/approve final wording?
- Is logo usage allowed on our press site and in the press release PDF? Any style constraints?
- Our PR lead asks whether Redwood requires an embargo/approval window (e.g., 48–72 hours) once we send the final materials.

We have a signed order form on file (attached). Hoping to confirm by Nov 20 so we can lock the outreach calendar.

Best,
Samantha
Samantha Lee
Head of Partnerships, HealthSync
S: (415) 555-0198

Attachments: Signed_Order_Form_HW-0192.pdf

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@healthsync.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 11:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: quote + logo usage & proposed timing for announcement

Hi Samantha —

Thanks for the note and for sending the signed order form. Quick answers up front: we typically require a short approval window (48 hours) for customer quotes and logo assets; logo use must follow our brand guidelines.

I copied Legal (Priya) to confirm the exact language we need in the Order Form / MSA that covers reference rights and publicity. Jonas, can you confirm whether the order form you closed already has the standard "reference right" clause or if we should attach an addendum?

If HealthSync wants to escrow a draft quote with us (we’ll store a timestamped copy), we can provisionally approve the content once legal confirms. Proposed timeline:
- Nov 20: Final draft to redwood (press@healthsync / PR sends files)
- Nov 22 by EOD: Redwood approval or redlines (48 hours)
- Dec 6: Public announcement (HealthSync schedule)

Attaching our logo pack for reference — please use the "horizontal" lockup on white/bg where possible and don't modify colors.

Thanks,
Irene
Head of Customer Success (on behalf of Partnerships)
Redwood Inference

Attachments: logo-pack-redwood.zip

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@healthsync.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Nov 2026 08:46:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: quote + logo usage & proposed timing for announcement

Folks —

I reviewed the attached Signed_Order_Form_HW-0192.pdf. The good news: the Order Form references our standard publicity consent paragraph but does not include explicit embargo timing. Two notes and a recommended path:

1) Publicity clause: the existing language grants "non-exclusive, worldwide" rights to use HealthSync's name and logo for Redwood marketing. That covers us using HealthSync as a reference. However, it does not explicitly grant HealthSync the right to quote Redwood verbatim — we usually require joint approval of quotes to ensure accuracy.

2) Approval window: to avoid last-minute pull requests, Redwood typically requests a 48–72 hour approval window for customer-provided quotes and press releases. We can commit to a 72-hour review if HealthSync needs slightly more turnaround.

3) Logo usage: we require compliance with our brand spec (do not re-color, do not alter lockups). If HealthSync needs higher-res assets, use the provided logo pack.

Recommended next steps:
- HealthSync sends the final quote + press PDF to press@healthsync.com and cc legal@healthsync.com + priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com
- We timestamp the submission and treat it as the material under review.
- If HealthSync prefers an explicit addendum, we can offer a short publicity addendum to the Order Form (1 paragraph) that clarifies approval timelines and permitted channels. Legal can draft that in <48 hours upon request.

Happy to draft the addendum language.

- Priya
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

On Nov 10, 2026, at 11:05, Irene Choi wrote:
> Hi Samantha —
> 
> Thanks for the note and for sending the signed order form...

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@healthsync.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 13 Nov 2026 10:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: quote + logo usage & proposed timing for announcement

Quick internal note + ask for Ops:

- Sales confirms there is no change to commercial terms and the Order Form is fully executed (Jonas pls confirm).
- Would prefer to avoid an addendum if legal is comfortable with the 72-hour review commitment instead — simpler for customer.

Monica — when HealthSync sends the final quote, can CS confirm the one-sentence quote aligns with our customer quote policy (no pricing, no PII) and provide a single consolidated asset package (logo files + one final .docx with the quote)? That will keep review fast.

Jonas — confirmed, the deal closed on Oct 28 and the signed OF is in Salesforce (deal HW-2026-0192).

Thanks,
Karthik
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

On Nov 11, 2026, at 08:46, Priya Natarajan wrote:
> Folks —
> 
> I reviewed the attached Signed_Order_Form...

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@healthsync.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 16:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: quote + logo usage & proposed timing for announcement

Hi Samantha —

Thanks for sending the draft quote and press PDF (received Nov 18). Per Priya's guidance, Redwood will provide approval or redlines within 72 hours of receipt.

Summary of agreed items:
- Redwood confirms reference rights under the Order Form permit HealthSync to refer to the relationship; Redwood reserves joint-approval of published customer quotes.
- Redwood will approve the quote or provide minor edits within 72 hours of receiving the final asset package.
- HealthSync may use the provided logo pack; please follow the brand constraints in the included "README" of the zip.
- If HealthSync would like an explicit addendum (short publicity clause with approval window), Legal will prepare a one-paragraph addendum to be signed in DocuSign. We do not think this is required for this announcement unless HealthSync requests it.

Next steps (for clarity):
- Nov 18: HealthSync sent final assets (timestamped) — thank you.
- Nov 20 by 16:30 PT: Redwood approval received (this message confirms approval; see attached HealthSync_PressQuote_Draft_v1.docx with minor grammar edits).
- Redwood will make the approved assets available internally for marketing and sales reference in the shared Drive link.

Samantha — please confirm you received the attached final docx and that the timeline works for your Dec 6 announcement.

Best,
Irene Choi
Head of Customer Success
Redwood Inference

Attachments: HealthSync_PressQuote_Draft_v1.docx

Cc: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@healthsync.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Nov 2026 08:46:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: quote + logo usage & proposed timing for announcement

Folks —

I reviewed the attached Signed_Order_Form_HW-0192.pdf. The good news: the Order Form references our standard publicity consent paragraph but does not include explicit embargo timing. Two notes and a recommended path:

1) Publicity clause: the existing language grants \"non-exclusive, worldwide\" rights to use HealthSync's name and logo for Redwood marketing. That covers us using HealthSync as a reference. However, it does not explicitly grant HealthSync the right to quote Redwood verbatim — we usually require joint approval of quotes to ensure accuracy.

2) Approval window: to avoid last-minute pull requests, Redwood typically requests a 48–72 hour approval window for customer-provided quotes and press releases. We can commit to a 72-hour review if HealthSync needs slightly more turnaround.

3) Logo usage: we require compliance with our brand spec (do not re-color, do not alter lockups). If HealthSync needs higher-res assets, use the provided logo pack.

Recommended next steps:
- HealthSync sends the final quote + press PDF to press@healthsync.com and cc legal@healthsync.com + priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com
- We timestamp the submission and treat it as the material under review.
- If HealthSync prefers an explicit addendum, we can offer a short publicity addendum to the Order Form (1 paragraph) that clarifies approval timelines and permitted channels. Legal can draft that in <48 hours upon request.

Happy to draft the addendum language.

- Priya
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

On Nov 10, 2026, at 11:05, Irene Choi wrote:
> Hi Samantha —
> 
> Thanks for the note and for sending the signed order form...


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@healthsync.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 13 Nov 2026 10:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: quote + logo usage & proposed timing for announcement

Quick internal note + ask for Ops:

- Sales confirms there is no change to commercial terms and the Order Form is fully executed (Jonas pls confirm).
- Would prefer to avoid an addendum if legal is comfortable with the 72-hour review commitment instead — simpler for customer.

Monica — when HealthSync sends the final quote, can CS confirm the one-sentence quote aligns with our customer quote policy (no pricing, no PII) and provide a single consolidated asset package (logo files + one final .docx with the quote)? That will keep review fast.

Jonas — confirmed, the deal closed on Oct 28 and the signed OF is in Salesforce (deal HW-2026-0192).

Thanks,
Karthik
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

On Nov 11, 2026, at 08:46, Priya Natarajan wrote:
> Folks —
> 
> I reviewed the attached Signed_Order_Form...


From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@healthsync.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 16:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: quote + logo usage & proposed timing for announcement

Hi Samantha —

Thanks for sending the draft quote and press PDF (received Nov 18). Per Priya's guidance, Redwood will provide approval or redlines within 72 hours of receipt.

Summary of agreed items:
- Redwood confirms reference rights under the Order Form permit HealthSync to refer to the relationship; Redwood reserves joint-approval of published customer quotes.
- Redwood will approve the quote or provide minor edits within 72 hours of receiving the final asset package.
- HealthSync may use the provided logo pack; please follow the brand constraints in the included \"README\" of the zip.
- If HealthSync would like an explicit addendum (short publicity clause with approval window), Legal will prepare a one-paragraph addendum to be signed in DocuSign. We do not think this is required for this announcement unless HealthSync requests it.

Next steps (for clarity):
- Nov 18: HealthSync sent final assets (timestamped) — thank you.
- Nov 20 by 16:30 PT: Redwood approval received (this message confirms approval; see attached HealthSync_PressQuote_Draft_v1.docx with minor grammar edits).
- Redwood will make the approved assets available internally for marketing and sales reference in the shared Drive link.

Samantha — please confirm you received the attached final docx and that the timeline works for your Dec 6 announcement.

Best,
Irene Choi
Head of Customer Success
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Renewal: SLA addendum & capacity bump for Q1 term
messages:
From: Elaine Park <elaine.park@geovista.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Reyes <tom.reyes@geovista.com>
Date: 2026-11-01T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Renewal conversation — SLA + support tier question

Hi Jordan,

Hope you’re well. Our annual agreement with Redwood is up for renewal at the end of December, and we’re evaluating whether to move to the Premium Support tier and formalize latency SLOs for our national deployment. A couple of quick asks to scope this: 

- Can Redwood commit to a 95th percentile p95 inference latency SLO (50ms for embeddings, 150ms for text-gen) for our dedicated pool?
- What does the Premium Support response SLA look like (pager escalation, on-call window, dedicated CSM)?
- We’re also considering a 2x capacity increase for Q1 to support forecasted growth — what pricing/lead time do you need?

If helpful, Tom (cc’d) can share our traffic forecast. Could we get a short addendum to attach to the renewal that spells out SLOs and support commitments?

Thanks — Elaine
Elaine Park
Director of ML Platforms, Geovista Analytics

-----
Attachment: traffic-forecast-Q1-2027.xlsx (spreadsheet stub)

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Elaine Park <elaine.park@geovista.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-03T11:04:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Renewal conversation — SLA + support tier question

Hi Elaine — thanks for the clear asks and the forecast. Quick summary and proposed next steps: 

- SLA / latency SLOs: We can author an SLA addendum that commits to p95 targets of 50ms for embeddings and 150ms for text generation when traffic runs on a dedicated Redwood pool sized per your forecast. The addendum will include incident credit terms for missed SLOs and an operational playbook.
- Premium Support: Premium includes a dedicated CSM, 1-hour SE response for Sev-1 during business hours, 24/7 on-call with escalation to engineering for P0s, and quarterly runbooks.
- Capacity bump: For a 2x committed capacity increase, we’ll propose a Dedicated pool reservation. Lead time is typically 3 weeks for procurement + capacity assignment; expedited provisioning possible for an expedited seat fee.

Next steps: Marcus (cc) will put together a Dedicated capacity quote and Neha will attach a draft SLA addendum and our latest SOC 2 summary. Can we aim to have a draft by 2026-11-12 so your procurement team can review before the renewal window?

Jordan
Jordan Blake
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Phone: (415) 555-0187

-----
Attachment(s) referenced: none

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Elaine Park <elaine.park@geovista.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-07T09:32:00-08:00
Subject: Dedicated capacity quote + timeline (draft)

Hi Elaine / Tom —

Draft quote attached: Dedicated-Capacity-Quote-GV-20261112.pdf. Summary: 

- Baseline: 4 reserved GPUs (nv4-equivalent) matching your current throughput
- Requested 2x bump: proposal to reserve 8 GPUs for Q1 (committed term Jan-Mar) with an option to scale to 12 during peak weeks via burst capacity.
- Pricing delta: +28% on monthly commitment for dedicated capacity vs current committed rate (discount included for annual renewal).
- Lead time: 3 business weeks for procurement + 1 week for validation run. Expedited provisioning can be done in 5 business days with an expedite fee.

I’ve included operational notes in the PDF about how we guarantee the p95 SLOs (KV-cache priming, proactive scaling windows, and route pinning to the dedicated pool). Neha — attaching the SOC2 summary and SLA addendum per Jordan’s ask.

Marcus Lin
Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference

-----
Attachment: Dedicated-Capacity-Quote-GV-20261112.pdf

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Elaine Park <elaine.park@geovista.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-12T14:21:00-08:00
Subject: SLA addendum draft + SOC 2 summary

Elaine —

Please find the initial SLA addendum (Redwood_SLA_Addendum_GV_v1.1.pdf) and a one-page SOC 2 summary attached. Key callouts in the addendum: 

- SLOs: p95 latency guarantees for embeddings and text-gen tied to dedicated-pool sizing and traffic thresholds.
- Service credits: credit bands at 2%, 5%, and 10% for graduated SLO breaches per monthly window.
- Incident response: defined on-call escalation path, 1 hour target first response for Sev-1 during business hours, and 4-hour target overnight.
- Support tier: Premium Support includes 4x annual playbook-run exercises and a dedicated CSM.
- Term and billing: addendum to be signed and attached to the renewal amendment; pricing for capacity bump included in the attached quote.

A few open items for procurement/legal review: exact traffic thresholds for SLO applicability, the maximum monthly credit cap, and the expedited provisioning fee amount. If you can highlight your preferences, we’ll lock the language.

Thanks — Neha
Neha Kapoor
Head of Customer Reliability, Redwood Inference

-----
Attachments: Redwood_SLA_Addendum_GV_v1.1.pdf; SOC2-report-2026-summary.pdf

From: Elaine Park <elaine.park@geovista.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Reyes <tom.reyes@geovista.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-16T08:45:00-08:00
Subject: Re: SLA addendum draft + SOC 2 summary

Thanks — we reviewed the draft with procurement and legal. Quick responses: 

- SLO applicability: We’re comfortable with the dedicated-pool sizing clause but need the explicit monthly traffic floor (200M tokens) for the SLO to apply; please insert that number so we can circulate internally.
- Credits: Agree to the bands but propose a hard cap equal to one month’s committed capacity spend.
- Expedited provisioning fee: Can you confirm the dollar amount for the expedite option? Procurement would like a fixed fee vs a percent.

We’re broadly aligned and would like to move forward if the three items above are reconciled. Tom and I are available Thursday for a 30-minute call to finalize language.

—Elaine

-----
Attachment: GV-procurement-comments.docx (document stub)

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Elaine Park <elaine.park@geovista.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-18T14:52:00-08:00
Subject: Re: SLA addendum draft + SOC 2 summary

Elaine / Tom —

Thanks for the clear procurement comments. Confirmations below and proposed final steps: 

- Monthly traffic floor: set to 200,000,000 tokens. We’ll update the addendum clause to reflect that exact threshold.
- Credit cap: we’ll set the maximum credit equal to one month’s committed capacity spend (per your ask).
- Expedited provisioning fee: fixed fee = $6,500 for expedite provisioning (covers priority engineering, automated AMI build, and priority capacity reservation).

If that works, we’ll publish Redwood_SLA_Addendum_GV_v1.2 and the updated quote today and route a DocuSign amendment for your signature. Proposed next step: 30-minute call Thursday 11/19 10:00am PT to walk the final text, then we’ll issue DocuSign.

Jordan Blake
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Mobile: (415) 555-0187

-----
Related link: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/12345/GV-2026-1087

messages:
From: Alice Connors <alice.connors@orthologic.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: OrthoLogic Compliance <compliance@orthologic.com>
Date: 2026-11-15T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Tenant-scoped debug logs — shorter retention & SOC 2 evidence mapping

Hi Jordan —

Thanks for the demo last week. Our security team surfaced a request: one of our product teams needs debug-level traces for a subset of tenants for 14 days (instead of Redwood's default 90-day session log window) to support internal fraud triage. We understand this deviates from the standard retention and that any customer-specific carve-out requires evidence for our auditors.

Can you confirm whether Redwood can: 
- Configure a tenant-scoped TTL of 14 days for debug logs only (no change to audit/security logs).
- Provide exported evidence suitable for our SOC 2 auditor that shows: retention window applied, deletion job ran, and the deletion proof (hash/manifest) for affected tenant IDs.

If possible, please attach any sample evidence artifacts and the process steps we should expect. We need to present this in our audit packet in two weeks.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

—Alice
Alice Connors
Head of Product Security
OrthoLogic
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Alice Connors <alice.connors@orthologic.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-15T11:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Tenant-scoped debug logs — shorter retention & SOC 2 evidence mapping

Alice —

Thanks for the clear ask. Short answer: yes, we can support tenant-scoped shorter TTLs for debug-level traces, with caveats. Looping in Priya (security/compliance) and Markus (ops/runtime) to confirm the evidence outputs and timing.

Quick notes so everyone knows the scope: 
- This would be a tenant-level config flag scoped to non-audit debug traces (not authentication or audit trail events).
- Deletion is performed by our scheduled retention job; for a customer carve-out we can run a targeted deletion job and produce a deletion manifest plus storage-level object tombstone timestamps.
- Evidence we can provide: deletion manifest (tenant IDs + object keys), job run logs with UTC timestamps, and an exported CSV of affected object paths. We typically attach those as part of a SOC 2 evidence bundle.

Priya/Markus: can you confirm whether the manifest + storage tombstone + job-run log are adequate for a SOC 2 auditor, or should we also produce a signed hash/manifest? Also flag any automation we need to run (manual vs. scheduled) and an ETA for a sample pack.

Jordan
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Cc: Alice Connors <alice.connors@orthologic.com>
Date: 2026-11-16T09:22:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Tenant-scoped debug logs — shorter retention & SOC 2 evidence mapping

Thanks Jordan. Alice — tagging the required artefacts and a short process we recommend for audit readiness.

Required evidence for SOC 2 (we've seen auditors accept this set):
1) Retention configuration snapshot (API response showing TTL settings for the tenant)
2) Deletion job run log with job id, UTC start/end, and the list of object keys acted on
3) Deletion manifest (CSV) with object path, SHA256 hash, and deletion timestamp
4) Storage provider tombstone record or equivalent (blob metadata showing deletion)
5) Internal change control record if any manual override was used (Jira/PR link)

Optional / helpful: signed manifest (we can sign the manifest with our attestation key) and a short runbook describing the deletion pipeline.

Operational notes from Markus: 
- We will need an explicit request from OrthoLogic (owner + tenant id list + retention window) and a signed DPA addendum acknowledging the deviation.
- For the sample pack (non-prod) we can deliver within 3 business days. For production tenant deletion evidence we prefer a scheduled maintenance window; the job is low-risk but we surface it through change control.

Next steps (proposed):
- Alice: send the tenant IDs and sign the one-page carve-out acknowledgement (I attached a template).
- Jordan: I'll prepare a sample evidence bundle example and provide a signed manifest option.
- Markus: can you confirm the storage-level tombstone output format and attach a sample?

Attachments: orthologic-retention-exception-form.xlsx

—Priya
Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference
From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
To: Alice Connors <alice.connors@orthologic.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-17T16:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Tenant-scoped debug logs — shorter retention & SOC 2 evidence mapping

Hi all —

Attaching a small sample of the outputs we can produce. For the storage provider we run a GC that issues tombstones; sample metadata looks like: 
- object: /tenants/orthologic/trace/2026-10-01/obj-12345.json
- tombstone_timestamp: 2026-11-17T02:15:08Z
- deletion_job_id: deljob-20261117-77

I've attached a one-page sample manifest and a draft SOC 2 evidence bundle structure. For the signed manifest we can sign with our internal attestation key (signature block included in the sample PDF).

Deliverables we will provide once we receive the signed exception form and tenant list: 
- Configuration snapshot (API response JSON)
- Deletion job run log (plain text)
- Deletion manifest (CSV) + SHA256 (we will also provide the signed manifest PDF)
- Storage tombstone excerpt (metadata)
- Change control ticket link (if manual window)

Attachments: redwood_soc2_evidence_mapping_v1.pdf

Markus Klein
Senior Engineering Manager, Runtime
Redwood Inference

On Mon, Nov 16, 2026 at 09:22 AM Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks Jordan. Alice — tagging the required artefacts and a short process we recommend for audit readiness.
> 
> [quoted content trimmed]

source.subject: NeoHospital: IAM evidence request — SSO assertions, SCIM deltas, RBAC samples
messages:
From: Jamie Ortiz <jamie.ortiz@neohospital.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Alex Rivera <alex.rivera@neohospital.com>
Date: 2026-11-10T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Request: SSO / SCIM / RBAC samples for SOC 2 evidence

Hi Priya,

Thanks again for the webinar last week. NeoHospital is kicking off a scoped SOC 2 readiness review and our auditor has asked for a narrow set of artifacts related to SSO and provisioning for the applications Redwood federates with. Specifically they'd like: 

- 5 representative SAML assertion samples across two SPs (including the NameID and attributes, timestamps)
- SCIM delta logs (last 30 days) for user provisioning and deprovisioning events
- RBAC ownership mapping: role -> permission list + owner for three primary app integrations
- Session activity samples showing session creation and expiry for web SSO (timezone: PST)

Can you confirm whether Redwood can provide these artifacts redacted for PII, and a short delivery plan (timeline + contact for the SFTP/secure transfer)? We are on a tight window — auditor wants the package by 2026-11-18.

Appreciate it,

Jamie Ortiz
Security Manager, NeoHospital

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Jamie Ortiz <jamie.ortiz@neohospital.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-10T11:04:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: SSO / SCIM / RBAC samples for SOC 2 evidence

Hi Jamie — thanks for the clear list. We can provide everything you asked for and will redact PII before transfer. Proposed workback: 

1) We’ll collect the 5 SAML assertions (earliest-to-latest samples) and remove any user-identifying fields, leaving NameID type/timestamp/attributes schema.
2) Export SCIM delta records for the last 30 days and provide a short CSV summary plus raw deltas in CSV.
3) RBAC mapping: export role -> permissions for the three apps you named during the call (AlertsApp, ChartSync, BillingUI) and include owner email and last modified.
4) Session activity: 48 sample rows showing session_start, session_end, ttl, and auth_method.

Delivery: We can stage the package in a secure SFTP location by 2026-11-17 EOD PT. Ben will be the lead for redaction and Mina will own the SCIM export.

I’ll attach a short workback (IAM_Evidence_Workback.xlsx) with milestones. If SFTP is not acceptable, we can use an expiring SharePoint link.

Action items for NeoHospital: confirm acceptable redaction pattern (hashing vs pseudonymization) and whether the auditor needs assertion raw XML or JSON translation.

Thanks — Priya
Priya Natarajan
Enterprise Security & Compliance, Redwood Inference

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Jamie Ortiz <jamie.ortiz@neohospital.com>
Date: 2026-11-11T10:22:00-08:00
Subject: Internal: NeoHospital IAM evidence — scope and extraction notes

Ben / Mina / Jonas — forwarding NeoHospital's ask (below). Quick notes for extraction: 

- SAML assertions: pull five assertions from the auth-log bucket (s3://auth-logs/prod/saml/2026/). Prefer recent window 2026-10-25 -> 2026-11-09. Keep one sample per SP and include one failed assertion example. Redact user.email and user.full_name; keep NameID type, AssertionIssueInstant, Audience, and attributes list.
- SCIM deltas: use scim-deltas-prod, export events where op in ["create","replace","delete"] for last 30 days. Provide CSV with columns: event_id, op, resourceType, id, timestamp, raw_payload (redacted). Max file size 10MB; if larger, deliver in split parts.
- RBAC mapping: run rbac-export job for Apps: AlertsApp, ChartSync, BillingUI. Include role_name, permission_list (comma-separated), owner_email, last_modified.
- Session activity: sample 48 lines from session-activity logs (filter by auth_method in ["saml","oauth2"], timezone PST). Include ttl and device_type.

Redaction guidance: hash(email) using SHA256 and remove raw IPs; retain first 3 chars of user agent string. Auditor accepts hashed emails but asked for salt description (we will provide algorithm and note that salt is not included).

I'll coordinate packaging. Ben — can you confirm you can complete redaction and upload to sftp://uploads.redwood-inference.com/neohospital by Tue 11/17? Mina — confirm SCIM export size/format. Jonas — add a short README describing extraction steps and where in infra each artifact came from.

Thanks — Priya

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jamie Ortiz <jamie.ortiz@neohospital.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-17T08:16:00-08:00
Subject: NeoHospital evidence package uploaded to SFTP (NEO-2304)

Jamie / Priya,

We uploaded the package to the secure sftp location you provided: sftp://uploads.redwood-inference.com/neohospital/NEO-2304-20261117/

Files included (redacted per request):
- saml_assertions_samples.jsonl (5 assertion objects: fields kept = AssertionIssueInstant, Audience, NameIDFormat, attributes schema; user emails hashed)
- scim_delta_20261017-20261109.csv (SCIM op deltas for last 30 days, raw_payload column redacted of PII)
- rbac_owners_mapping.csv (role_name, permission_list, owner_email_hashed, last_modified)
- session_activity_sample.csv (48 rows: session_start, session_end, ttl_seconds, auth_method, device_type)
- README_Neohospital_evidence.txt (extraction notes, hash algorithm details, locations in infra)

Notes on redaction: we hashed emails with SHA256 and removed raw IPs. The README contains the salt algorithm description and our redaction checklist. We included one intentionally failed SAML assertion to show error handling and audience mismatch trace.

If you want the raw XML assertions instead of JSONL, let us know and we'll prepare an encrypted, access-controlled transfer (requires NeoHospital written approval).

Ben Carter
Security & Compliance Engineer, Redwood Inference

Attachments: saml_assertions_samples.jsonl (stub), scim_delta_20261017-20261109.csv (stub)

From: Jamie Ortiz <jamie.ortiz@neohospital.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Alex Rivera <alex.rivera@neohospital.com>
Date: 2026-11-17T15:45:00-08:00
Subject: Re: NeoHospital evidence package uploaded to SFTP (NEO-2304)

Ben — received and spot-checked a few files. README looks good and redaction matches our policy. Thank you for the quick turnaround. Our auditor will pull these later today; we may follow up with a few clarifying questions around one assertion timestamp and the exact salt layout (won't require raw PII).

I’ll confirm once auditor completes initial review. Appreciate the responsiveness.

—Jamie

- SCIM deltas: use scim-deltas-prod, export events where op in [\"create\",\"replace\",\"delete\"] for last 30 days. Provide CSV with columns: event_id, op, resourceType, id, timestamp, raw_payload (redacted). Max file size 10MB; if larger, deliver in split parts.
- RBAC mapping: run rbac-export job for Apps: AlertsApp, ChartSync, BillingUI. Include role_name, permission_list (comma-separated), owner_email, last_modified.
- Session activity: sample 48 lines from session-activity logs (filter by auth_method in [\"saml\",\"oauth2\"], timezone PST). Include ttl and device_type.

Redaction guidance: hash(email) using SHA256 and remove raw IPs; retain first 3 chars of user agent string. Auditor accepts hashed emails but asked for salt description (we will provide algorithm and note that salt is not included).

I'll coordinate packaging. Ben — can you confirm you can complete redaction and upload to sftp://uploads.redwood-inference.com/neohospital by Tue 11/17? Mina — confirm SCIM export size/format. Jonas — add a short README describing extraction steps and where in infra each artifact came from.

Thanks — Priya

To: Jamie Ortiz <jamie.ortiz@neohospital.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-17T08:16:00-08:00
Subject: NeoHospital evidence package uploaded to SFTP (NEO-2304)

Jamie / Priya,

We uploaded the package to the secure sftp location you provided: sftp://uploads.redwood-inference.com/neohospital/NEO-2304-20261117/

Files included (redacted per request):
- saml_assertions_samples.jsonl (5 assertion objects: fields kept = AssertionIssueInstant, Audience, NameIDFormat, attributes schema; user emails hashed)
- scim_delta_20261017-20261109.csv (SCIM op deltas for last 30 days, raw_payload column redacted of PII)
