text:
From: Jamie Ortiz <jamie.ortiz@neohospital.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Alex Rivera <alex.rivera@neohospital.com>
Date: 2026-11-10T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Request: SSO / SCIM / RBAC samples for SOC 2 evidence

Hi Priya,

Thanks again for the webinar last week. NeoHospital is kicking off a scoped SOC 2 readiness review and our auditor has asked for a narrow set of artifacts related to SSO and provisioning for the applications Redwood federates with. Specifically they'd like: 

- 5 representative SAML assertion samples across two SPs (including the NameID and attributes, timestamps)
- SCIM delta logs (last 30 days) for user provisioning and deprovisioning events
- RBAC ownership mapping: role -> permission list + owner for three primary app integrations
- Session activity samples showing session creation and expiry for web SSO (timezone: PST)

Can you confirm whether Redwood can provide these artifacts redacted for PII, and a short delivery plan (timeline + contact for the SFTP/secure transfer)? We are on a tight window — auditor wants the package by 2026-11-18.

Appreciate it,

Jamie Ortiz
Security Manager, NeoHospital


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Jamie Ortiz <jamie.ortiz@neohospital.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-10T11:04:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: SSO / SCIM / RBAC samples for SOC 2 evidence

Hi Jamie — thanks for the clear list. We can provide everything you asked for and will redact PII before transfer. Proposed workback: 

1) We’ll collect the 5 SAML assertions (earliest-to-latest samples) and remove any user-identifying fields, leaving NameID type/timestamp/attributes schema.
2) Export SCIM delta records for the last 30 days and provide a short CSV summary plus raw deltas in CSV.
3) RBAC mapping: export role -> permissions for the three apps you named during the call (AlertsApp, ChartSync, BillingUI) and include owner email and last modified.
4) Session activity: 48 sample rows showing session_start, session_end, ttl, and auth_method.

Delivery: We can stage the package in a secure SFTP location by 2026-11-17 EOD PT. Ben will be the lead for redaction and Mina will own the SCIM export.

I’ll attach a short workback (IAM_Evidence_Workback.xlsx) with milestones. If SFTP is not acceptable, we can use an expiring SharePoint link.

Action items for NeoHospital: confirm acceptable redaction pattern (hashing vs pseudonymization) and whether the auditor needs assertion raw XML or JSON translation.

Thanks — Priya
Priya Natarajan
Enterprise Security & Compliance, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Trial kickoff: success criteria, timeline & ownership
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Gomez <agomez@novalabs.io>, Novalabs Procurement <contracts@novalabs.io>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 14 Nov 2026 09:22:00 -0800
Subject: Trial kickoff: success criteria, timeline & ownership

Thanks again for a productive call this morning, Alicia — quick recap and proposed next steps so we have a single source of truth to share with your team.

1) High-level success criteria (proposed)
- Latency: 95th pctile < 120ms for inference in your app flow
- Throughput: sustain 50 concurrent user sessions over 8-hour window
- Functional: end-to-end integration with your events pipeline and auth (token exchange)
- Quality: model prompts/outputs meet your internal eval set thresholds (see sheet)

2) Evaluation plan and timeline
- Week 0 (this week): finalize access and procurement contacts
- Week 1: Redwood spins up PoC environment and provides test endpoints
- Week 2: Integration + smoke tests by Novalabs engineering
- Week 3: Quantitative eval run and joint review (go/no-go)

3) Ownership (working RACI headline; detailed RACI attached)
- Infra: Redwood infra lead (Karthik) — provision and networking
- App integration: Novalabs eng (Alicia + engineering team) with Redwood CS (Monica) support
- Security & compliance review: Priya (Redwood legal/security) + Novalabs InfoSec
- Procurement & contract: Novalabs Procurement (Marco / contracts@novalabs.io)

4) Blockers & asks from Novalabs
- Confirm procurement lead/contact and expected procurement timeline
- Provide staging VPC CIDR and any firewall constraints
- Send your evaluation test set or sample queries for the quality threshold

Attachments: trial_success_criteria.xlsx (xlsx), trial_milestones.pdf (pdf)

Next steps:
- Alicia: confirm procurement contact + estimated procurement SLA by EOD Monday
- Redwood: share a short RACI table + schedule kickoff call (30 min) to align environment details

I will follow up with the detailed RACI and a calendar invite. Thanks — looking forward to getting this running.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Solutions | Redwood Inference

From: Alicia Gomez <agomez@novalabs.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Novalabs Procurement <contracts@novalabs.io>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 14 Nov 2026 11:10:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Trial kickoff: success criteria, timeline & ownership

Thanks Ben — that aligns with what we discussed. A few notes from our side: 
- Procurement lead is Marco Diaz (copied to this thread). Marco, Novalabs typical review window is ~2 weeks for an SOW/DPA (may be faster if limited scope).
- I'll ask our infra team for the staging VPC details and get back on CIDR + firewall rules by Monday.
- Attached is a short internal doc describing evaluation constraints and compliance items: Novalabs_eval_requirements.docx.

We're comfortable with the proposed success criteria; the one item we want to lock down is the auth flow — we'll need a token exchange that supports our short-lived session policy (details in the attachment).

Next from us: confirm procurement timeline (Marco), provide CIDR/firewall info (our infra), and a short list of sample queries by Monday afternoon.

Thanks — Alicia
Alicia Gomez
Head of Product, Novalabs

[Attachment: Novalabs_eval_requirements.docx (docx)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Gomez <agomez@novalabs.io>, Marco Diaz <m.diaz@novalabs.io>, Novalabs Procurement <contracts@novalabs.io>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 14 Nov 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Trial kickoff: success criteria, timeline & ownership

Great — thanks for the fast turnaround, Alicia. Attaching a compact RACI CSV for the kickoff and calling out key owners inline below so we can lock calendar invites: 

Inline RACI summary (editable):
- Executive sponsor (Novalabs): Alicia Gomez — overall success decision
- Procurement owner (Novalabs): Marco Diaz / contracts@novalabs.io — contract & billing
- Infra owner (Redwood): Karthik Iyer — PoC infra provisioning, network setup, test endpoints
- App integration owner (Novalabs): Novalabs eng (TBD) with Monica Patel (Redwood CS) as primary integration support
- Security reviewer (Redwood): Priya Natarajan; Novalabs InfoSec (TBD) — SOC 2 evidence, DPA, data handling

Attachments: novalabs_raci.csv (csv) — includes milestone owners and acceptance gates.

Proposed kickoff times (30 min): Mon 11/16 9:00-9:30am PT or Mon 11/16 1:00-1:30pm PT. I will send an invite for both and we can pick the one that works.

Ben

[Attachment: novalabs_raci.csv (csv)]

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Alicia Gomez <agomez@novalabs.io>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Marco Diaz <m.diaz@novalabs.io>
Date: Sun, 15 Nov 2026 08:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Trial kickoff: success criteria, timeline & ownership

Quick infra note from my side after reviewing the requirements/metrics Ben shared: 
- Minimum staging spec: 2xA100-equivalent GPUs (or quantized CPU fallback) + 64GB RAM for the model profile you want to run. If you prefer faster parallelism we can propose 4x smaller GPUs.
- Network: need a staging VPC peering or IP allowlist for Redwood test endpoint. Provide CIDR and desired egress rules.
- Estimated provisioning/setup: 2 business days once we have CIDR + firewall rules and a signed access addendum (if required).

If procurement timing is tight, we can use Redwood Dedicated sandbox capacity to avoid waiting for full contract (short-term, limited telemetry). Recommend aiming to have endpoints ready by Wed 11/18 to keep Week 2 integration on track.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Infrastructure Lead | Redwood Inference

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alicia Gomez <agomez@novalabs.io>, Marco Diaz <m.diaz@novalabs.io>, Novalabs Procurement <contracts@novalabs.io>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Trial kickoff: success criteria, timeline & ownership

Looping in a short note on compliance and procurement gating items: 
- We can proceed with a limited-scope SOW + mutual DPA for the trial. I'll circulate a draft DPA and SOW (attached: redwood_dpa_draft.pdf).
- Novalabs InfoSec will want SOC 2 Type II evidence and a short architecture diagram showing data flows (we will provide a template).
- Procurement: Marco, please advise if two-week review is accurate — if faster approval is possible we can tighten the schedule.

I'll coordinate redlines on the DPA and circle back with the legal point-of-contact for Novalabs. Once Marco confirms procurement cadence I'll mark any contract-level blockers.

Priya Natarajan
Head of Legal & Security | Redwood Inference

[Attachment: redwood_dpa_draft.pdf (pdf)]

From: Alicia Gomez <agomez@novalabs.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Novalabs Procurement <contracts@novalabs.io>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 14 Nov 2026 11:10:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Trial kickoff: success criteria, timeline & ownership

Thanks Ben — that aligns with what we discussed. A few notes from our side: 
- Procurement lead is Marco Diaz (copied to this thread). Marco, Novalabs typical review window is ~2 weeks for an SOW/DPA (may be faster if limited scope).
- I'll ask our infra team for the staging VPC details and get back on CIDR + firewall rules by Monday.
- Attached is a short internal doc describing evaluation constraints and compliance items: Novalabs_eval_requirements.docx.

We're comfortable with the proposed success criteria; the one item we want to lock down is the auth flow — we'll need a token exchange that supports our short-lived session policy (details in the attachment).

Next from us: confirm procurement timeline (Marco), provide CIDR/firewall info (our infra), and a short list of sample queries by Monday afternoon.

Thanks — Alicia
Alicia Gomez
Head of Product, Novalabs

[Attachment: Novalabs_eval_requirements.docx (docx)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Gomez <agomez@novalabs.io>, Marco Diaz <m.diaz@novalabs.io>, Novalabs Procurement <contracts@novalabs.io>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 14 Nov 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Trial kickoff: success criteria, timeline & ownership

Great — thanks for the fast turnaround, Alicia. Attaching a compact RACI CSV for the kickoff and calling out key owners inline below so we can lock calendar invites: 

Inline RACI summary (editable):
- Executive sponsor (Novalabs): Alicia Gomez — overall success decision
- Procurement owner (Novalabs): Marco Diaz / contracts@novalabs.io — contract & billing
- Infra owner (Redwood): Karthik Iyer — PoC infra provisioning, network setup, test endpoints
- App integration owner (Novalabs): Novalabs eng (TBD) with Monica Patel (Redwood CS) as primary integration support
- Security reviewer (Redwood): Priya Natarajan; Novalabs InfoSec (TBD) — SOC 2 evidence, DPA, data handling

Attachments: novalabs_raci.csv (csv) — includes milestone owners and acceptance gates.

Proposed kickoff times (30 min): Mon 11/16 9:00-9:30am PT or Mon 11/16 1:00-1:30pm PT. I will send an invite for both and we can pick the one that works.

Ben

[Attachment: novalabs_raci.csv (csv)]

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Alicia Gomez <agomez@novalabs.io>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Marco Diaz <m.diaz@novalabs.io>
Date: Sun, 15 Nov 2026 08:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Trial kickoff: success criteria, timeline & ownership

Quick infra note from my side after reviewing the requirements/metrics Ben shared: 
- Minimum staging spec: 2xA100-equivalent GPUs (or quantized CPU fallback) + 64GB RAM for the model profile you want to run. If you prefer faster parallelism we can propose 4x smaller GPUs.
- Network: need a staging VPC peering or IP allowlist for Redwood test endpoint. Provide CIDR and desired egress rules.
- Estimated provisioning/setup: 2 business days once we have CIDR + firewall rules and a signed access addendum (if required).

If procurement timing is tight, we can use Redwood Dedicated sandbox capacity to avoid waiting for full contract (short-term, limited telemetry). Recommend aiming to have endpoints ready by Wed 11/18 to keep Week 2 integration on track.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Infrastructure Lead | Redwood Inference


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alicia Gomez <agomez@novalabs.io>, Marco Diaz <m.diaz@novalabs.io>, Novalabs Procurement <contracts@novalabs.io>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Trial kickoff: success criteria, timeline & ownership

Looping in a short note on compliance and procurement gating items: 
- We can proceed with a limited-scope SOW + mutual DPA for the trial. I'll circulate a draft DPA and SOW (attached: redwood_dpa_draft.pdf).
- Novalabs InfoSec will want SOC 2 Type II evidence and a short architecture diagram showing data flows (we will provide a template).
- Procurement: Marco, please advise if two-week review is accurate — if faster approval is possible we can tighten the schedule.

I'll coordinate redlines on the DPA and circle back with the legal point-of-contact for Novalabs. Once Marco confirms procurement cadence I'll mark any contract-level blockers.

Priya Natarajan
Head of Legal & Security | Redwood Inference

[Attachment: redwood_dpa_draft.pdf (pdf)]

messages:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Green <alex.green@careco.health>, Priya Sinha <priya.sinha@careco.health>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 15:08:00 -0800
Subject: Aligning next steps: CareCo technical deep-dive windows & join details

Hi Alex and Priya,

Thanks again for the discovery earlier — really helpful to hear about the CareCo inference workload and latency targets. As discussed, next step is a technical deep-dive where we walk through:
- example traffic patterns and expected LLM sequence lengths
- integration touchpoints (VPC, auth, and S3 access)
- performance expectations and cost profile

Proposed 60–75 minute windows (all PT):
A) Wed 11/11 10:30–11:45
B) Thu 11/12 09:00–10:15
C) Thu 11/12 14:00–15:15

Please confirm which option works and who from your side will join. If you prefer we can host the Zoom bridge and send the calendar invite — I can also include a short agenda (attached) and a preliminary attendee roster.

Attendees from our side: Marissa Cole (Solutions Engineer), Dylan Brooks (Sales Engineer), myself (Solutions).

Attached: CareCo_prelim_agenda.pdf (high level topics).

Thanks — Priya

--
Priya Natarajan
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0127
redwoodinference.com

[Attachment: CareCo_prelim_agenda.pdf (pdf)]

From: Alex Green <alex.green@careco.health>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Sinha <priya.sinha@careco.health>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.patel@careco.health>
Date: Wed, 11 Nov 2026 08:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Aligning next steps: CareCo technical deep-dive windows & join details

Thanks Priya — good recap. We'd like to move forward with slot A (Wed 11/11 10:30 PT). Confirmed attendees from CareCo: me (Alex Green), Priya Sinha (Platform PM), Samir Patel (Infra lead). Samir will want a brief section on network/setup and KMS integration.

One ask: could you include a short appendix on your VPC/connectivity options and a note about on-prem/private deployment possibilities? That will help us loop in security if needed.

If you can send the calendar invite with a Zoom link and the attendee roster, we'll accept and add our security contact if required.

Appreciate it — Alex

On Tue, Nov 10, 2026 at 3:08 PM Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Thanks again for the discovery earlier — really helpful to hear about the CareCo inference workload and latency targets.
> ...

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Green <alex.green@careco.health>, Priya Sinha <priya.sinha@careco.health>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Nov 2026 09:24:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Aligning next steps: CareCo technical deep-dive windows & join details

Great — slot A confirmed. Calendar invite and Zoom below. I added Marissa and Dylan to the invite so they can lead the walkthrough; Dylan will cover perf profiling and Marissa will demo the integration checklist.

Zoom: https://zoom.us/j/9876543210?pwd=careco123 (passcode: Redwood2026)
Google Calendar: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=careco-deepdive-20261111

Final attendee roster (so we have the right people on the bridge):
- Redwood: Priya Natarajan (AE), Marissa Cole (SE), Dylan Brooks (SWE/Pre-sales)
- CareCo: Alex Green (Platform), Priya Sinha (PM), Samir Patel (Infra)

Agenda (60 mins):
1) 5m introductions & success criteria
2) 20m traffic/workload profile + target latencies
3) 20m integration (VPC, auth, KMS options & network requirements)
4) 10m perf/cost levers (batching/caching/quantization suggestions)
5) 5m next steps & action items

Attachments: CareCo_attendee-roster.xlsx (roster + email addresses), CareCo_prelim_agenda.pdf

Action items I committed to before the meeting:
- Attach 1-page VPC/connectivity appendix (sent with this mail)
- Share SSO/KMS integration checklist with Samir beforehand

If Samir wants to invite Security, that's fine — we'll keep the agenda tight and can add an extra 15 minutes at the end if needed. I'll send the formal calendar invite in a follow-up, but the Zoom link above should work immediately.

Thanks all — Priya

--
Priya Natarajan
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0127
[Attachment: CareCo_attendee-roster.xlsx (xlsx)]
